daemonologist.features.2.description: Your ability to siphon power from Celestials and Fiends is enhanced. You gain the following benefits.
daemonologist.features.2.details.archDae: {@b Arch Daemon Boon.} While you are siphoning power from Arch Daemons, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. In addition, Fiends that know languages can understand your speech and you can understand theirs, even if you do not share a language.
daemonologist.features.2.details.archSer: {@b Arch Seraph Boon.} While you are siphoning power from Arch Seraphs, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage. In addition, Celestials that know languages can understand your speech and you can understand theirs, even if you do not share a language.
daemonologist.features.2.details.switchS: {@b Switch Sides.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can change which power you are siphoning from. Once you switch, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
daemonologist.features.2.name: Borrowed Tongues and Hides
daemonologist.features.3.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you adopt an infernal or celestial countenance for 10 minutes. For the duration, your appearance gains aspects of the power you choose. You gain the following benefits.
daemonologist.features.3.details.command: {@b Commanding Presence.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks.
daemonologist.features.3.details.improve: {@b Improved Spells.} When you expend a spell slot to cast a spell from the Arch Daemon or Arch Seraph table, the spell is cast as if you had spent a spell slot of one level higher.
daemonologist.features.3.details.unearth: {@b Unearthly Wings.} You gain a Fly Speed of 60 feet.
daemonologist.features.3.details.usage: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5+ spell slot (no action required).
daemonologist.features.3.name: Unearthly Countenance
daemonologist.features.4.description:
You use the powers at your command to call forth warring celestials and infernals. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you summon a manifestation of the war between Arch Daemons and Arch Seraphs in a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within 120 feet of yourself. Each creature in the Sphere must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes {@dice 4d10} Necrotic damage, {@dice 4d10} Radiant damage, and has the {@status Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

As part of the same action, you can change the power you are siphoning from, and you also regain 1 Wizard spell slot of your choice of 5th level or lower.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
daemonologist.features.4.name: Eternal War Eruption
daemonologist.name: Демонолог
daemonologist.shortDescription: Steal Scraps of Divine and Infernal Power
dance.description: Bards of the College of Dance know that the Words of Creation can't be contained within speech or song; the words are uttered by the movements of celestial bodies and flow through the motions of the smallest creatures. These Bards practice a way of being in harmony with the whirling cosmos that emphasizes agility, speed, and grace.
dance.features.0.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you gain the following benefits.
dance.features.0.details.agileStrikes: When you expend a use of your Bardic Inspiration as part of an action, a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, or a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can make one {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as part of that action, {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
dance.features.0.details.bardicDamage: You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack rolls of your Unarmed Strikes. When you deal damage with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die plus your Dexterity modifier, instead of the strike's normal damage. This roll doesn't expend the die.
dance.features.0.details.danceVirtuoso: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any Charisma ({@skill Performance}) check you make that involves dancing.
dance.features.0.details.unarmoredDefens: Your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers.
dance.features.0.name: Dazzling Footwork
dance.features.1.description: When an enemy you can see ends its turn within 5 feet of an ally of yours, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to give that ally the option to use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. No creature provokes {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} from that movement.
dance.features.1.name: Inspiring Movement
dance.features.2.description: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can spend one use of Bardic Inspiration if you don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. When you do so, roll your Bardic Inspiration die, and choose a number of creatures within 60 feet of yourself (you can choose yourself), up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of those creatures gains a bonus to their Initiative equal to the number rolled.
dance.features.2.name: Tandem Footwork
dance.features.3.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail. You can share this benefit with allies: when a creature within 5 feet of you makes a Dexterity saving throw against the same effect, the creature takes only half damage on a failed save, and no damage on a successful one, provided the creature isn't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.
dance.features.3.name: Leading Evasion
dance.name: Коллегия Танца
dance.shortDescription: Move in Harmony with the Cosmos
deadeye.description: Метко выпущенная пуля могущественнее меча, стрелы или заклинания. Вы верите, что любой жестокий конфликт должен звучать как один громкий хлопок и долгая тишина. Такие выстрелы требуют совершенства, даже на расстоянии — ведь когда они сделаны правильно, они столь же смертельны для цели, сколь и впечатляющи для зрителей.
deadeye.features.0.description: [Манёвр] Один раз за ход, когда вы промахиваетесь броском дальнобойной атаки, вы можете потратить одну Кость риска и добавить её к броску атаки, потенциально превратив промах в попадание.
deadeye.features.0.name: Орлиный глаз
deadeye.features.1.description: Вы тренировались стрелять из устойчивой позиции {@condition Prone|XPHB|Лёжа}, что даёт следующие условия.
deadeye.features.1.details.fireWhilePron: Вы не совершаете броски дальнобойных атак с {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB|Помехой} из-за состояния {@condition Prone|XPHB|Лёжа}.
deadeye.features.1.details.quickStand: Когда у вас состояние {@condition Prone|XPHB|Лёжа}, вы можете подняться и тем самым прекратить состояние, потратив всего 5 футов перемещения.
deadeye.features.1.name: Стойка снайпера
deadeye.features.2.description: Вы превосходно стреляете из укрытия, что даёт вам следующие преимущества.
deadeye.features.2.details.camouflage: Вы можете совершать действие {@action Hide|XPHB|Скрытие}, даже если вы не находитесь в {@condition Heavily Obscured|XPHB|Сильно затенённой} области и не за {@variantrule Three-Quarters Cover|XPHB|Укрытием на три четверти} или {@variantrule Total Cover|XPHB|Полным укрытием}, при условии, что у вас состояние {@condition Prone|XPHB|Лёжа}. Состояние {@condition Invisible|XPHB|Невидимый} от этого действия {@action Hide|XPHB|Скрытие} прекращается, если у вас нет состояния {@condition Prone|XPHB|Лёжа}.
deadeye.features.2.details.snipersNest: Если вы совершаете бросок атаки, будучи скрытым, и бросок промахивается, совершение броска атаки не раскрывает вашего местонахождения.
deadeye.features.2.name: Скрытая позиция
deadeye.features.3.description: Каждый раз, когда существо промахивается по вам броском атаки, вы можете {@action Reaction|XPHB|Реакцией} прекратить состояние {@condition Prone|XPHB|Лёжа} на себе и переместиться на расстояние вплоть до половины вашей {@variantrule Speed|XPHB|Скорости}.
deadeye.features.3.name: Перепозиционирование
deadeye.features.4.description: Когда вы совершаете действие {@action Attack|XPHB|Атака}, вы можете решить совершить только один бросок дальнобойной атаки оружием, чтобы сделать Сосредоточенный выстрел. Вы совершаете этот бросок атаки с {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом}, и при попадании наносите {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Критическое попадание}.
deadeye.features.4.name: Сосредоточенный выстрел
deadeye.name: Меткий Стрелок
deadeye.shortDescription: Стреляйте с точностью прямо в яблочко
devotion.description: The Oath of Devotion binds a Paladin to the loftiest ideals of justice and order. These Paladins meet the ideal of the knight in shining armor. They hold themselves to the highest standards of conduct, and some, for better or worse, hold the rest of the world to the same standards. Many who swear this oath are devoted to gods of law and good and use their gods' tenets as the measure of their devotion.
devotion.features.0.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Devotion Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
devotion.features.0.name: Oath of Devotion Spells
devotion.features.1.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to imbue one Melee weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For 10 minutes or until you use this feature again, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls you make with that weapon (minimum bonus of +1), and each time you hit with it, you cause it to deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. The weapon also emits {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} 20 feet beyond that. You can end this effect early (no action required). This effect also ends if you aren't carrying the weapon.
devotion.features.1.name: Sacred Weapon
devotion.features.2.description: You and your allies have Immunity to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition while in your Aura of Protection. If a {@condition Charmed|XPHB} ally enters the aura, that condition has no effect on that ally while there.
devotion.features.2.name: Aura of Devotion
devotion.features.3.description: Your magical smite now radiates protective energy. Whenever you cast {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}, you and your allies have {@variantrule Half Cover|XPHB} while in your Aura of Protection. The aura has this benefit until the start of your next turn.
devotion.features.3.name: Smite of Protection
devotion.features.4.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can imbue your Aura of Protection with holy power, granting the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Holy Ward.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any saving throw you are forced to make by a Fiend or an Undead. {@b Radiant Damage.} Whenever an enemy starts its turn in the aura, that creature takes Radiant damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. {@b Sunlight.} The aura is filled with {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} that is sunlight.
devotion.features.4.name: Holy Nimbus
devotion.name: Клятва Преданности
devotion.shortDescription: Uphold the Ideals of Justice and Order
devourer.description: Жители Этариса не всегда хорошо отзываются об Охотниках на монстров, и многие считают их столь же испорченными и бесчеловечными, как и зло, которое они побеждают. Многие ужасающие истории об Охотниках на монстров можно прямо или косвенно связать с Гильдией Пожирателей, которых обвиняют в том, что они чудовищные людоеды. Правда ненамного лучше. Пожиратели проводят дни, поедая плоть и кровь убитых ими монстров, и со временем их обмен веществ изменился, чтобы выносить эту отвратительную практику. Пожиратели обретают мутации вскоре после поедания добычи.
devourer.features.0.description: Вы получаете владение {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB|Снаряжением алхимика} и {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB|Поварскими принадлежностями}.
devourer.features.0.name: Алхимическая гастрономия
devourer.features.1.description: Вы получаете способность поглощать останки монстров, что заставляет ваше тело обретать мощные и пугающие мутации. Они описаны в разделе «Мутации» далее в описании подкласса.
devourer.features.1.details.consumingPor: {@b Поглощение порций.} {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием} вы можете поглотить порцию. После поглощения порции вы выбираете мутацию, которую получите, в зависимости от типа существа. Вы можете получать выгоду от поглощённых порций количество раз, равное 1 + ваш модификатор Интеллекта (минимум 1). Когда вы завершаете {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}, вы восстанавливаете способность безопасно поглощать порции. Вы можете получать выгоду от нескольких порций одновременно, но не можете получить одну и ту же мутацию более одного раза одновременно.
devourer.features.1.details.salvagingPor: {@b Заготовка порций.} Действием {@action Utilize|XPHB|Применение} вы можете извлечь небольшую порцию из физических останков существа. С каждого существа можно извлечь только одну порцию. Запишите тип существа монстра. Извлечённая порция сохраняется до завершения {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгого отдыха}, после чего теряет свою силу.
devourer.features.1.name: Преобразующий метаболизм
devourer.features.2.description: Мутации представлены здесь в алфавитном порядке, причём требуемые типы существ указаны в скобках после названия мутации.
devourer.features.2.details.acidBlood: {@b Кислотная кровь (Аберрация, Слизь, Растение).} В течение 1 минуты ваша кровь становится кислотной. Каждый раз, когда существо в пределах 5 футов от вас попадает по вам атакой, оно получает {@damage 2d6} урона Кислотой.
devourer.features.2.details.acumen: {@b Проницательность (Небожитель, Исчадие, Фея).} В течение 1 часа вы совершаете с {@status Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} проверки Мудрости ({@skill Insight|XPHB|Проницательность}). Кроме того, выберите {@skill Intimidation|XPHB|Запугивание} или {@skill Persuasion|XPHB|Убеждение}. Вы совершаете с {@status Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} проверки характеристик с использованием выбранного навыка.
devourer.features.2.details.awareness: {@b Осознанность (Все).} В течение 10 минут вы знаете местонахождение существ того же типа, что и поглощённое вами существо, в пределах 60 футов от вас, не находящихся за Полным укрытием.
devourer.features.2.details.flight: {@b Полёт (Небожитель, Дракон, Элементаль).} В течение 10 минут на вашей спине вырастают пернатые, кожистые или призрачные крылья. Вы получаете Скорость полёта, равную вашей Скорости. Вы не можете получать выгоду от этой мутации, нося {@item Heavy armor|XPHB|Тяжёлый доспех}.
devourer.features.2.details.infused: {@b Напитанный (Дракон, Элементаль, Слизь).} Выберите один из следующих типов урона: Кислота, Холод, Огонь, Электричество, Яд или Звук. В течение 10 минут каждый раз, когда вы попадаете атакой, вы можете заставить её наносить выбранный тип вместо обычного типа урона.
devourer.features.2.details.invisibility: {@b Невидимость (Фея, Исчадие, Нежить).} В течение 10 минут у вас состояние {@condition Invisible|XPHB|Невидимый}. Этот эффект прекращается раньше, если вы отменяете его (действие не требуется), атакуете или сотворяете заклинание.
devourer.features.2.details.might: {@b Мощь (Зверь, Конструкт, Великан).} В течение 1 минуты вы совершаете с {@status Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} проверки и спасброски Силы. Вы также совершаете с {@status Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} броски атаки с использованием Силы и наносите дополнительно {@damage 1d6} урона целям, по которым попадаете атакой на основе Силы. Урон того же типа, что наносит атака.
devourer.features.2.details.naturalWeapo: {@b Природное оружие (Зверь, Дракон, Чудовище).} В течение 1 часа вы получаете природное оружие. Вы можете бросать {@dice 1d6} вместо обычного урона вашего {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Безоружного удара}. Этот урон может быть Колющим или Рубящим. Вы выбираете каждый раз при совершении атаки. Вы также можете совершать {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Безоружный удар} {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием}. Вы можете использовать модификатор Ловкости вместо модификатора Силы для бросков атаки и урона ваших {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB|Безоружных ударов}.
devourer.features.2.details.regeneration: {@b Регенерация (Зверь, Великан, Чудовище).} Вы восстанавливаете {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Хиты} в количестве, равном {@dice 2d8} + ваш уровень Охотника на монстров.
devourer.features.2.details.resistance: {@b Сопротивление (Конструкт, Элементаль, Нежить).} В течение 1 минуты вы обладаете {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Сопротивлением} к Дробящему, Колющему и Рубящему урону.
devourer.features.2.details.telepathy: {@b Телепатия (Аберрация, Исчадие, Растение).} В течение 1 часа вы можете телепатически говорить с любым видимым вами существом в пределах 60 футов от себя. Ваши телепатические послания идут на известном вам языке, и существо понимает вас, только если знает этот язык. Кроме того, вы обладаете {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Сопротивлением} к Психическому урону.
devourer.features.2.name: Мутации
devourer.features.3.description: Поглощение монстров обостряет ваше понимание их и их поведения. Вы получаете этот дополнительный эффект, когда поглощаете порцию монстра: в течение 1 минуты, когда вы совершаете атаку как часть {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Реакции}, вы наносите дополнительно {@damage 1d6} урона. Урон того же типа, что и оружие или {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Безоружный удар}, использованные для атаки.
devourer.features.3.name: Синхронизированный отклик
devourer.features.4.description: Ваш голод по врагу позволяет вам отведать его сущности во время битвы. Когда вы наносите урон существу рукопашной атакой оружием или {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Безоружным ударом}, вы получаете {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Временные хиты} в количестве, равном половине нанесённого урона. Если цель — тип существа из вашего Гримуара монстров, вы вместо этого получаете {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Временные хиты} в количестве, равном нанесённому урону. Вы можете использовать это умение количество раз, равное вашему модификатору Интеллекта (минимум 1 раз). Вы восстанавливаете все потраченные использования, когда завершаете {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Короткий отдых} или {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}.
devourer.features.4.name: Грызущий голод
devourer.features.5.description: Вы можете потратить 1 час и алхимические ингредиенты стоимостью 20 ЗМ (например, особые травы или заготовки монстров), чтобы своим {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB|Снаряжением алхимика} превратить порцию монстра в декокт. Декокт — это магическое зелье, дарующее преимущества поглощённой порции монстра. Вы можете иметь активными до 4 непоглощённых декоктов. Вы можете уничтожить декокт действием {@action Utilize|XPHB|Применение}. Существо, отличное от вас, может поглотить 1 декокт без неблагоприятных эффектов. Существо получает 1 уровень {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB|Истощения} за каждый декокт, который оно поглощает после первого. Существо должно завершить {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}, прежде чем оно восстановит способность безопасно поглощать декокт.
devourer.features.5.name: Алхимические декокты
devourer.features.6.description: Ваш голод по порциям монстров возрос до уровня ненасытного. Теперь вы можете безопасно поглощать на 1 порцию больше. Кроме того, в течение 1 минуты после поглощения порции вы совершаете с {@status Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} броски атаки, совершаемые как часть {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Реакции}.
devourer.features.6.name: Изысканный вкус
devourer.name: Гильдия Пожирателей
divine-soul.description: Sometimes the spark of magic that fuels a sorcerer comes from a divine source that glimmers within the soul. Having such a blessed soul is a sign that your innate magic might come from a distant but powerful familial connection to a divine being. Perhaps your ancestor was an angel, transformed into a mortal and sent to fight in a god's name. Or your birth might align with an ancient prophecy, marking you as a servant of the gods or a chosen vessel of divine magic. A Divine Soul, with a natural magnetism, is seen as a threat by some religious hierarchies. As an outsider who commands sacred power, a Divine Soul can undermine an existing order by claiming a direct tie to the divine. In some cultures, only those who can claim the power of a Divine Soul may command religious power. In these lands, ecclesiastical positions are dominated by a few bloodlines and preserved over generations.
divine-soul.features.0.description: Your link to the divine allows you to learn spells from the cleric class. When your Spellcasting feature lets you learn or replace a sorcerer cantrip or a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose the new spell from the {@filter Cleric spell list} or the {@filter Sorcerer spell list}. You must otherwise obey all the restrictions for selecting the spell, and it becomes a sorcerer spell for you. In addition, choose an affinity for the source of your divine power: good, evil, law, chaos, or neutrality. You learn an additional spell based on that affinity, as shown below. It is a sorcerer spell for you, but it doesn't count against your number of sorcerer spells known. If you later replace this spell, you must replace it with a spell from the {@filter Cleric spell list}.
divine-soul.features.0.name: Divine Magic
divine-soul.features.1.description: Divine power guards your destiny. If you fail a saving throw or miss with an attack roll, you can roll {@dice 2d4} and add it to the total, possibly changing the outcome. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
divine-soul.features.1.name: Favored by the Gods
divine-soul.features.2.description: The divine energy coursing through you can empower healing spells. Whenever you or an ally within 5 feet of you rolls dice to determine the number of hit points a spell restores, you can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll any number of those dice once, provided you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. You can use this feature only once per turn.
divine-soul.features.2.name: Empowered Healing
divine-soul.features.3.description:
You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to manifest a pair of spectral wings from your back. While the wings are present, you have a flying speed of 30 feet. The wings last until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, you die, or you dismiss them as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.

The affinity you chose for your Divine Magic feature determines the appearance of the spectral wings: eagle wings for good or law, bat wings for evil or chaos, and dragonfly wings for neutrality.
divine-soul.features.3.name: Otherworldly Wings
divine-soul.features.4.description:
You gain the ability to overcome grievous injuries. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} when you have fewer than half of your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} remaining, you can regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your Hit Point maximum.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
divine-soul.features.4.name: Unearthly Recovery
divine-soul.name: Божественная Душа
divine-soul.shortDescription: Channel Divine Power Through Your Soul
diviner.description: The counsel of a Diviner is sought by those who want a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future. As a Diviner, you strive to part the veils of space, time, and consciousness. You work to master spells of discernment, remote viewing, supernatural knowledge, and foresight.
diviner.features.0.description:
Choose two Wizard spells from the Divination school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Divination school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
diviner.features.0.name: Divination Savant
diviner.features.1.description:
Glimpses of the future begin to press on your awareness. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, roll two {@dice d20}s and record the numbers rolled. You can replace any {@dice D20 Test} made by you or a creature that you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you can replace a roll in this way only once per turn.

Each foretelling roll can be used only once. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you lose any unused foretelling rolls.
diviner.features.1.name: Portent
diviner.features.2.description: Casting Divination spells comes so easily to you that it expends only a fraction of your spellcasting efforts. When you cast a Divination spell using a level 2+ spell slot, you regain one expended spell slot. The slot you regain must be of a level lower than the slot you expended and can't be higher than level 5.
diviner.features.2.name: Expert Divination
diviner.features.3.description: You can increase your powers of perception. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you start a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
diviner.features.3.details.darkvision: {@b Darkvision.} You gain {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 120 feet.
diviner.features.3.details.greaterCompre: {@b Greater Comprehension.} You can read any language.
diviner.features.3.details.seeInvisibili: {@b See Invisibility.} You can cast {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} without expending a spell slot.
diviner.features.3.name: The Third Eye
diviner.features.4.description: The visions in your dreams intensify and paint a more accurate picture in your mind of what is to come. Roll three {@dice d20}s for your Portent feature rather than two.
diviner.features.4.name: Greater Portent
diviner.name: Прорицатель
diviner.shortDescription: Learn the Secrets of the Multiverse
draconic.description: Your innate magic comes from the gift of a dragon. Perhaps an ancient dragon facing death bequeathed some of its magical power to you or your ancestor. You might have absorbed magic from a site infused with dragons' power. Or perhaps you handled a treasure taken from a dragon's hoard that was steeped in draconic power. Or you might have a dragon for an ancestor.
draconic.features.0.description: The magic in your body manifests physical traits of your draconic gift. Your Hit Point maximum increases by 3, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Sorcerer level. Parts of you are also covered by dragon-like scales. While you aren't wearing armor, your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 10 plus your Dexterity modifier and Charisma modifier.
draconic.features.0.name: Draconic Resilience
draconic.features.1.description: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Draconic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
draconic.features.1.name: Draconic Spells
draconic.features.2.description: Your draconic magic has an affinity with a damage type associated with dragons. Choose one of those types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. You have Resistance to that damage type, and when you cast a spell that deals damage of that type, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell.
draconic.features.2.name: Elemental Affinity
draconic.features.3.description:
As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can cause draconic wings to appear on your back. The wings last for 1 hour or until you dismiss them (no action required). For the duration, you have a Fly Speed of 60 feet.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you spend 3 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.
draconic.features.3.name: Dragon Wings
draconic.features.4.description:
You can cast {@spell Summon Dragon|XPHB} without a Material component. You can also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting.
draconic.features.4.name: Dragon Companion
draconic.name: Драконья Кровь
drakewarden.description: Your connection to the natural world takes the form of a draconic spirit, which can manifest as a drake companion. As your powers grow, your drake companion grows as well, blossoming from a small creature into a majestic winged drake large enough to ride.
drakewarden.features.0.description: The bond you share with your drake creates a connection to dragonkind, granting you understanding and empowering your presence. You gain the following benefits.
drakewarden.features.0.details.thaumatur: {@b Thaumaturgy.} You learn the {@spell Thaumaturgy|XPHB} cantrip. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.
drakewarden.features.0.details.tongueOfD: {@b Tongue of Dragons.} You learn to speak, read, and write {@language Draconic|XPHB} or one other language of your choice.
drakewarden.features.0.name: Draconic Gift
drakewarden.features.1.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically summon the drake that is bound to you. It appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 30 feet of you. The drake is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands. See its game statistics in the {@creature Drake Companion} stat block, which uses your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (PB) in several places. When you summon the drake, choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. The chosen type determines the drake's Damage Immunity and the damage of its Infused Strikes trait.
drakewarden.features.1.details.combat: In combat, the drake shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its {@action Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, unless you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to command it to take another action. That action can be one in its stat block or some other action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the drake can take any action of its choice, not just {@action Dodge|XPHB}.
drakewarden.features.1.details.duration: The drake remains until it is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, until you use this feature to summon the drake again, or until you die. Anything the drake was wearing or carrying is left behind when the drake vanishes.
drakewarden.features.1.details.resummon: Once you summon the drake, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a spell slot of any level to summon it again.
drakewarden.features.1.name: Drake Companion
drakewarden.features.2.description: The bond you share with your drake intensifies, protecting you and stoking the drake's fury. When you summon your drake, it grows wings on its back and gains a flying speed equal to its walking speed. In addition, while your drake is summoned, you and the drake gain the following benefits.
drakewarden.features.2.details.drakeMoun: {@b Drake Mount.} The drake grows to Medium size. Reflecting your special bond, you can use the drake as a mount if your size is Medium or smaller. While you are riding your drake, it can't use the flying speed of this feature.
drakewarden.features.2.details.magicFang: {@b Magic Fang.} The drake's Bite attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the type chosen for the drake's Draconic Essence.
drakewarden.features.2.details.resistanc: {@b Resistance.} You gain resistance to the damage type chosen for the drake's Draconic Essence.
drakewarden.features.2.name: Bond of Fang and Scale
drakewarden.features.3.description: As an action, you can exhale a 30-foot cone of damaging breath or cause your drake to exhale it. Choose acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage (your choice doesn't have to match your drake's Draconic Essence). Each creature in the cone must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 8d6} damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. This damage increases to {@damage 10d6} when you reach 15th level in this class. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 3rd level or higher to use it again.
drakewarden.features.3.name: Drake's Breath
drakewarden.features.4.description: Your bond to your drake reaches the pinnacle of its power. While your drake is summoned, you and the drake gain the following benefits.
drakewarden.features.4.details.empowered: {@b Empowered Bite.} The drake's Bite attack deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the type chosen for its Draconic Essence (for a total of {@damage 2d6} extra damage).
drakewarden.features.4.details.largeDrak: {@b Large Drake.} The drake grows to Large size. When you ride your drake, it is no longer prohibited from using the flying speed of Bond of Fang and Scale.
drakewarden.features.4.details.reflexive: {@b Reflexive Resistance.} When either you or the drake takes damage while you're within 30 feet of each other, you can use your reaction to give yourself or the drake resistance to that instance of damage. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
drakewarden.features.4.name: Perfected Bond
drakewarden.name: Хранитель Дрейков
dreams.description: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature. Druids who are members of the Circle of Dreams hail from regions that have strong ties to the Feywild and its dreamlike realms. The druids' guardianship of the natural world makes for a natural alliance between them and good-aligned fey. These druids seek to fill the world with dreamy wonder. Their magic mends wounds and brings joy to downcast hearts, and the realms they protect are gleaming, fruitful places, where dream and reality blur together and where the weary can find rest.
dreams.features.0.description: You become imbued with the blessings of the Summer Court. You are a font of energy that offers respite from injuries. You have a pool of fey energy represented by a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Druid level. As a bonus action, you can choose one creature you can see within 120 feet of you and spend a number of those dice equal to half your druid level or less. Roll the spent dice and add them together. The target regains a number of hit points equal to the total. The target also gains 1 temporary hit point per die spent. You regain all expended dice when you finish a long rest.
dreams.features.0.name: Balm of the Summer Court
dreams.features.1.description: Home can be wherever you are. During a short or long rest, you can invoke the shadowy power of the Gleaming Court to help guard your respite. At the start of the rest, you touch a point in space, and an invisible, 30-foot-radius sphere of magic appears, centered on that point. Total cover blocks the sphere. While within the sphere, you and your allies gain a +5 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) and Wisdom (Perception) checks, and any light from open flames in the sphere (a campfire, torches, or the like) isn't visible outside it. The sphere vanishes at the end of the rest or when you leave the sphere.
dreams.features.1.name: Hearth of Moonlight and Shadow
dreams.features.2.description: You can use the hidden, magical pathways that some fey use to traverse space in the blink of an eye. As a bonus action on your turn, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Alternatively, you can use your action to teleport one willing creature you touch up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.
dreams.features.2.name: Hidden Paths
dreams.features.3.description: The magic of the Feywild grants you the ability to travel mentally or physically through dreamlands. When you finish a short rest, you can cast one of the following spells, without expending a spell slot or requiring material components: {@spell Dream} (with you as the messenger), {@spell Scrying}, or {@spell Teleportation Circle}. This use of {@spell Teleportation Circle} is special. Rather than opening a portal to a permanent teleportation circle, it opens a portal to the last place where you finished a long rest on your current plane of existence. If you haven't taken a long rest on your current plane, the spell fails but isn't wasted. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
dreams.features.3.name: Walker in Dreams
dreams.name: Круг Грёз
drunken-master.description: The Way of the Drunken Master teaches its students to move with the jerky, unpredictable movements of a drunkard. A drunken master sways, tottering on unsteady feet, to present what seems like an incompetent combatant who proves frustrating to engage. The drunken master's erratic stumbles conceal a carefully executed dance of blocks, parries, advances, attacks, and retreats. A drunken master often enjoys playing the fool to bring gladness to the despondent or to demonstrate humility to the arrogant, but when battle is joined, the drunken master can be a maddening, masterful foe.
drunken-master.features.0.description: When you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance} skill if you don't already have it. Your martial arts technique mixes combat training with the precision of a dancer and the antics of a jester. You also gain proficiency with {@item brewer's supplies|XPHB} if you don't already have it.
drunken-master.features.0.name: Bonus Proficiencies
drunken-master.features.1.description: At 3rd level, you learn how to twist and turn quickly as part of your Flurry of Blows. Whenever you use Flurry of Blows, you gain the benefit of the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action, and your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the current turn.
drunken-master.features.1.name: Drunken Technique
drunken-master.features.2.description: Starting at 6th level, you can move in sudden, swaying ways. You gain the following benefits.
drunken-master.features.2.details.leapTo: {@b Leap to Your Feet.} When you're {@condition prone|XPHB}, you can stand up by spending 5 feet of movement, rather than half your speed.
drunken-master.features.2.details.redire: {@b Redirect Attack.} When a creature misses you with a melee attack roll, you can spend 1 ki point as a reaction to cause that attack to hit one creature of your choice, other than the attacker, that you can see within 5 feet of you.
drunken-master.features.2.name: Tipsy Sway
drunken-master.features.3.description: Starting at 11th level, you always seem to get a lucky bounce at the right moment. When you make an ability check, an attack roll, or a saving throw and have {@variantrule disadvantage|XPHB} on the roll, you can spend 2 ki points to cancel the disadvantage for that roll.
drunken-master.features.3.name: Drunkard's Luck
drunken-master.features.4.description: At 17th level, you gain the ability to make an overwhelming number of attacks against a group of enemies. When you use your Flurry of Blows, you can make up to three additional attacks with it (up to a total of five Flurry of Blows attacks), provided that each Flurry of Blows attack targets a different creature this turn.
drunken-master.features.4.name: Intoxicated Frenzy
drunken-master.name: Путь Пьяного Мастера
echo-knight.description: A mysterious and feared frontline warrior of the Kryn Dynasty, the Echo Knight has mastered the art of using dunamis to summon the fading shades of unrealized timelines to aid them in battle. Surrounded by echoes of their own might, they charge into the fray as a cycling swarm of shadows and strikes.
echo-knight.features.0.description: 3rd-level Echo Knight feature. You can use a bonus action to magically manifest an echo of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within 15 feet of you. This echo is a magical, translucent, gray image of you that lasts until it is destroyed, until you dismiss it as a bonus action, until you manifest another echo, or until you're {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}. Your echo has AC 14 + your proficiency bonus, 1 hit point, and immunity to all conditions. If it has to make a saving throw, it uses your saving throw bonus for the roll. It is the same size as you, and it occupies its space. On your turn, you can mentally command the echo to move up to 30 feet in any direction (no action required). If your echo is ever more than 30 feet from you at the end of your turn, it is destroyed.
echo-knight.features.0.details.attackFro: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, any attack you make with that action can originate from your space or the echo's space. You make this choice for each attack.
echo-knight.features.0.details.opportuni: When a creature that you can see within 5 feet of your echo moves at least 5 feet away from it, you can use your reaction to make an opportunity attack against that creature as if you were in the echo's space.
echo-knight.features.0.details.teleport: As a bonus action, you can teleport, magically swapping places with your echo at a cost of 15 feet of your movement, regardless of the distance between the two of you.
echo-knight.features.0.name: Manifest Echo
echo-knight.features.1.description: You can heighten your echo's fury. Whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make one additional melee attack from the echo's position. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
echo-knight.features.1.name: Unleash Incarnation
echo-knight.features.2.description: You can temporarily transfer your consciousness to your echo. As an action, you can see through your echo's eyes and hear through its ears. During this time, you are {@condition deafened|XPHB} and {@condition blinded|XPHB}. You can sustain this effect for up to 10 minutes, and you can end it at any time (requires no action). While your echo is being used in this way, it can be up to 1,000 feet away from you without being destroyed.
echo-knight.features.2.name: Echo Avatar
echo-knight.features.3.description: You can make your echo throw itself in front of an attack directed at another creature that you can see. Before the attack roll is made, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to teleport the echo to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the targeted creature. The attack roll that triggered the reaction is instead made against your echo. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
echo-knight.features.3.name: Shadow Martyr
echo-knight.features.4.description: You've learned to absorb the fleeting magic of your echo. When an echo of yours is destroyed by taking damage, you can gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d6} + your Constitution modifier, provided you don't already have temporary hit points. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
echo-knight.features.4.name: Reclaim Potential
echo-knight.features.5.description: You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to create two echoes with your Manifest Echo feature, and these echoes can coexist. If you try to create a third echo, the previous two echoes are destroyed. Anything you can do from one echo's position can be done from the other's instead. In addition, when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no uses of your Unleash Incarnation feature left, you regain one use of that feature.
echo-knight.features.5.name: Legion of One
echo-knight.name: Рыцарь Эха
eldritch-knight.description: Eldritch Knights combine the martial mastery common to all Fighters with a careful study of magic. Their spells both complement and extend their combat skills, providing additional protection to shore up their armor and also allowing them to engage many foes at once with explosive magic.
eldritch-knight.features.0.description: You have learned to cast spells. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules as an Eldritch Knight.
eldritch-knight.features.0.details.cantr: You know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Wizard spell list}. {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB} and {@spell Shocking Grasp|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Fighter level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Wizard spell list}. When you reach Fighter level 10, you learn another {@filter Wizard|XPHB} cantrip of your choice.
eldritch-knight.features.0.details.chang: Whenever you gain a Fighter level, you can replace one spell on your list with another {@filter Wizard|XPHB} spell for which you have spell slots.
eldritch-knight.features.0.details.prepa: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose three level 1 spells from the {@filter Wizard spell list}. {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB}, {@spell Jump|XPHB}, and {@spell Shield|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Fighter levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Eldritch Knight Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Wizard spell list} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 7 Fighter, your list of prepared spells can include five Wizard spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.
eldritch-knight.features.0.details.spell: The Eldritch Knight Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your {@filter Wizard|XPHB} spells.
You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your {@filter Wizard|XPHB} spells.
eldritch-knight.features.0.name: Использование заклинаний
eldritch-knight.features.1.description: You learn a ritual that creates a magical bond between yourself and one weapon. You perform the ritual over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}. The weapon must be within your reach throughout the ritual, at the conclusion of which you touch the weapon and forge the bond. Once you have bonded a weapon to yourself, you can't be disarmed of that weapon unless you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. If it is on the same plane of existence, you can summon that weapon as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, causing it to teleport instantly to your hand. You can have up to two bonded weapons, but you can summon only one at a time with a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. If you attempt to bond with a third weapon, you must break the bond with one of the other two.
eldritch-knight.features.1.name: War Bond
eldritch-knight.features.2.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a casting of one of your {@filter Wizard|XPHB} cantrips that has a casting time of an action.
eldritch-knight.features.2.name: Военная Магия
eldritch-knight.features.3.description: You learn how to make your weapon strikes undercut a creature's ability to withstand your spells. When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon, that creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.
eldritch-knight.features.3.name: Eldritch Strike
eldritch-knight.features.4.description: When you use your Action Surge, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can teleport before or after the additional action.
eldritch-knight.features.4.name: Arcane Charge
eldritch-knight.features.5.description: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace two of the attacks with a casting of one of your level 1 or level 2 {@filter Wizard|XPHB} spells that has a casting time of an action.
eldritch-knight.features.5.name: Improved War Magic
eldritch-knight.name: Мистический Рыцарь
eldritch-knight.shortDescription: Support Combat Skills with Arcane Magic
eldritch.description: The Eldritch Domain empowers the followers of the unknown and distant forces of chaos, divine entities of eldritch oblivion, and dead gods. Wield the Power that Killed the Gods. Even the most benevolent divine beings operate outside the bounds of mortal comprehension. These eldritch forces sing a siren song that calls to mortal worshippers through lucid dreams and terrible whispers.
eldritch.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Eldritch Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
eldritch.features.0.name: Eldritch Domain Spells
eldritch.features.1.description: You've been gifted with the ability to impart a fleeting taste of the unknowable on others. When you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast a spell using a spell slot that targets one or more creatures, you can force one target of the original spell to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, roll on the Eldritch Effects table, and the target creature suffers that effect for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. This effect ends early if you use this feature.
eldritch.features.1.name: Eldritch Contagion
eldritch.features.2.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you expend a use of your Channel Divinity to choose a point within 120 feet of you that you can see and roll on the Eldritch Effects table. Each creature in a 15-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} centered on that point must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or suffer the rolled effect for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.
eldritch.features.2.name: Prophecy of Doom
eldritch.features.3.description: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions. Additionally, your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means unless you allow it. The attempt automatically fails, and the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or take Psychic damage equal to your Cleric level.
eldritch.features.3.name: Otherworldly Calm
eldritch.features.4.description: When a creature fails a Wisdom saving throw against your Prophecy of Doom feature, you can deal {@damage 10d10} Psychic damage to it. Once a creature takes damage in this way, it is immune to this effect for 10 minutes, after which it can be affected again.
eldritch.features.4.name: Sing the Song that Ends the World
eldritch.name: Домен Древнего Ужаса
eldritch.shortDescription: Wield the Power that Killed the Gods
elements.description: Warriors of the Elements tap into the power of the Elemental Planes. Harnessing their supernatural focus, these Monks momentarily tame the energy of the Elemental Chaos to empower themselves in and out of battle.
elements.features.0.description: At the start of your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point to imbue yourself with elemental energy. The energy lasts for 10 minutes or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. You gain the following benefits while this feature is active.
elements.features.0.details.elementalStr: {@b Elemental Strikes.} Whenever you hit with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can cause it to deal your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage rather than its normal damage type. When you deal one of these types with it, you can also force the target to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you can move the target up to 10 feet toward or away from you, as elemental energy swirls around it.
elements.features.0.details.reach: {@b Reach.} When you make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, your reach is 10 feet greater than normal, as elemental energy extends from you.
elements.features.0.name: Elemental Attunement
elements.features.1.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 2 Focus Points and choose a point within 120 feet of yourself. A burst of elemental energy erupts in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} centered on that point. Each creature in the {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage equal to three rolls of your Martial Arts die. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. You choose the damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.
elements.features.1.name: Elemental Burst
elements.features.2.description: When you use your Elemental Attunement feature, you also gain a Fly Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for the duration of that feature.
elements.features.2.name: Stride of the Elements
elements.features.3.description: When you use your Elemental Attunement feature, you also gain the following benefits for the duration of that feature.
elements.features.3.details.damageResist: {@b Damage Resistance.} You gain Resistance to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. At the start of each of your turns, you can change this choice.
elements.features.3.details.destructiveS: {@b Destructive Stride.} When you use your Step of the Wind, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 20 feet until the end of the turn. For that duration, any creature of your choice takes damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die when you enter a space within 5 feet of it. The damage type is your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.
elements.features.3.details.empoweredStr: {@b Empowered Strikes.} Once on each of your turns, you can deal extra damage to a target equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die when you hit it with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. The extra damage is the same type you chose for the Damage Resistance in this feature.
elements.features.3.name: Elemental Epitome
elements.name: Воин Стихий
eloquence.description: Adherents of the College of Eloquence master the art of oratory. Persuasion is regarded as a high art, and a well-reasoned, well-spoken argument often proves more persuasive than facts. These bards wield a blend of logic and theatrical wordplay, winning over skeptics and detractors with logical arguments and plucking at heartstrings to appeal to the emotions of audiences.
eloquence.features.0.description: You are a master at saying the right thing at the right time. When you make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) or Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
eloquence.features.0.name: Silver Tongue
eloquence.features.1.description: You can spin words laced with magic that unsettle a creature and cause it to doubt itself. As a bonus action, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration and choose one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. Roll the Bardic Inspiration die. The creature must subtract the number rolled from the next saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.
eloquence.features.1.name: Unsettling Words
eloquence.features.2.description: Your inspiring words are so persuasive that others feel driven to succeed. When a creature adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to its ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll fails, the creature can keep the Bardic Inspiration die.
eloquence.features.2.name: Unfailing Inspiration
eloquence.features.3.description:
You have gained the ability to make your speech intelligible to any creature. As an action, choose one or more creatures within 60 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). The chosen creatures can magically understand you, regardless of the language you speak, for 1 hour.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
eloquence.features.3.name: Universal Speech
eloquence.features.4.description:
When you successfully inspire someone, the power of your eloquence can now spread to others. When a creature within 60 feet of you adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to its ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll succeeds, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to encourage a different creature (other than yourself) that can hear you within 60 feet of you, giving it a Bardic Inspiration die without expending any of your Bardic Inspiration uses.

You can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
eloquence.features.4.name: Infectious Inspiration
eloquence.name: Коллегия Красноречия
entropy.description: Druids of the Circle of Entropy see doom as a constant of the natural world. Lives end, works erode, and the civilizations of Etharis ultimately come to ruin. The inevitable end of the old brings opportunity for the new. These fatalistic Druids see themselves as agents of ruin, ending those whose time has passed to make space for those whose time has yet to come.
entropy.features.0.description: You have mastered talents, both magical and martial, in your pursuit of the inevitable destruction of mortals and their works. When you finish a {@variantrule Short|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you gain one of the following benefits until you finish your next {@variantrule Short|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
entropy.features.0.details.elementalCata: {@b Elemental Cataclysm.} As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a spell slot to cause elemental energy to burst in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} centered on a point within 60 feet of yourself. Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, or Lightning. Each creature in the {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes {@dice 1d6} damage of the chosen type per level of the spell slot expended, and then has {@status Vulnerability} to that damage type for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the {@status Vulnerability} on a success.
entropy.features.0.details.ruinousSmite: {@b Ruinous Smite.} Once per turn when you deal damage with an attack with a weapon or an {@action Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can choose to expend a spell slot to deal an extra {@dice 1d8} Necrotic damage, plus another {@dice 1d8} per level of the spell slot.
entropy.features.0.details.weaponMastery: {@b Weapon Mastery.} Your supernatural connection to destruction allows you to use the mastery property of one kind of weapon of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as {@item Shortbows|XPHB} or {@item Quarterstaffs|XPHB}. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change the kind of weapon you choose.
entropy.features.0.name: Catastrophic Power
entropy.features.1.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to adopt an aspect of entropy and the inevitable end of all things for 10 minutes. You gain the following benefits: {@b All Things Pass.} You have {@status Advantage} on attack rolls against {@status Bloodied} creatures. {@b Inexorable Onslaught.} You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn. {@b Ironskin Armor.} Your base AC becomes 17 plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1) if your AC is lower than that.
entropy.features.1.name: Ruin Incarnate
entropy.features.2.description: Your mystical connection to catastrophe and destruction grows stronger. You gain the following benefits. {@b Elemental Assault.} While your Ruin Incarnate feature is active, whenever you hit with a weapon or an {@action Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can cause it to deal your choice of Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Necrotic damage rather than its normal damage type. {@b Increased Might.} While your Ruin Incarnate is active, you can add your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1) to your Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
entropy.features.2.name: Many Roads to Ruin
entropy.features.3.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can strike the earth with a thunderous tremor and grow. When you do so, your size increases by one category (from Medium to Large, for example) for 10 minutes. In addition, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from your new form must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, or have the {@condition Prone} condition. The tremor deals Bludgeoning damage to each structure in contact with the ground in the area. To determine this damage, roll a number of {@dice d4}s equal to your Druid level. While your size is increased by this feature, your attacks with weapons and {@action Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} deal an extra {@dice 1d4} damage on a hit. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
entropy.features.3.name: Shake the Earth
entropy.features.4.description: You have mastered the ability to hasten the inevitable slide toward entropy. You gain the following benefits. {@b Enhanced Ruinous Smite.} Until the end of your next turn, any creature affected by your Ruinous Smite suffers a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19-20 on the {@dice d20}. {@b Improved Inexorable Onslaught.} While your Ruin Incarnate feature is active, you can attack with a weapon or an {@action Unarmed Strike|XPHB} three times instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn. {@b World Breaker.} You regain your use of the Shake the Earth feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
entropy.features.4.name: Entropy's Apex
entropy.name: Круг Энтропии
entropy.shortDescription: Become an Instrument of Decay and Ruin
evoker.description: Your studies focus on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid. Some Evokers find employment in military forces, serving as artillery to blast armies from afar. Others use their power to protect others, while some seek their own gain.
evoker.features.0.description:
Choose two Wizard spells from the Evocation school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Evocation school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
evoker.features.0.name: Evocation Savant
evoker.features.1.description: Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When you cast a cantrip at a creature and you miss with the attack roll or the target succeeds on a saving throw against the cantrip, the target takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.
evoker.features.1.name: Potent Cantrip
evoker.features.2.description: You can create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your evocations. When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 plus the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.
evoker.features.2.name: Sculpt Spells
evoker.features.3.description: Whenever you cast a Wizard spell from the Evocation school, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell.
evoker.features.3.name: Empowered Evocation
evoker.features.4.description:
You can increase the power of your spells. When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot of levels 1–5 that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell on the turn you cast it.

The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you take {@dice 2d12} Necrotic damage for each level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and Immunity. Each time you use this feature again before finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the Necrotic damage per spell level increases by {@dice 1d12}.
evoker.features.4.name: Overchannel
evoker.name: Воплотитель
fathomless.description:
You have plunged into a pact with the deeps. An entity of the ocean, the Elemental Plane of Water, or another otherworldly sea now allows you to draw on its thalassic power. Is it merely using you to learn about terrestrial realms, or does it want you to open cosmic floodgates and drown the world?

Perhaps you were born into a generational cult that venerates the Fathomless and its spawn. Or you might have been shipwrecked and on the brink of drowning when your patron's grasp offered you a chance at life. Whatever the reason for your pact, the sea and its unknown depths call to you.

Entities of the deep that might empower a warlock include krakens, ancient water elementals, godlike hallucinations dreamed into being by kuo-toa, merfolk demigods, and sea hag covens.
fathomless.features.0.description: The Fathomless lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.
fathomless.features.0.name: Expanded Spell List
fathomless.features.1.description:
You can magically summon a spectral tentacle that strikes at your foes. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, create a 10-foot-long tentacle at a point you can see within 60 feet of you. The tentacle lasts for 1 minute or until you use this feature to create another tentacle.

When you create the tentacle, you can make a melee spell attack against one creature within 10 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes {@dice 1d8} cold damage, and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. When you reach 10th level in this class, the damage increases to {@dice 2d8}.

As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can move the tentacle up to 30 feet and repeat the attack.

You can summon the tentacle a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
fathomless.features.1.name: Tentacle of the Deeps
fathomless.features.2.description: You gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, and you can breathe underwater.
fathomless.features.2.name: Gift of the Sea
fathomless.features.3.description: You are now even more at home in the depths. You gain resistance to cold damage. In addition, when you are fully submerged, any creature that is also fully submerged can understand your speech, and you can understand theirs.
fathomless.features.3.name: Oceanic Soul
fathomless.features.4.description: Your Tentacle of the Deeps can defend you and others, interposing itself between them and harm. When you or a creature you can see takes damage while within 10 feet of the tentacle, you can use your reaction to choose one of those creatures and reduce the damage to that creature by {@dice 1d8}. When you reach 10th level in this class, the damage reduced by the tentacle increases to {@dice 2d8}.
fathomless.features.4.name: Guardian Coil
fathomless.features.5.description:
You learn the spell {@spell Evard's Black Tentacles}. It counts as a warlock spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of spells you know. You can also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

Whenever you cast this spell, your patron's magic bolsters you, granting you a number of temporary hit points equal to your warlock level. Moreover, damage can't break your {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} on this spell.
fathomless.features.5.name: Grasping Tentacles
fathomless.features.6.description:
You can magically open temporary conduits to watery destinations. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can teleport yourself and up to five other willing creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you. Amid a whirl of tentacles, you all vanish and then reappear up to 1 mile away in a body of water you've seen (pond size or larger) or within 30 feet of it, each of you appearing in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of the others.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
fathomless.features.6.name: Fathomless Plunge
fathomless.name: Бездонный
fathomless.shortDescription: Plunge into a Pact with the Deeps
fey-wanderer.description: A Fey Wanderer Ranger's mystical wanderings in the Feywild have transformed them, granting the beguiling charm and dreadful power of that enchanted plane. Your weapons and magic carry the power of the Feywild, allowing you to guard the mortal realm against threats from beyond the veil.
fey-wanderer.features.0.description: You can augment your weapon strikes with mind-scarring magic drawn from the gloomy hollows of the Feywild. When you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. The extra damage increases to {@damage 1d6} when you reach Ranger level 11.
fey-wanderer.features.0.name: Dreadful Strikes
fey-wanderer.features.1.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Fey Wanderer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
fey-wanderer.features.1.name: Fey Wanderer Spells
fey-wanderer.features.2.description: Whenever you make a Charisma check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum bonus of +1). You also gain proficiency in one of these skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}.
fey-wanderer.features.2.name: Otherworldly Glamour
fey-wanderer.features.3.description: The magic of the Feywild guards your mind. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. In addition, whenever you or a creature you can see within 120 feet of you succeeds on a saving throw to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to force a different creature you can see within 120 feet of yourself to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} (your choice) for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
fey-wanderer.features.3.name: Beguiling Twist
fey-wanderer.features.4.description: You can cast {@spell Summon Fey|XPHB} without a Material component. You can also cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB}. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting.
fey-wanderer.features.4.name: Fey Reinforcements
fey-wanderer.features.5.description: You can cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. In addition, whenever you cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, you can bring along one willing creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself. That creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of your destination space.
fey-wanderer.features.5.name: Misty Wanderer
fey-wanderer.name: Странник Фей
fiend.description: Your pact draws on the Lower Planes, the realms of perdition. You might forge a bargain with a demon lord such as Demogorgon or Orcus; an archdevil such as Asmodeus; or a pit fiend, balor, yugoloth, or night hag that is especially mighty. That patron's aims are evil—the corruption or destruction of all things, ultimately including you—and your path is defined by the extent to which you strive against those aims.
fiend.features.0.description: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Fiend Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
fiend.features.0.name: Fiend Spells
fiend.features.1.description: When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Warlock level (minimum of 1 Temporary Hit Point). You also gain this benefit if someone else reduces an enemy within 10 feet of you to 0 Hit Points.
fiend.features.1.name: Dark One's Blessing
fiend.features.2.description:
You can call on your fiendish patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add {@dice 1d10} to your roll. You can do so after seeing the roll but before any of the roll's effects occur.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), but you can use it no more than once per roll. You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
fiend.features.2.name: Dark One's Own Luck
fiend.features.3.description: Choose one damage type, other than Force, whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type until you choose a different one with this feature.
fiend.features.3.name: Fiendish Resilience
fiend.features.4.description:
Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can try to instantly transport the target through the Lower Planes. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, or the target disappears and hurtles through a nightmare landscape. The target takes {@dice 8d10} Psychic damage if it isn't a Fiend, and it has the {@status Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn, when it returns to the space it previously occupied or the nearest unoccupied space.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a Pact Magic spell slot (no action required) to restore your use of it.
fiend.features.4.name: Hurl Through Hell
fiend.name: Исчадие
fiend.shortDescription: Make a Deal with the Lower Planes
first-vampire.description: You have made a pact with a powerful vampire cursed by the gods or born as a creature of the night. Hundreds of haunted beings serve this vampire, so why enter a pact with you? Because you can act in the light of day unhindered? To cull weaker vampires?
first-vampire.features.0.description:
You gain an innate power to drain life from the living. After you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} or {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to make an Unarmed Strike. On a hit, the Unarmed Strike deals Necrotic damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Charisma modifier instead of its normal damage.

When you hit a creature with Drain Life, you can expend a Pact Magic spell slot to deal an extra {@dice 1d8} Necrotic damage to the target, plus another {@dice 1d8} per level of the spell slot. When you expend a spell slot in this way, you regain Hit Points equal to the amount of damage dealt.
first-vampire.features.0.name: Drain Life
first-vampire.features.1.description: As a predator of the night, you have been blessed with enhanced vision in darkness. You have {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision}, its range increases by 60 feet.
first-vampire.features.1.name: Nocturnal Predator
first-vampire.features.2.description: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the First Vampire Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
first-vampire.features.2.name: First Vampire Spells
first-vampire.features.3.description:
You always have the {@spell Polymorph} spell prepared. With this feature, you can cast it only on yourself without expending a spell slot and without Material components to transform into a {@creature Bat}, {@creature Rat}, or {@creature Wolf}.

Your game statistics are replaced by the Beast's stat block, but you retain your creature type; {@variantrule Hit Points}; {@variantrule Hit Point Dice}; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats. You also retain your skill and saving throw proficiencies and use your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus} for them, in addition to gaining the proficiencies of the creature. If a skill or saving throw modifier in the Beast's stat block is higher than yours, use the one in the stat block.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
first-vampire.features.3.name: Creature of the Night
first-vampire.features.4.description:
When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points with your Drain Life feature, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to consume the last of its fleeting mortality. When you do so, you regain one of your expended Pact Magic spell slots.
