Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
first-vampire.features.4.name: Eldritch Appetite
first-vampire.features.5.description:
Your vampire patron grants you a taste of true immortality. You no longer age, and you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can gain the following benefits for 1 minute:
first-vampire.features.5.details.enhance: When you use your Drain Life feature, you deal an extra {@dice 1d8} Necrotic damage without expending a spell slot.
first-vampire.features.5.details.regener: At the start of each of your turns, you regain {@dice 1d6} Hit Points if you have at least 1 Hit Point and aren't in direct sunlight or running water. If you take Radiant damage, you don't regain Hit Points from this feature at the start of your next turn.
first-vampire.features.5.details.uses: Once you use this {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
first-vampire.features.5.name: Eternal Night
first-vampire.name: Первый Вампир
fools.description: It's commonly believed the College of Fools is merely a front for a cult of Beleth. Bards in the College would say their relationship with the Arch Daemon of Fear is much more complicated. These Bards choose to laugh at Etharis's many horrors rather than be dismayed by them—a whimsical, if ghoulish, philosophy. Their delight in other creatures' terror encourages their outsized antics and cruel jests. One person's trick is another person's terror, after all.
fools.features.0.description: You know the {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB} cantrip. If you already know it, you learn a different Bard cantrip of your choice. The cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known. Additionally, you always have the {@spell Dissonant Whispers|XPHB} spell prepared. In addition, when you take the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Influence|XPHB} action on your turn, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on the same turn to cast {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB}.
fools.features.0.name: Antagonistic Antics
fools.features.1.description: When a creature that you can see or hear within 30 feet of yourself fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration, roll your Bardic Inspiration die and deal Psychic damage equal to the number rolled plus your Charisma modifier. In addition, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} it makes before the end of its next turn.
fools.features.1.name: Cruel Jest
fools.features.2.description: Your dark comedy keeps your allies from succumbing to fear. You and any creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you are immune to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. If a creature is already {@condition Frightened|XPHB} when it receives a Bardic Inspiration die from you, the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition ends on it.
fools.features.2.name: Gallows Humor
fools.features.3.description:
When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} and cast a Bard spell that has a casting time of an action. If the spell deals damage and targets the creature that reduced you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, that creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any saving throw against the spell.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
fools.features.3.name: Last Laugh
fools.name: Коллегия Шутов
forge.description: The gods of the forge are patrons of artisans who work with metal, from a humble blacksmith who keeps a village in horseshoes and plow blades to the mighty elf artisan whose diamond-tipped arrows of mithral have felled demon lords. The gods of the forge teach that, with patience and hard work, even the most intractable metal can be transformed from a lump of ore to a beautifully wrought object. Clerics of these deities search for objects lost to the forces of darkness, liberate mines overrun by orcs, and uncover rare and wondrous materials necessary to create potent magic items. Followers of these gods take great pride in their work, and they are willing to craft and use heavy armor and powerful weapons to protect them. Deities of this domain include {@deity Gond}, {@deity Reorx}, {@deity Onatar}, {@deity Moradin}, {@deity Hephaestus}, and {@deity Goibhniu}.
forge.features.0.description: At each indicated cleric level, add the listed spells to your spells prepared.
forge.features.0.name: Forge Domain Spells
forge.features.1.description: When you choose this domain, you gain proficiency with heavy armor and {@item smith's tools|XPHB}.
forge.features.1.name: Дополнительное владение
forge.features.2.description: At 1st level, you gain the ability to imbue magic into a weapon or armor. At the end of a long rest, you can touch one nonmagical object that is a suit of armor or a {@filter simple or martial weapon|XPHB}. Until the end of your next long rest or until you die, the object becomes a magic item, granting a +1 bonus to AC if it's armor or a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls if it's a weapon. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
forge.features.2.name: Blessing of the Forge
forge.features.3.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to create simple items. You conduct an hour-long ritual that crafts a nonmagical item that must include some metal: a {@filter simple or martial weapon|XPHB}, a suit of armor, ten pieces of ammunition, a set of tools, or another metal object (see chapter 5, "Equipment," in the Player's Handbook for examples of these items). The creation is completed at the end of the hour, appearing in an unoccupied space of your choice on a surface within 5 feet of you. The thing you create can be something that is worth no more than 100 gp. As part of this ritual, you must lay out metal, which can include coins, with a value equal to the creation. The metal irretrievably coalesces and transforms into the creation at the ritual's end, magically forming even nonmetal parts of the creation. The ritual can create a duplicate of a nonmagical item that contains metal, such as a key, if you possess the original during the ritual.
forge.features.3.name: Channel Divinity: Artisan's Blessing
forge.features.4.description: Starting at 6th level, your mastery of the forge grants you special abilities. You gain resistance to fire damage. While wearing heavy armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
forge.features.4.name: Soul of the Forge
forge.features.5.description: At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with the fiery power of the forge. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} fire damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to {@damage 2d8}.
forge.features.5.name: Divine Strike
forge.features.6.description: At 17th level, your blessed affinity with fire and metal becomes more powerful. You gain immunity to fire damage. While wearing heavy armor, you have resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.
forge.features.6.name: Saint of Forge and Fire
forge.name: Домен Кузни
fractured.description: Barbarians are defined by their rage, channelling it to unleash brief but potent destruction. Few are brave or unwise enough to study esoteric psychological techniques that split their rage from the rest of their psyche, dividing their identity into two parts: ego and id. When their ego is in control, the Fractured—as these Barbarians are known—possess self-control and cunning beyond most other Barbarians. When they allow their id to take control, their countenance turns monstrous and their bodies swell with the power of rage made physically manifest.
fractured.features.0.description: When you activate your Rage, your countenance distorts and your body swells. Creatures that haven't witnessed your transformation, now or previously, don't recognize you. In addition, while your Rage is active, you gain the following benefits:
fractured.features.0.details.push: When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can push it 10 feet or force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
fractured.features.0.details.sizeAndReac: You count as one size larger when determining the success or failure of a Grapple, and when you make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, your reach is 5 feet greater than normal.
fractured.features.0.details.unarmedStri: You can roll {@dice 1d8} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, and whenever you deal damage with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.
fractured.features.0.name: Face of Rage
fractured.features.1.description: You are proficient in one of the following skills of your choice: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, Persuasion, or Religion. In addition, you gain proficiency with one type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice or you know one language of your choice.
fractured.features.1.name: Mask of Civility
fractured.features.2.description: While your Rage is not active, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage. While your Rage is active, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to every damage type except Force and Psychic.
fractured.features.2.name: Brains and Brawn
fractured.features.3.description: While your Rage is not active, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} or {@action Help|XPHB} action as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. While your Rage is active, your attack rolls with {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
fractured.features.3.name: Cunning and Brutal
fractured.features.4.description: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} instead, and you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum. In addition, if your Rage is active, your Rage ends. If your Rage was not active, immediately activate your Rage (even if you have no remaining uses of your Rage). If any of these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} remain after 1 minute, they vanish. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
fractured.features.4.name: Better Half
fractured.name: Путь Расколотого
fractured.shortDescription: Two Personalities Are Better than One
genie.description:
You have made a pact with one of the rarest kinds of genie, a noble genie. Such entities rule vast fiefs on the Elemental Planes and have great influence over lesser genies and elemental creatures. Noble genies are varied in their motivations, but most are arrogant and wield power that rivals that of lesser deities. They delight in turning the table on mortals, who often bind genies into servitude, and readily enter into pacts that expand their reach.

You choose your patron's kind or determine it randomly, using the Genie Kind table.
genie.features.0.description: The Genie lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The Genie Expanded Spells table shows the genie spells that are added to the warlock spell list for you, along with the spells associated in the table with your patron's kind: dao, djinni, efreeti, or marid.
genie.features.0.name: Expanded Spell List
genie.features.1.description: Your patron gifts you a magical vessel that grants you a measure of the genie's power. The vessel is a Tiny object, and you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells. You decide what the object is, or you can determine what it is randomly by rolling on the Genie's Vessel table.
genie.features.1.details.bottledRespite: {@b Bottled Respite.} As an action, you can magically vanish and enter your vessel, which remains in the space you left. The interior of the vessel is an extradimensional space in the shape of a 20-foot-radius cylinder, 20 feet high, and resembles your vessel. The interior is appointed with cushions and low tables and is a comfortable temperature. While inside, you can hear the area around your vessel as if you were in its space. You can remain inside the vessel up to a number of hours equal to twice your proficiency bonus. You exit the vessel early if you use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to leave, if you die, or if the vessel is destroyed. When you exit the vessel, you appear in the unoccupied space closest to it. Any objects left in the vessel remain there until carried out, and if the vessel is destroyed, every object stored there harmlessly appears in the unoccupied spaces closest to the vessel's former space. Once you enter the vessel, you can't enter again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
genie.features.1.details.geniesWrath: {@b Genie's Wrath.} Once during each of your turns when you hit with an attack roll, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to your proficiency bonus. The type of this damage is determined by your patron: bludgeoning (dao), thunder (djinni), fire (efreeti), or cold (marid).
genie.features.1.details.usage: While you are touching the vessel, you can use it in the following ways:
genie.features.1.details.vesselPropertie:
The vessel's AC equals your spell save DC. Its hit points equal your warlock level plus your proficiency bonus, and it is immune to poison and psychic damage.

If the vessel is destroyed or you lose it, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony can be performed during a short or long rest, and the previous vessel is destroyed if it still exists. The vessel vanishes in a flare of elemental power when you die.
genie.features.1.name: Genie's Vessel
genie.features.2.description: You begin to take on characteristics of your patron's kind. You now have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to a damage type determined by your patron's kind: Bludgeoning (dao), Thunder (djinni), Fire (efreeti), or Cold (marid).
genie.features.2.details.flyingSpeed: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can give yourself a flying speed of 30 feet that lasts for 10 minutes, during which you can hover. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
genie.features.2.name: Elemental Gift
genie.features.3.description:
When you enter your Genie's Vessel via the Bottled Respite feature, you can now choose up to five willing creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you, and the chosen creatures are drawn into the vessel with you.

As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can eject any number of creatures from the vessel, and everyone is ejected if you leave or die or if the vessel is destroyed.

In addition, anyone (including you) who remains within the vessel for at least 10 minutes gains the benefit of finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and anyone can add their proficiency bonus to the number of hit points they regain if they spend any Hit Dice as part of a short rest there.
genie.features.3.name: Sanctuary Vessel
genie.features.4.description:
You entreat your patron to grant you a small wish. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can speak your desire to your Genie's Vessel, requesting the effect of one spell that is level 6 or lower and has a casting time of 1 action. The spell can be from any class's spell list, and you don't need to meet the requirements in that spell, including costly components; the spell simply takes effect as part of this action.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish {@dice 1d4} Long Rests.
genie.features.4.name: Limited Wish
genie.name: Джинн
giant.description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Giant draw strength from the same primal forces as giants. As they rage, these barbarians surge with elemental power and grow in size, taking on forms that evoke the glory of giants.
giant.features.0.description: When you choose this path, you learn to speak, read, and write Giant or one other language of your choice if you already know Giant. Additionally, you learn a cantrip of your choice: either {@spell druidcraft|XPHB} or {@spell thaumaturgy|XPHB}. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.
giant.features.0.name: Giant Power
giant.features.1.description: Your rages pull strength from the primal might of giants, transforming you into a hulking force of destruction. While raging, you gain the following benefits:
giant.features.1.details.crushingThrow: When you make a successful ranged attack with a thrown weapon using Strength, you can add your Rage Damage bonus to the attack's damage roll.
giant.features.1.details.giantStature: Your reach increases by 5 feet, and if you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If there isn't enough room for you to increase your size, your size doesn't change.
giant.features.1.name: Giant's Havoc
giant.features.2.description: Your bond with the elemental might of giants grows, and you learn to infuse weapons with primordial energy. When you enter your rage, you can choose one weapon that you are holding and infuse it with one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, thunder, or lightning. While you wield the infused weapon during your rage, the weapon's damage type changes to the chosen type, it deals an extra {@dice 1d6} damage of the chosen type when it hits, and it gains the thrown property, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. If you throw the weapon, it reappears in your hand the instant after it hits or misses a target. The infused weapon's benefits are suppressed while a creature other than you wields it. While raging and holding the infused weapon, you can use a bonus action to change the infused weapon's current damage type to another one from the damage type options above.
giant.features.2.name: Elemental Cleaver
giant.features.3.description: Your connection to giant strength allows you to hurl both allies and enemies on the battlefield. As a bonus action while raging, you can choose one Medium or smaller creature within your reach and move it to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself. An unwilling creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) to avoid the effect. If, at the end of this movement, the thrown creature isn't on a surface or liquid that can support it, the creature falls, taking damage as normal and landing {@condition prone|XPHB}.
giant.features.3.name: Mighty Impel
giant.features.4.description: The primordial power of your rage intensifies. When you rage, your reach increases by 10 feet, your size can increase to Large or Huge (your choice), and you can use your Mighty Impel to move creatures that are Large or smaller. In addition, the extra damage dealt by your Elemental Cleaver feature increases to {@dice 2d6}.
giant.features.4.name: Demiurgic Colossus
giant.name: Путь Гиганта
giant.shortDescription: Channel the Primal Might of Giants
glamour.description: The College of Glamour traces its origins to the beguiling magic of the Feywild. Bards who study this magic weave threads of beauty and terror into their songs and stories, and the mightiest among them can cloak themselves in otherworldly majesty. Their performances stir up wistful longing for forgotten innocence, evoke unconscious memories of long-held fears, and tug at the emotions of even the most hard-hearted listeners.
glamour.features.0.description: You always have the {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} and {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB} spells prepared. In addition, immediately after you cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a spell slot, you can cause a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition (your choice) for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending one use of your Bardic Inspiration (no action required).
glamour.features.0.name: Beguiling Magic
glamour.features.1.description: You can weave fey magic into a song or a dance to fill others with vigor. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a use of Bardic Inspiration, rolling a Bardic Inspiration die. When you do so, choose a number of other creatures within 60 feet of yourself, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of those creatures gains a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to two times the number rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die, and then each can use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.
glamour.features.1.name: Mantle of Inspiration
glamour.features.2.description:
You can cast {@spell Command|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. You appear unearthly beautiful while concentrating on the spell.

Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of the feature by expending a use of your Bardic Inspiration (no action required).
glamour.features.2.name: Mantle of Majesty
glamour.features.3.description:
As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can assume a magically majestic presence for 1 minute or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. For the duration, whenever any creature tries to attack you for the first time on a turn, the attacker must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it can't attack you on this turn, and it must choose a new target for its attack or the attack is wasted. On a successful save, it can attack you on this turn, but it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any saving throw it makes against your spells on your next turn.

Once you assume this majestic presence, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
glamour.features.3.name: Unbreakable Majesty
glamour.name: Коллегия Гламура
gloom-stalker.description: Gloom Stalkers are at home in the darkest places, wielding shadow magic to combat threats that lurk in the dark. These Rangers are adept at ambushes and striking from the shadows, drawing on the power of the Shadowfell to enhance their abilities.
gloom-stalker.features.0.description: You master the art of the ambush. You gain the following benefits.
gloom-stalker.features.0.details.ambushe: {@b Ambusher's Leap.} At the start of your first turn of each combat, your {@variantrule Walking Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn.
gloom-stalker.features.0.details.dreadfu: {@b Dreadful Strike.} When you attack a creature and hit it with a weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage. You can use this benefit only once per turn, you can use it a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
gloom-stalker.features.0.details.initiat: {@b Initiative Bonus.} When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the roll.
gloom-stalker.features.0.name: Dread Ambusher
gloom-stalker.features.1.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Gloom Stalker Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
gloom-stalker.features.1.name: Gloom Stalker Spells
gloom-stalker.features.2.description: You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet. You are also adept at evading creatures that rely on {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}. While you are entirely in {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition to any creature that relies on {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} to see you in that {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}.
gloom-stalker.features.2.name: Umbral Sight
gloom-stalker.features.3.description: You have honed your ability to resist mind-altering powers. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).
gloom-stalker.features.3.name: Iron Mind
gloom-stalker.features.4.description: The Psychic damage of your Dreadful Strike becomes {@damage 2d8}. In addition, when you use the Dreadful Strike effect of your Dread Ambusher feature, you can cause one of the following additional effects.
gloom-stalker.features.4.details.massFea: {@b Mass Fear.} The target and each creature within 10 feet of it must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.
gloom-stalker.features.4.details.suddenS: {@b Sudden Strike.} You can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and that is within the weapon's range.
gloom-stalker.features.4.name: Stalker's Flurry
gloom-stalker.features.5.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll. Whether the attack hits or misses, you can then teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.
gloom-stalker.features.5.name: Shadowy Dodge
gloom-stalker.name: Мрачный Скиталец
gloom-stalker.shortDescription: Draw on Shadow Magic to Fight Your Foes
glory.description: Paladins who take the Oath of Glory believe they and their companions are destined to achieve glory through deeds of heroism. They train diligently and encourage their companions so they're all ready when destiny calls. These Paladins share the following tenets: actions over words, challenges are but tests, and one must hone the body for it is a vessel for the soul.
glory.features.0.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Glory Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
glory.features.0.name: Oath of Glory Spells
glory.features.1.description: Immediately after you cast {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity and distribute {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} to creatures of your choice within 30 feet of yourself, which can include you. The total number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equals {@dice 2d8} plus your Paladin level, divided among the chosen creatures however you like.
glory.features.1.name: Inspiring Smite
glory.features.2.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to augment your athleticism. For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) and Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks, and the distance of your {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} and {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} increases by 10 feet (this extra distance costs movement as normal).
glory.features.2.name: Peerless Athlete
glory.features.3.description: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. In addition, whenever an ally enters your Aura of Protection for the first time on a turn or starts their turn there, the ally's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of their next turn.
glory.features.3.name: Aura of Alacrity
glory.features.4.description: You can turn defense into a sudden strike. When you or another creature you can see within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add your Charisma modifier to the target's AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss. If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker as part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, provided the attacker is within your weapon's range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
glory.features.4.name: Glorious Defense
glory.features.5.description: You can empower yourself with the legends—whether true or exaggerated—of your great deeds. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Charismatic.} You are blessed with an otherworldly presence and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all Charisma checks. {@b Saving Throw Reroll.} If you fail a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reroll it. You must use the new roll. {@b Unerring Strike.} Once on each of your turns when you make an attack roll with a weapon and miss, you can cause that attack to hit instead.
glory.features.5.name: Living Legend
glory.name: Клятва Славы
glory.shortDescription: Strive for the Heights of Heroism
grave.description: Gods of the grave watch over the line between life and death. To these deities, death and the afterlife are a foundational part of the multiverse. To desecrate the peace of the dead is an abomination. Deities of the grave include {@deity Kelemvor}, {@deity Wee Jas}, the ancestral spirits of the Undying Court, {@deity Hades}, {@deity Anubis}, and {@deity Osiris}. Followers of these deities seek to put wandering spirits to rest, destroy the undead, and ease the suffering of the dying. Their magic also allows them to stave off death for a time, particularly for a person who still has some great work to accomplish in the world. This is a delay of death, not a denial of it, for death will eventually get its due.
grave.features.0.description: At each indicated cleric level, add the listed spells to your spells prepared.
grave.features.0.name: Grave Domain Spells
grave.features.1.description: At 1st level, you gain the ability to manipulate the line between life and death. When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore hit points with a spell to a creature at 0 hit points, you instead use the highest number possible for each die. In addition, you learn the {@spell spare the dying} cantrip, which doesn't count against the number of cleric cantrips you know. For you, it has a range of 30 feet, and you can cast it as a bonus action.
grave.features.1.name: Circle of Mortality
grave.features.2.description: At 1st level, you gain the ability to occasionally sense the presence of the undead, whose existence is an insult to the natural cycle of life. As an action, you can open your awareness to magically detect undead. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any undead within 60 feet of you that isn't behind {@variantrule total cover|XPHB} and that isn't protected from divination magic. This sense doesn't tell you anything about a creature's capabilities or identity. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
grave.features.2.name: Eyes of the Grave
grave.features.3.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to mark another creature's life force for termination. As an action, you choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you, cursing it until the end of your next turn. The next time you or an ally of yours hits the cursed creature with an attack, the creature has vulnerability to all of that attack's damage, and then the curse ends.
grave.features.3.name: Channel Divinity: Path to the Grave
grave.features.4.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to impede death's progress. As a reaction when you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you suffers a critical hit, you can turn that hit into a normal hit. Any effects triggered by a critical hit are canceled. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
grave.features.4.name: Sentinel at Death's Door
grave.features.5.description: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.
grave.features.5.name: Действенное заклинательство
grave.features.6.description: Starting at 17th level, you can seize a trace of vitality from a parting soul and use it to heal the living. When an enemy you can see dies within 60 feet of you, you or one creature of your choice that is within 60 feet of you regains hit points equal to the enemy's number of Hit Dice. You can use this feature only if you aren't {@condition incapacitated}. Once you use it, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.
grave.features.6.name: Keeper of Souls
grave.name: Домен Могилы
grave.shortDescription: Guardian of the Line Between Life and Death
graviturgy.description: Understanding and mastering the forces that draw bodies of matter together or drive them apart, the students of the Graviturgy arcane tradition learn to further bend and manipulate the violent energy of gravity to their benefit, and the terrible detriment of their enemies.
graviturgy.features.0.description:
{@i 2nd-level Graviturgy Magic feature}

As an {@action Action|XPHB}, you can magically alter the weight of one object or creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The object or creature must be Large or smaller. The target's weight is halved or doubled for up to 1 minute or until your {@status Concentration|XPHB} ends (as if you were concentrating on a spell).

While the weight of a creature is halved by this effect, the creature's speed increases by 10 feet, it can jump twice as far as normal, and it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. While the weight of a creature is doubled by this effect, the creature's speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.

Upon reaching 10th level in this class, you can target an object or a creature that is Huge or smaller.
graviturgy.features.0.name: Adjust Density
graviturgy.features.1.description:
{@i 6th-level Graviturgy Magic feature}

You've learned how to manipulate gravity around a living being: whenever you cast a spell on a creature, you can move the target 5 feet to an unoccupied space of your choice if the target is willing to move, the spell hits it with an attack, or it fails a saving throw against the spell.
graviturgy.features.1.name: Gravity Well
graviturgy.features.2.description:
{@i 10th-level Graviturgy Magic feature}

When another creature that you can see within 60 feet of you hits with a weapon attack, you can use your {@action Reaction|XPHB} to increase the attack's velocity, causing the attack's target to take an extra {@dice 1d10} damage of the weapon's type.

Alternatively, if a creature within 60 feet of you takes damage from a fall, you can use your {@action Reaction|XPHB} to increase the fall's damage by {@dice 2d10}.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
graviturgy.features.2.name: Violent Attraction
graviturgy.features.3.name: Event Horizon
graviturgy.name: Гравитургия
great-old-one.description: When you choose this subclass, you might bind yourself to an unspeakable being from the Far Realm or an elder god—a being such as Tharizdun, the Chained God; Zargon, the Returner; Hadar, the Dark Hunger; or Great Cthulhu. Or you might invoke several entities without yoking yourself to one. The motives of these beings are incomprehensible, and the Great Old One might be indifferent to your existence. But the secrets you've learned nevertheless allow you to draw strange magic from it.
great-old-one.features.0.description: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Great Old One Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
great-old-one.features.0.name: Great Old One Spells
great-old-one.features.1.description:
You can form a telepathic connection between your mind and the mind of another. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You and the chosen creature can communicate telepathically with each other while the two of you are within a number of miles of each other equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 mile). To understand each other, you each must mentally use a language the other knows.

The telepathic connection lasts for a number of minutes equal to your Warlock level. It ends early if you use this feature to connect with a different creature.
great-old-one.features.1.name: Awakened Mind
great-old-one.features.2.description: When you cast a Warlock spell that deals damage, you can change its damage type to Psychic. In addition, when you cast a Warlock spell that is an Enchantment or Illusion, you can do so without Verbal or Somatic components.
great-old-one.features.2.name: Psychic Spells
great-old-one.features.3.description:
When you form a telepathic bond with a creature using your Awakened Mind, you can force that creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature for the duration of the bond.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a Pact Magic spell slot (no action required) to restore your use of it.
great-old-one.features.3.name: Clairvoyant Combatant
great-old-one.features.4.description: Your alien patron grants you a powerful curse. You always have the {@spell Hex|XPHB} spell prepared. When you cast {@spell Hex|XPHB} and choose an ability, the target also has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws of the chosen ability for the duration of the spell.
great-old-one.features.4.name: Eldritch Hex
great-old-one.features.5.description: Your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means unless you allow it. You also have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage, and whenever a creature deals Psychic damage to you, that creature takes the same amount of damage that you take.
great-old-one.features.5.name: Thought Shield
great-old-one.features.6.description:
When you cast {@spell Summon Aberration|XPHB}, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB}. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting, and when summoned, the Aberration has a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Warlock level plus your Charisma modifier.

In addition, the first time each turn the Aberration hits a creature under the effect of your {@spell Hex|XPHB}, the Aberration deals extra Psychic damage to the target equal to the bonus damage of that spell.
great-old-one.features.6.name: Create Thrall
great-old-one.name: Великий Древний
great-old-one.shortDescription: Unearth Forbidden Lore of Ineffable Beings
green-reaper.description: A Green Reaper is an expert in the use of natural venoms and toxic substances, wielding the lethal power of nature's poisons to slay their foes. These Rangers harvest deadly compounds from plants and creatures, turning them into potent weapons.
green-reaper.features.0.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can apply a natural venom to one weapon or piece of ammunition that you're holding. The next time you hit a creature with that weapon or ammunition, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d4} Poison damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. This feature improves when you reach Ranger level 11: the Poison damage increases to {@damage 2d4}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
green-reaper.features.0.name: Envenomed Attack
green-reaper.features.1.description: You gain proficiency with the {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency with another type of Artisan's Tools of your choice. Whenever you make an ability check using the {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} is doubled for the check. In addition, you've honed the ability to harvest and distill toxic components from plants and creatures. When you deal Poison damage to a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend a spell slot to infuse the attack with a concentrated toxin. The target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Poison damage per level of the spell slot expended and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. A {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the condition on a success. When the creature first fails this save, it also suffers a Toxin Effect determined by the level of the spell slot you expended, as shown in the Toxin Effects table.
green-reaper.features.1.name: Toxic Tradecraft
green-reaper.features.2.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Green Reaper Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
green-reaper.features.2.name: Green Reaper Spells
green-reaper.features.3.description: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. In addition, you can cast the {@spell Protection from Poison|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
green-reaper.features.3.name: Poison Control
green-reaper.features.4.description: Whenever you would deal Poison damage with a weapon attack, you can change that damage to be either Acid or Necrotic instead.
green-reaper.features.4.name: Variegated Vexations
green-reaper.features.5.description: You have learned to quickly inure yourself against harm. Immediately before you take damage from a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage you take. If any of these Temporary Hit Points remain at the end of your next turn, they vanish.
green-reaper.features.5.name: Pain Tolerance
green-reaper.name: Зелёный Жнец
haunted.description: Unlike other Sorcerers, the circumstances of your birth were mundane and you have no arcane inheritance passed down from strange ancestors. Instead, you gained your sorcerous powers after you survived an experience that left you near death. Since that experience, you've had a preternatural sense for danger and a ghostly companion that either can't, or won't, leave you alone. Some Haunted, as Sorcerers who share your origin are called, develop cordial relationships with their phantom, while others find their spectral companion to be a relentless nuisance.
haunted.features.0.description: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Haunted Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
haunted.features.0.name: Haunted Spells
haunted.features.1.description: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can add your Charisma modifier to the roll.
haunted.features.1.name: Sixth Sense
haunted.features.2.description: You learn the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell and can cast it as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action without expending a spell slot. The familiar takes the form of a {@creature Specter}, though it is an Undead instead of a Celestial, Fey, or Fiend. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can command your phantom companion to gain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until it attacks or you cast a spell through it. While {@condition Invisible|XPHB}, it leaves no physical evidence of its passage and can be tracked only by magic. Any equipment or objects it is holding remains visible. Additionally, when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can forgo one of your own attacks to allow your familiar to make its Life Drain attack with its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
haunted.features.2.name: Phantom Companion
haunted.features.3.description:
Your bond with your phantom companion empowers it. You gain the following benefits:

- Your familiar's Hit Point maximum increases by four times your Sorcerer level.
- You can cast spells as if you were in the familiar's space.
- When you use your action to cast a spell, you can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to command your phantom companion to use its Life Drain attack with its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
haunted.features.3.name: Strength of Spirit
haunted.features.4.description: You have Resistance to Necrotic damage, and when you cast a Sorcerer spell that deals damage, it can deal your choice of Necrotic damage or its normal damage type.
haunted.features.4.name: Deathly Pallor
haunted.features.5.description:
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can direct your phantom companion to possess a creature of your choice within 5 feet of your phantom. The target makes a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, your phantom companion enters the target's body for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target resists the efforts to possess it, and your familiar can't possess it again for 24 hours.

Once the phantom companion possesses a creature's body, it controls that creature. The familiar's Hit Points, Hit Point Dice, Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Speed, and senses are replaced by the creature's. The phantom companion otherwise keeps its game statistics.

While the target is possessed, you have a telepathic link with your phantom companion as long as the two of you are within 100 feet. You can use this telepathic link to issue commands to your phantom companion (no action required) unless you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. Your phantom companion does its best to obey on its turn. If it completes an order and doesn't receive further direction from you, the phantom companion acts and moves as it likes, focusing on protecting itself.

You can command the target to take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} but must take your own {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to do so.

Whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the save, ending the possession on itself on a success, and your phantom companion reappears in the closest unoccupied space.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
haunted.features.5.name: Phantom Possession
haunted.features.6.description:
You can transmute your physical form into a spectral one when near death. When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can drop to 1 Hit Point instead and gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Hit Point maximum. At the start of each of your turns, you lose 10 Temporary Hit Points and creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you take 10 Necrotic damage. While you have Temporary Hit Points granted by this feature, you have Resistance to all damage, a Fly Speed of 30 feet, can Hover, and you can move through occupied spaces as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. If you end your turn in such a space, you are shunted to the last unoccupied space you were in.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
haunted.features.6.name: Become Death
haunted.name: Преследуемый
hexblade.description:
You have made your pact with a mysterious entity from the Shadowfell—a force that manifests in sentient magic weapons carved from the stuff of shadow. The mighty sword Blackrazor is the most notable of these weapons, which have been spread across the multiverse over the ages. The shadowy force behind these weapons can offer power to warlocks who form pacts with it. Many Hexblade warlocks create weapons that emulate those formed in the Shadowfell. Others forgo such arms, content to weave the dark magic of that plane into their spellcasting.

Because the Raven Queen is known to have forged the first of these weapons, many sages speculate that she and the force are one and that the weapons, along with Hexblade warlocks, are tools she uses to manipulate events on the Material Plane to her inscrutable ends.
hexblade.features.0.description: The Hexblade lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.
hexblade.features.0.name: Expanded Spell List
hexblade.features.1.description: You gain the ability to place a baleful curse on someone. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target is cursed for 1 minute. The curse ends early if the target dies, you die, or you are {@status Incapacitated|XPHB}. Until the curse ends, you gain the following benefits:
hexblade.features.1.details.criticalHits: Any attack roll you make against the cursed target is a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
hexblade.features.1.details.damageBonus: You gain a bonus to damage rolls against the cursed target. The bonus equals your proficiency bonus.
hexblade.features.1.details.lifeSteal: If the cursed target dies, you regain hit points equal to your warlock level + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 hit point).
hexblade.features.1.details.uses: You can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
hexblade.features.1.name: Hexblade's Curse
hexblade.features.2.description:
You acquire the training necessary to effectively arm yourself for battle. You gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.

The influence of your patron also allows you to mystically channel your will through a particular weapon. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can touch one weapon that you are proficient with and that lacks the two-handed property. When you attack with that weapon, you can use your Charisma modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity, for the attack and damage rolls. This benefit lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If you later gain the Pact of the Blade feature, this benefit extends to every pact weapon you conjure with that feature, no matter the weapon's type.
hexblade.features.2.name: Hex Warrior
hexblade.features.3.description:
Starting at 6th level, you can curse the soul of a person you slay, temporarily binding it to your service. When you slay a humanoid, you can cause its spirit to rise from its corpse as a {@creature specter}, the statistics for which are in the Monster Manual. When the specter appears, it gains temporary hit points equal to half your warlock level. Roll initiative for the specter, which has its own turns. It obeys your verbal commands, and it gains a special bonus to its attack rolls equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of +0).

The specter remains in your service until the end of your next long rest, at which point it vanishes to the afterlife.

Once you bind a specter with this feature, you can't use the feature again until you finish a long rest.
hexblade.features.3.name: Accursed Specter
hexblade.features.4.description: Your hex grows more powerful. If the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse hits you with an attack roll, you can use your {@action Reaction|XPHB} to roll a {@dice d6}. On a 4 or higher, the attack instead misses you, regardless of its roll.
hexblade.features.4.name: Armor of Hexes
hexblade.features.5.description: You can spread your Hexblade's Curse from a slain creature to another creature. When the creature cursed by your Hexblade's Curse dies, you can apply the curse to a different creature you can see within 30 feet of you, provided you aren't {@status Incapacitated|XPHB}. When you apply the curse in this way, you don't regain hit points from the death of the previously cursed creature.
hexblade.features.5.name: Master of Hexes
hexblade.name: Ведьмин Клинок
high-roller.description: Удача — вещь капризная, если только вы не Азартный Игрок. Эти Стрелки — мастера карточных и кубковых игр, сочетающие любовь к риску с талантом стрельбы. Азартные Игроки испытывают свою удачу, пока она не закончится, а затем испытывают её ещё сильнее. Зачем довольствоваться выигрышем, когда можно поставить всё и сорвать куш?
high-roller.features.0.description: Вы получаете владение всеми {@item Gaming Sets|XPHB|Игровыми наборами} и одним из следующих навыков на ваш выбор: {@skill Deception|XPHB|Обман}, {@skill Insight|XPHB|Проницательность} или {@skill Perception|XPHB|Восприятие}.
high-roller.features.0.name: Покерное лицо
high-roller.features.1.description: [Манёвр] Когда вы совершаете бросок урона Дальнобойным оружием, вы можете {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием} потратить одну Кость риска и объявить бросок скрытым. Бросьте урон тайно и объявите любой желаемый итог. ГМ может вызвать ваш блеф — в таком случае вы раскрываете кости урона, которые бросили. Это имеет разные последствия в зависимости от того, лгали ли вы.
high-roller.features.1.details.gmDoesntC: Используйте объявленный вами итог, даже если вы выбросили другой результат.
high-roller.features.1.name: Кости лжеца
high-roller.features.2.description: Один раз за ход, когда вы совершаете бросок атаки против врага и бросок не имеет {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB|Помехи}, вы можете решить совершить бросок с {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB|Помехой}. Если вы это делаете, вы восстанавливаете одну потраченную Кость риска.
high-roller.features.2.name: Рискованное дело
high-roller.features.3.description: Вы можете использовать манёвры Дух авантюриста и На волоске, не тратя Кость риска. Если вы это делаете, бросьте {@dice d6} вместо Кости риска.
high-roller.features.3.name: Рискующий
high-roller.features.4.description: Когда вы наносите {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Критическое попадание} Дальнобойным оружием, вы можете рискнуть ради большего результата. Бросьте {@dice d20}. Если выпадает 10 или больше, бросьте все кости урона атаки четыре раза и сложите их вместо обычных двух раз для {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Критического попадания}. Если на {@dice d20} выпадает 9 или меньше, {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Критическое попадание} становится обычным попаданием.
high-roller.features.4.name: Двойная ставка или ничего
high-roller.name: Азартный Игрок
highway-rider.description: Stalking the backroads, the Highway Rider strikes fear into the heart of every traveler and penny-pinching merchant. They run down their prize astride a swift and loyal steed—and then make a quick getaway.
highway-rider.features.0.description: You gain proficiency with Blackpowder Pistols. In addition, when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll, you can immediately take a Reaction for one of the following options: make one attack with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}; move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}; a controlled mount moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}; or take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} or {@action Utilize|XPHB} action.
highway-rider.features.0.name: Hair Trigger
highway-rider.features.1.description: You always have the {@spell Find Steed|XPHB} spell prepared. With this feature, you can cast it without a spell slot or components, and your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
highway-rider.features.1.name: Trusty Mount
highway-rider.features.2.description: You don't need {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll to use your Sneak Attack if you or a controlled mount you ride moves at least 20 feet, and you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.
highway-rider.features.2.name: Ride Them Down
highway-rider.name: Дорожный Всадник
highway-rider.shortDescription: A Steed Makes the Best Partner in Crime
horizon-walker.description: Horizon Walkers guard the world against threats that originate from other planes or that seek to ravage the mortal realm with otherworldly magic. They seek out planar portals and keep watch over them, venturing to the Inner Planes and the Outer Planes as needed to pursue their foes. These rangers are also friends to any forces in the multiverse—especially benevolent dragons, fey, and elementals—that work to preserve life and the order of the planes.
horizon-walker.features.0.description: Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Horizon Walker Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.
horizon-walker.features.0.name: Horizon Walker Magic
horizon-walker.features.1.description: At 3rd level, you gain the ability to magically sense the presence of a planar portal. As an action, you detect the distance and direction to the closest planar portal within 1 mile of you. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. See the "Planar Travel" section in chapter 2 of the Dungeon Master's Guide for examples of planar portals.
horizon-walker.features.1.name: Detect Portal
horizon-walker.features.2.description: At 3rd level, you learn to draw on the energy of the multiverse to augment your attacks. As a bonus action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The next time you hit that creature on this turn with a weapon attack, all damage dealt by the attack becomes force damage, and the creature takes an extra 1d8 force damage from the attack. When you reach 11th level in this class, the extra damage increases to 2d8.
horizon-walker.features.2.name: Planar Warrior
horizon-walker.features.3.description: At 7th level, you learn to step through the Ethereal Plane. As a bonus action, you can cast the {@spell Etherealness} spell with this feature, without expending a spell slot, but the spell ends at the end of the current turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
horizon-walker.features.3.name: Ethereal Step
horizon-walker.features.4.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to pass between the planes in the blink of an eye. When you take the {@action Attack} action, you can teleport up to 10 feet before each attack to an unoccupied space you can see. If you attack at least two different creatures with the action, you can make one additional attack with it against a third creature.
horizon-walker.features.4.name: Distant Strike
horizon-walker.features.5.description: At 15th level, your ability to move between planes enables you to slip through the planar boundaries to lessen the harm done to you during battle. When you take damage from an attack, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to all of that attack's damage on this turn.
horizon-walker.features.5.name: Spectral Defense
horizon-walker.name: Странник Горизонта
horizon-walker.shortDescription: Guard the World Against Planar Threats
hunter.description: You stalk prey in the wilds and elsewhere, using your abilities as a Hunter to protect nature and people everywhere from forces that would destroy them.
hunter.features.0.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace the chosen option with the other one.
hunter.features.0.details.colossusSlayer: {@b Colossus Slayer.} Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d8} damage to the target if it's missing any of its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.
hunter.features.0.details.hordeBreaker: {@b Horde Breaker.} Once on each of your turns when you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.
hunter.features.0.name: Hunter's Prey
hunter.features.1.description: You can call on the forces of nature to reveal certain strengths and weaknesses of your prey. While a creature is marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if the creature has any, you know what they are.
hunter.features.1.name: Hunter's Lore
hunter.features.2.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace the chosen option with the other one.
hunter.features.2.details.escapeTheHorde: {@b Escape the Horde.} {@action Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.
hunter.features.2.details.multiattackDef: {@b Multiattack Defense.} When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all other attack rolls against you this turn.
hunter.features.2.name: Defensive Tactics
hunter.features.3.description: Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you can also deal that spell's extra damage to a different creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature.
hunter.features.3.name: Superior Hunter's Prey
hunter.features.4.description: When you take damage, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to give yourself {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage and any other damage of the same type until the end of the current turn.
hunter.features.4.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
hunter.name: Охотник
hunter.shortDescription: Protect Nature and People from Destruction
illusionist.description: You specialize in magic that dazzles the senses and tricks the mind, and the illusions you craft make the impossible seem real.
illusionist.features.0.description:
Choose two Wizard spells from the Illusion school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Illusion school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
illusionist.features.0.name: Illusion Savant
illusionist.features.1.description:
You can cast Illusion spells without providing Verbal components, and if an Illusion spell you cast has a range of 10+ feet, the range increases by 60 feet.

You also know the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} cantrip. If you already know it, you learn a different Wizard cantrip of your choice. The cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known. You can create both a sound and an image with a single casting of {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB}, and you can cast it as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
illusionist.features.1.name: Improved Illusions
illusionist.features.2.description: You always have the {@spell Summon Beast|XPHB} and {@spell Summon Fey|XPHB} spells prepared. Whenever you cast either spell, you can change its school to Illusion, which causes the summoned creature to appear spectral. You can cast the Illusion version of each spell without expending a spell slot, but casting it without a slot halves the creature's Hit Points. Once you cast either spell without a spell slot, you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast the spell in that way again.
illusionist.features.2.name: Phantasmal Creatures
illusionist.features.3.description:
When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to interpose an illusory duplicate of yourself between the attacker and yourself. The attack automatically misses you, then the illusion dissipates.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 2+ spell slot (no action required).
illusionist.features.3.name: Illusory Self
illusionist.features.4.description:
You have learned to weave shadow magic into your illusions to give them a semi-reality. When you cast an Illusion spell with a spell slot, you can choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of the illusion and make that object real. You can do this on your turn as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} while the spell is ongoing. The object remains real for 1 minute, during which it can't deal damage or give any conditions.

For example, you can create an illusion of a bridge over a chasm and then make it real and cross it.
illusionist.features.4.name: Illusory Reality
illusionist.name: Иллюзионист
inquisition.description: The Inquisition Domain reflects the order of the multiverse and the rejection of tainted magic—so far as certain celestial powers see fit. Only the divine casters are pure and fit for use. Since arcane magic is strong enough to challenge the gods, divine beings of this domain, such as the Arch Seraph Empyreus, demand magic-using mortals be kept in check. Most zealots root out all arcanists, while some strike fragile truces when complete removal isn't feasible. Cleanse the Heretics.
inquisition.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Inquisition Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
inquisition.features.0.name: Inquisition Domain Spells
inquisition.features.1.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage to the target. If the creature is {@status Concentrating|XPHB} on a spell, you deal an extra {@damage 2d6} Force damage instead. At Cleric level 14, the extra Force damage increases to {@damage 2d6}, or {@damage 3d6} if the creature is {@status Concentrating|XPHB} on a spell. If a creature fails its saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} as a result of taking damage from this feature, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the extra Force damage dealt. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
inquisition.features.1.name: Witch Hunter's Strike
inquisition.features.2.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to bestow a temporary resilience against arcane harm for 10 minutes. Choose a creature you can see (including yourself) within 30 feet of yourself. The chosen creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your Cleric level. While a creature has {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} granted by your Spell Shield, the creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells, and it has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the damage of spells. If any of these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} remain when Spell Shield ends, they vanish.
inquisition.features.2.name: Spell Shield
inquisition.features.3.description: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} in response to a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cast a spell, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 1d6} Force damage, plus another {@damage 1d6} per level of the spell slot the creature expended. Cantrips are considered level 1 spells for this ability. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
inquisition.features.3.name: Rebuke Invoker
inquisition.features.4.description: Spell Shield can target a number of creatures up to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one creature).
inquisition.features.4.name: Supernal Safeguard
inquisition.name: Домен Инквизиции
inquisitive.description: As an Inquisitive, you excel at rooting out secrets and unraveling mysteries. You rely on your sharp eye for detail and your finely honed ability to read the words and deeds of other creatures to determine their true intent. You excel at defeating creatures that hide among and prey upon ordinary folk, and your mastery of lore and your keen deductions make you well equipped to expose and end hidden evils.
inquisitive.features.0.description: Whenever you make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check to determine whether a creature is lying to you, treat a roll of 7 or lower on the {@dice d20} as an 8.
inquisitive.features.0.name: Ear for Deceit
inquisitive.features.1.description: You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check to spot a hidden creature or object or to make an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to uncover or decipher clues.
inquisitive.features.1.name: Eye for Detail
inquisitive.features.2.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check against a creature you can see that isn't {@condition Incapacitated}, contested by the target's Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check. If you succeed, you can use your Sneak Attack against that target even if you don't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll, but not if you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on it. This benefit lasts for 1 minute or until you successfully use this feature against a different target.
inquisitive.features.2.name: Insightful Fighting
inquisitive.name: Сыщик
inquisitive.shortDescription: Root Out Secrets and Unravel Mysteries
kensei.description: Monks of the Kensei train relentlessly with their weapons, to the point where the weapon becomes an extension of the body. Founded on a mastery of sword fighting, the tradition has expanded to include many different weapons. A kensei sees a weapon in much the same way a calligrapher or painter regards a pen or brush. Whatever the weapon, the kensei views it as a tool used to express the beauty and precision of the martial arts. That such mastery makes a kensei a peerless warrior is but a side effect of intense devotion, practice, and study.
kensei.features.0.description: When you choose this tradition at 3rd level, your special martial arts training leads you to master the use of certain weapons. You gain the following benefits.
kensei.features.0.details.agileParry: {@b Agile Parry.} If you make an unarmed strike as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and are holding a kensei weapon, you can use it to defend yourself if it is a melee weapon. You gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, while the weapon is in your hand and you aren't {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}.
kensei.features.0.details.kenseiShot: {@b Kensei's Shot.} You can use a bonus action on your turn to make your ranged attacks with a kensei weapon more deadly. When you do so, any target you hit with a ranged attack using a kensei weapon takes an extra {@damage 1d4} damage of the weapon's type. You retain this benefit until the end of the current turn.
kensei.features.0.details.kenseiWeapons: {@b Kensei Weapons.} Choose two types of weapons to be your kensei weapons: one melee weapon and one ranged weapon. Each of these weapons can be any {@filter simple or martial weapon that lacks the heavy and special properties|XPHB}. The longbow is also a valid choice. You gain proficiency with these weapons if you don't already have it. Weapons of the chosen types are monk weapons for you. Many of this tradition's features work only with your kensei weapons. When you reach 6th, 11th, and 17th level in this class, you can choose another type of weapon—either melee or ranged—to be a kensei weapon for you, following the criteria above.
kensei.features.0.details.wayOfTheBrush: {@b Way of the Brush.} You gain proficiency with your choice of {@item calligrapher's supplies|XPHB} or {@item painter's supplies|XPHB}.
kensei.features.0.name: Path of the Kensei
kensei.features.1.description: At 6th level, you extend your ki into your kensei weapons, granting you the following benefits.
kensei.features.1.details.deftStrike: {@b Deft Strike.} When you hit a target with a kensei weapon, you can spend 1 ki point to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target equal to your Martial Arts die. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.
kensei.features.1.details.magicKenseiWea: {@b Magic Kensei Weapons.} Your attacks with your kensei weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
kensei.features.1.name: One with the Blade
kensei.features.2.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to augment your weapons further with your ki. As a bonus action, you can expend up to 3 ki points to grant one kensei weapon you touch a bonus to attack and damage rolls when you attack with it. The bonus equals the number of ki points you spent. This bonus lasts for 1 minute or until you use this feature again. This feature has no effect on a magic weapon that already has a bonus to attack and damage rolls.
kensei.features.2.name: Sharpen the Blade
kensei.features.3.description: At 17th level, your mastery of weapons grants you extraordinary accuracy. If you miss with an attack roll using a monk weapon on your turn, you can reroll it. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.
kensei.features.3.name: Unerring Accuracy
kensei.name: Путь Кенсей
kensei.shortDescription: Master Weapons as Extensions of Self
knowledge.description: The Knowledge Domain values learning and understanding above all. Clerics who tap into this domain study esoteric lore, collect old tomes, delve into secret places, and examine the processes of the mind. To them, knowledge is more valuable than material wealth, and learning is an act of worship. Libraries, universities, and other educational institutions also draw on the power of the Knowledge Domain. Gods of knowledge vary from masters of arcane magic to patrons of craft and invention. In Faerûn, Clerics of the Knowledge Domain worship deities of learning and ingenuity such as {@deity Oghma} and {@deity Gond}, among many others. Unearth Secrets and Master the Mind.
knowledge.features.0.description: You gain proficiency with one type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice and in two of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Arcana}, {@skill History}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill Religion}. You have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in those two skills.
knowledge.features.0.name: Blessings of Knowledge
knowledge.features.1.description: When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Knowledge Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
knowledge.features.1.name: Knowledge Domain Spells
knowledge.features.2.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to manifest your magical knowledge. Choose one spell from the Divination school on the Knowledge Domain Spells table that you have prepared. As part of that action, you cast that spell without expending a spell slot or needing Material components.
knowledge.features.2.name: Mind Magic
knowledge.features.3.description: You gain {@sense telepathy|XPHB} out to 60 feet. When you use this telepathy, you can simultaneously contact a number of creatures equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one). Additionally, you gain proficiency in Intelligence saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain saving throw proficiency with one ability in which you lack it.
knowledge.features.3.name: Unfettered Mind
knowledge.features.4.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you magically expand your mind to the future. For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Tests|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of this feature by expending a level 6+ spell slot (no action required).
knowledge.features.4.name: Divine Foreknowledge
knowledge.name: Домен Знаний
knowledge.shortDescription: Unearth Secrets and Master the Mind
land.description: The Circle of the Land comprises mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites. These Druids meet within sacred circles of trees or standing stones to whisper primal secrets in Druidic. The circle's wisest members preside as the chief priests of their communities.
land.features.0.description: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, choose one type of land: arid, polar, temperate, or tropical. Consult the table below that corresponds to the chosen type; you have the spells listed for your Druid level and lower prepared.
land.features.0.name: Circle of the Land Spells
land.features.1.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape and choose a point within 60 feet of yourself. Vitality-giving flowers and life-draining thorns appear for a moment in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} centered on that point. Each creature of your choice in the {@variantrule Sphere|XPHB} must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. One creature of your choice in that area regains {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The damage and healing increase by {@dice 1d6} when you reach Druid levels 10 ({@dice 3d6}) and 14 ({@dice 4d6}).
land.features.1.name: Land's Aid
land.features.2.description: You can cast one of the level 1+ spells that you have prepared from your Circle Spells feature without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you do so again.
