You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b One with Plague.} You are Immune to Poison damage and the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition, and you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. {@b Bolstered by Rot.} Your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum can't be reduced. {@b Entropic Radiance.} Whenever an enemy starts its turn within your Aura of Protection, it takes Necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.
pestilence.features.5.name: Plaguebringer
pestilence.name: Клятва Чумы
phantom.description: Many Rogues walk a fine line between life and death, risking their own lives and taking the lives of others. While adventuring on that line, some Rogues discover a mystical connection to death itself. These Rogues take knowledge from the dead and become combatants imbued with the power of death.
phantom.features.0.description: Echoes of those who have died cling to you. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can choose one skill or tool proficiency that you lack and gain it, as a ghostly presence shares its knowledge with you. You lose this proficiency when you use this feature to choose a different proficiency that you lack.
phantom.features.0.name: Whispers of the Dead
phantom.features.1.description: As you nudge someone closer to the grave, you can channel the power of death to harm someone else as well. Immediately after you deal your Sneak Attack damage to a creature on your turn, you can target a second creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature. Roll half the number of Sneak Attack dice for your level (round up), and the second creature takes necrotic damage equal to the roll's total, as wails of the dead sound for a moment. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
phantom.features.1.name: Wails from the Grave
phantom.name: Фантом
plague-doctor.description: Blending spellcasting with science, you distill your magic into concoctions that harm or heal. Plague Doctors often wear grotesque masks protecting them from their toxic ingredients. Many fearfully regard the mask as a sign of pestilence, making Plague Doctors a source of both hope and trepidation.
plague-doctor.features.0.description:
You gain proficiency in the {@skill Medicine} skill and proficiency with the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} and {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}.

Additionally, you have learned to create magical potions.
plague-doctor.features.0.details.consumi: {@b Consuming.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, a creature can drink the potion or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of it. When a creature consumes the potion, it becomes the target of the spell as if you had cast it. If the spell requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, the creature that consumes the potion Concentrates on it. Unconsumed potions last until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} or until you use this feature again.
plague-doctor.features.0.details.craftin: {@b Crafting.} With 10 minutes of work or when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can prepare magical potions if you have an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} or {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}. When you do so, you must expend a level 1+ spell slot for each potion you create and choose a spell from your spellbook that targets only one creature. The chosen spell must be of an equal or lower level than the expended spell slot.
plague-doctor.features.0.name: Potion Craft
plague-doctor.features.1.description: When you craft a potion, you can choose to expend a spell slot without choosing a spell to craft a dose of Good Medicine. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can drink the dose or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of yourself. When Good Medicine is consumed, roll a number of {@dice d8}s equal to the level of the spell slot expended, and the target regains Hit Points equal to the roll's total. If you expended a level 3+ spell slot on this feature, Good Medicine also removes the {@status Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
plague-doctor.features.1.name: Good Medicine
plague-doctor.features.2.description:
When you craft a potion, you can choose to expend a spell slot without choosing a spell to craft a dose of Bad Medicine. When you create a dose, choose one effect per level of the spell slot expended.

• The creature has the {@status Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
• The creature's Speed is halved.
• The creature takes an extra {@dice 1d4} Necrotic damage the first time it takes damage each turn.
• The creature takes {@dice 1d6} Poison damage each time it takes an action, {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, or {@action Reaction|XPHB}.
• The creature takes Acid damage equal to the level of the spell slot expended at the start of each of its turns.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can hurl a dose of Bad Medicine at a point you can see within 30 feet. Creatures within 10 feet of that point must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target suffers one of the chosen effects for 1 minute. On each of its turns, the target can take an action and repeat the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.
plague-doctor.features.2.name: Bad Medicine
plague-doctor.features.3.description: Being persistently exposed to the deadliest ailments known has given you some small measure of resistance. After you take Necrotic or Poison damage, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the damage taken. In addition, you are Immune to the {@status Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
plague-doctor.features.3.name: Breathe It In
plague-doctor.features.4.description: When Good Medicine restores Hit Points to a creature, that creature regains {@dice 2d8} additional Hit Points. When Bad Medicine deals Acid damage to a creature, that creature takes {@dice 2d8} extra Acid damage. Additionally, target creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on their saving throw.
plague-doctor.features.4.name: Medicinal Master
plague-doctor.name: Чумной Доктор
plague-doctor.shortDescription: Plague Your Enemies with Magical Concoctions
pride.description: Monks who become Warriors of Pride value themselves above all. These monks focus on mastering their form, ego, and their destructive power. They display monastic aesthetics publicly with the intention of gaining the respect and admiration of "lesser beings." The prideful traditions of this order include adorning themselves with jewelry, hiding their scars, and commanding respect from those who would oppose them.
pride.features.0.description: You have gained a knack for telling embellished tales of your past achievements. You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}.
pride.features.0.name: Tall Tales
pride.features.1.description: Your ego strengthens as you fight to prove your value. When you expend a Focus Point, you can also gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Point|XPHB}). While you are {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB}, you gain twice that amount instead.
pride.features.1.name: Bruised Ego
pride.features.2.description: While you are {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB}, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} and Monk weapons.
pride.features.2.name: Assertive Attacker
pride.features.3.description: Damage dealt to you is damage dealt to your pride, and that is something you simply can't allow. Whenever a creature deals damage to you, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 Focus Point. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature until the end of your next turn.
pride.features.3.name: Inescapable Retribution
pride.features.4.description: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} while you are {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB}, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra damage dealt by the attack.
pride.features.4.name: Redoubled Efforts
pride.features.5.description: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can expend 1 Focus Point to enter a trance. While in this trance, you have the following effects.
pride.features.5.details.cantCast: You can't cast or concentrate on spells.
pride.features.5.details.deathSaves: You suffer 1 {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure from damage from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} instead of 2.
pride.features.5.details.maintenance: Whenever you start your turn with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you must expend 1 Focus Point to maintain a trance, and you make {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XPHB} as normal.
pride.features.5.details.unconsciousImmu: You are immune to the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.
pride.features.5.name: Ever Prideful
pride.features.6.description: Injuries to your pride enrage you. You are considered {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB} if your current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are below your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum.
pride.features.6.name: Egotistical
pride.name: Воин Гордыни
primal-spirit.description: Many Barbarians are in tune with the natural world, but few are as mystically intertwined with it as those who walk the Path of the Primal Spirit. These Barbarians forge powerful connections with beasts and nature spirits, inspiring such ethereal beings to manifest and journey on far-flung adventures.
primal-spirit.features.0.description: You magically summon a primal spirit that adopts the form of a beast and accompanies you on your adventures. Choose its stat block: {@creature Primal Guardian|XPHB} or {@creature Primal Striker|XPHB}. In addition, choose an environment that will modify the creature's stat block: Land, Sea, or Sky. You also determine the kind of animal it is, choosing a kind appropriate for the stat block. Whatever companion you choose, it bears eldrich markings indicating its otherworldly origin.
primal-spirit.features.0.details.beastIn: The companion acts during your turn. It can move and use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action unless you take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to command it to take an action in its stat block or some other action. You can also sacrifice one of your attacks when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to command the beast to take the Beast's Strike action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the companion acts on its own and isn't limited to the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action.
primal-spirit.features.0.details.friendl: The companion is {@variantrule Friendly|XPHB} to you and your allies and obeys your commands. It vanishes if you die.
primal-spirit.features.0.details.restori: If the companion has died within the last hour, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to touch it and expend a use of your Rage. The companion returns to life immediately with all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can summon a different primal companion, which appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. You choose its stat block and appearance. If you already have a beast from this feature, the old one vanishes.
primal-spirit.features.0.name: Первобытный спутник
primal-spirit.features.1.description: While your Rage is active, your primal companion has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
primal-spirit.features.1.name: Shared Rage
primal-spirit.features.2.description: You always have the {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for them.
primal-spirit.features.2.name: Kin to Beasts
primal-spirit.features.3.description: You take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to enter a trance and choose your primal companion or one Beast currently under the effect of your {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} spell within 60 feet of yourself. For the duration of this trance, you possess the chosen creature. Once you possess a creature's body, you control it. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and senses are replaced by the creature's. You otherwise keep your game statistics. This possession ends if you choose to exit the trance (no action required by you), if the Beast you're possessing is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, or if you and the Beast are on different planes of existence. While in this trance, your body falls into a catatonic state. You can't move or take {@variantrule Reactions|XPHB}, and you're unaware of your surroundings. You can remain in the trance for a number of hours up to half your Barbarian level plus your Constitution modifier. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending one use of your Rage (no action).
primal-spirit.features.3.name: Skinrider's Trance
primal-spirit.features.4.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can choose a new form for your primal companion, causing it to transform instantaneously. When you cause your primal companion to transform in this way, its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} change to its new {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending one use of your Rage (no action required).
primal-spirit.features.4.name: Shape of the Wild
primal-spirit.name: Путь Первобытного Духа
primordial-archer.description: In the areas of Etharis where nature still holds sway, many cling to the ancient customs of woodland hunters and witches. Marking themselves with wild plants and bone needles, theirs is a tradition of cunning, cruelty, and reprisal against all trespassers. Unlike the Druids, these wood witches twist natural magic into hexes, imbuing their weapons with nature's fury.
primordial-archer.features.0.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can imbue a Longbow or Shortbow with elemental energy for 1 minute. Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. For the duration, the imbued weapon deals damage of the selected type instead of its normal type and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the chosen type when it hits. At the start of each of your turns, you can change this choice. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
primordial-archer.features.0.name: Elemental Arrows
primordial-archer.features.1.description: You gain an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} and are proficient with it. You can use the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to stabilize an Unconscious creature within 5 feet of you that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as if using a {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB} without needing to make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. If you take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, an Unconscious creature within 5 feet of you that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} gains 1 Hit Point instead. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
primordial-archer.features.1.name: Herbal Lore
primordial-archer.features.2.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Primordial Archer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
primordial-archer.features.2.name: Primordial Archer Spells
primordial-archer.features.3.description: With 1 hour of work or when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can use an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} to mark yourself with elemental patterns. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.
primordial-archer.features.3.name: Weave the Elements
primordial-archer.features.4.description: You gain the ability to imbue curses into your arrows. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a ranged attack using a Longbow or Shortbow, you can expend a level 1+ spell slot to choose one of the following effects (no action required). The listed damage increases by {@damage 2d6} for each spell slot above 1.
primordial-archer.features.4.details.arc: {@b Arcing Shot.} Electricity crackles around the arrow. Make a ranged attack roll with the same weapon against a second creature within 30 feet of the first that is also within your range. Each creature hit takes {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage.
primordial-archer.features.4.details.ent: {@b Entangling Shot.} The wooden shaft of the arrow sprouts tiny green leaves. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage and has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, the creature takes the damage only. A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
primordial-archer.features.4.details.hex: {@b Hexing Shot.} The magic surrounding your arrow clouds the mind of your target. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage and gains the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute (your choice). The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.
primordial-archer.features.4.details.vip: {@b Viper Shot.} The arrow transforms into a hissing serpent. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Poison damage and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.
primordial-archer.features.4.name: Witching Arrows
primordial-archer.features.5.description: Taking damage can't break your {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} on any Ranger spells you cast. In addition, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to change the damage type you chose for the Weave the Elements feature to a different damage type in the list. When you do, you can choose a creature that you can see within 30 feet of yourself. That creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 6d6} damage of either damage type (your choice). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
primordial-archer.features.5.name: Primordial Magic
primordial-archer.name: Первозданный Стрелок
primordial-archer.shortDescription: Channel the Wrath of the Wilds
psi-warrior.description: Psi Warriors are Fighters who augment their physical might with psi-infused weapon strikes, telekinetic lashes, and barriers of mental force. Many githyanki train to become such combatants, as do some of the most disciplined high elves. In the world of Eberron, many young kalashtar dream of mastering their innate psionic talent and joining the Psi Warriors. As a Psi Warrior, you hone your psionic abilities alongside your combat skills, using each to enhance your fighting prowess.
psi-warrior.features.0.description: You harbor a wellspring of psionic energy within yourself. It is represented by your Psionic Energy Dice, which fuel powers you have from this subclass. The Psi Warrior Energy Dice table shows the die size and number of these dice you have when you reach certain Fighter levels. Any features in this subclass that use a Psionic Energy Die use only the dice from this subclass. Some of your powers expend the Psionic Energy Die, as specified in a power's description, and you can't use a power if it requires you to use a die when all your Psionic Energy Dice are expended. You regain one of your expended Psionic Energy Dice when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all of them when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
psi-warrior.features.0.details.protectiv: {@b Protective Field.} When you or another creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend one Psionic Energy Die, roll the die, and reduce the damage taken by the number rolled plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum reduction of 1), as you create a momentary shield of telekinetic force.
psi-warrior.features.0.details.psionicSt: {@b Psionic Strike.} You can propel your weapons with psionic force. Once on each of your turns, immediately after you hit a target within 30 feet of you with an attack and deal damage to it with a weapon, you can expend one Psionic Energy Die, rolling it and dealing force damage to the target equal to the number rolled plus your Intelligence modifier.
psi-warrior.features.0.details.telekinet: {@b Telekinetic Movement.} You can move an object or a creature with your mind. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you target one loose object that is Large or smaller or one willing creature, other than yourself. If you can see the target and it is within 30 feet of you, you can move it up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Alternatively, if it is a Tiny object, you can move it to or from your hand. Either way, you can move the target horizontally, vertically, or both. Once you take this action, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to take it again.
psi-warrior.features.0.name: Psionic Power
psi-warrior.features.1.description: You have mastered new ways to use your telekinetic abilities, detailed below.
psi-warrior.features.1.details.psiPowere: {@b Psi-Powered Leap.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain a Fly Speed equal to twice your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} until the end of the current turn. Once you take this Bonus Action, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to take it again.
psi-warrior.features.1.details.telekinet: {@b Telekinetic Thrust.} When you deal damage to a target with your Psionic Strike, you can force the target to make a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Intelligence modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, you can knock the target {@condition Prone|XPHB} or move it up to 10 feet in any direction horizontally.
psi-warrior.features.1.name: Telekinetic Adept
psi-warrior.features.2.description: The psionic energy flowing through you has bolstered your mind. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage. Moreover, if you start your turn with the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can expend a Psionic Energy Die and end every effect on yourself subjected to those conditions.
psi-warrior.features.2.name: Guarded Mind
psi-warrior.features.3.description: You can shield yourself and others with telekinetic force. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can choose creatures, which can include yourself, that you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number of creatures equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of the chosen creatures has {@variantrule Half Cover|XPHB} for 1 minute or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die to use it again.
psi-warrior.features.3.name: Bulwark of Force
psi-warrior.features.4.description: You always have the {@spell Telekinesis|XPHB} spell prepared. With this feature, you can cast it without a spell slot or components, and your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence. On each of your turns while you maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on it, including the turn when you cast it, you can make one attack with a weapon as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die (no action required) to restore your use of it.
psi-warrior.features.4.name: Telekinetic Master
psi-warrior.name: Пси-воин
psi-warrior.shortDescription: Augment Physical Might with Psionic Power
purification.description: The Purification Domain believes sickness, mutation, and other forms of bodily corruption are signs of a corresponding spiritual malady. Virtue can't exist alongside impurity. The wickedness of humanity and other mortal, sentient creatures is a contagion that manifests plagues such as the Weeping Pox. When there's a risk of such corruption spreading, there's only one surefire way to contain the rot and atone for the sins that led to this point: Burn it out. Purge Sickness with Suffering.
purification.features.0.description: When you deal damage with a cantrip or an attack with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can deal an additional {@damage 1d8} Fire damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
purification.features.0.name: Cleanse with Fire
purification.features.1.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Purification Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
purification.features.1.name: Purification Domain Spells
purification.features.2.description: When you hit a creature with a melee attack with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, instead of dealing the strike's normal damage, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to sear a symbol into the creature's flesh, marking it with a glowing brand for 1 minute. During that time, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against your spells. Additionally, the creature gains {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire damage you deal, even if it normally has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Fire damage.
purification.features.2.name: Unclean Brand
purification.features.3.description: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end diseases and against any effect that would change your form, such as the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch a willing creature to grant this benefit, but the creature takes Fire damage equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1). This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}. Once you grant this benefit, it lasts for 1 hour or until you grant this benefit again.
purification.features.3.name: Ward Against Corruption
purification.features.4.description: You can cast {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} and {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} on a willing creature without expending spell slots and without Material components, but the target takes {@damage 1d6} Fire damage for each level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.
purification.features.4.name: Sear Imperfections
purification.name: Домен Очищения
redemption.description: The Oath of Redemption sets a Paladin on a difficult path, one that requires a holy warrior to use violence only as a last resort. Paladins who dedicate themselves to this oath believe that any person can be redeemed and that the path of benevolence and justice is one that anyone can walk.
redemption.features.0.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Redemption Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
redemption.features.0.name: Oath of Redemption Spells
redemption.features.1.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to augment your presence with divine power. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you grant yourself a +5 bonus to Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks for the next 10 minutes.
redemption.features.1.name: Emissary of Peace
redemption.features.2.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to rebuke those who use violence. Immediately after an attacker within 30 feet of you deals damage with an attack against a creature other than you, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the attacker takes Radiant damage equal to the damage it just dealt. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage.
redemption.features.2.name: Rebuke the Violent
redemption.features.3.description: You can shield your allies from harm at the cost of your own health. When a creature within your Aura of Protection takes damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to magically take that damage instead. This feature doesn't transfer any other effects that might accompany the damage, and this damage can't be reduced in any way.
redemption.features.3.name: Aura of the Guardian
redemption.features.4.description: A holy presence mends your wounds in battle. You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d6} plus half your Paladin level if you end your turn in combat with fewer than half of your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} remaining and you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.
redemption.features.4.name: Protective Spirit
redemption.features.5.description: You become an avatar of peace, which gives you the benefits below. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Damage Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage dealt by other creatures (their attacks, spells, and other effects). {@b Radiant Rebuke.} Whenever a creature damages you, it takes Radiant damage equal to half the amount it dealt to you. If you attack a creature, cast a spell on it, or deal damage to it by any means but this feature, neither benefit works against that creature until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
redemption.features.5.name: Emissary of Redemption
redemption.name: Клятва Искупления
redemption.shortDescription: Seek Peace and Redemption for All
regret.description: No one is born intent on bringing evil into this world, yet many lives are transformed by the suffering inflicted upon them. Warriors of Regret are haunted by the shadows of evil deeds done. Through training and reflection, Warriors of Regret accept their past can't be erased. Instead, they focus on forging remorse and grief into a weapon against their foes. Though their misdeeds follow their every step, Warriors of Regret are not powerless to atone for them.
regret.features.0.description: Once per turn, on your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point (no action required) to create a shade of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of yourself. The shade is intangible and doesn't occupy its space. It lasts until the end of your next turn, but it ends early if you dismiss it (no action required) or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. While it persists, you gain the following benefits.
regret.features.0.details.move: {@b Move.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move the shade up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is within 60 feet of yourself.
regret.features.0.details.shadeStrike: {@b Shade Strike.} When you use Flurry of Blows, you can have the attacks originate from the shade instead of from yourself. Attacks originating from the shade deal Necrotic or Force damage (your choice) rather than their normal damage type.
regret.features.0.name: Shade of Regret
regret.features.1.description: When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action, instead of moving, you can teleport yourself or an ally within 60 feet of you to the location of your shade.
regret.features.1.name: The Road Not Traveled
regret.features.2.description: You can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to take the Help action or touch a creature and restore a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier. You can use this feature as though you were in the shade's space.
regret.features.2.name: Aid Not Given
regret.features.3.description: You can expend 3 Focus Points to release your pent-up guilt in a crushing wave that drops your foes to their knees. Creatures of your choice in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you or your shade must make a Wisdom saving throw against your Focus Point save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes Psychic damage equal to three rolls of your Martial Arts die and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.
regret.features.3.name: Crushing Guilt
regret.features.4.description: Your Shade of Regret now lasts for 10 minutes. After using Flurry of Blows, your Shade can make one additional {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as per Shade Strike. You can also use the Stunning Strike feature through the Shade Strike.
regret.features.4.name: Relive the Past
regret.name: Воин Сожаления
requiems.description: Performing macabre melodies filled with grief-stricken chords and mournful refrains, the College of Requiems stirs the very bones of the dead. Requiem Bards weave necromantic magic into their funerary songs to control and empower a host of Undead minions.
requiems.features.0.description: You learn two Necromancy cantrips of your choice. These count as Bard spells for you but don't count against the number of cantrips you know.
requiems.features.0.name: Chilling Melody
requiems.features.1.description: Your Bardic Inspiration can pluck at the tethers of life. Each creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can use it for one of the following effects.
requiems.features.1.details.defense: When the creature is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die to be reduced to a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die instead.
requiems.features.1.details.offense: Immediately after the creature hits a target with an attack roll, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled as extra Necrotic damage dealt by the attack.
requiems.features.1.name: Pluck the Heartstrings
requiems.features.2.description:
You can use your music to raise the dead. As an action, you target one pile of bones or a corpse of a Medium or Small Humanoid within 10 feet of you and expend one use of Bardic Inspiration. The target becomes an Undead creature that uses the {@creature Skeleton|XPHB} or {@creature Zombie|XPHB} stat block (your choice). The creature is friendly to you and your companions for the duration.

In combat, the creature shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its move to avoid danger.

The creature remains animate for 1 hour, after which it collapses. You can have a number of Undead animated by this feature at the same time equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.
requiems.features.2.name: Stir the Bones
requiems.features.3.description:
When a creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to have the creature drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} instead. The creature's appearance becomes deathly pale, and it gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage except Radiant for 1 minute.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
requiems.features.3.name: Dual Death
requiems.name: Коллегия Реквиемов
requiems.shortDescription: Sing the Songs of the Dead
rune-knight.description: Rune Knights enhance their martial prowess using the supernatural power of runes, an ancient practice that originated with giants. Rune cutters can be found among any family of giants, and you likely learned your methods first or second hand from such a mystical artisan. Whether you found the giant's cave scratched with strange markings, were ## trained by a sage who had studied giant runecraft, or met the giant yourself, you studied the giant's craft and learned how to apply the magic of the runes to empower your equipment.
rune-knight.features.0.description: You gain proficiency with {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}, and you learn to speak, read, and write Giant.
rune-knight.features.0.name: Bonus Proficiencies
rune-knight.features.1.description: You can use magic runes to enhance your gear. You learn two runes of your choice, from among the runes described below, and each time you gain a level in this class, you can replace one rune you know with a different one from this feature. When you reach certain levels in this class, you learn additional runes, as shown in the Runes Known table. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can touch a number of objects equal to the number of runes you know, and you inscribe a different rune onto each of the objects. To be eligible, an object must be a weapon, a suit of armor, a shield, a piece of jewelry, or something else you can wear or hold in a hand. Your rune remains on an object until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, and an object can bear only one of your runes at a time.
rune-knight.features.1.details.cloudRune: {@b Cloud Rune.} This rune emulates the deceptive magic used by some cloud giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) checks and Charisma ({@skill Deception}) checks. In addition, when you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to invoke the rune and choose a different creature within 30 feet of you, other than the attacker. The chosen creature becomes the target of the attack, using the same roll. This magic can transfer the attack's effects regardless of the attack's range. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.1.details.fireRune: {@b Fire Rune.} This rune's magic channels the masterful craftsmanship of great smiths. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses a tool proficiency. In addition, when you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon, you can invoke the rune to summon fiery shackles: the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} fire damage, and it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be {@condition restrained|XPHB} for 1 minute. While {@condition restrained|XPHB} by the shackles, the target takes {@damage 2d6} fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, banishing the shackles on a success. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.1.details.frostRune: {@b Frost Rune.} This rune's magic evokes the might of those who survive in the wintry wilderness, such as frost giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling}) checks and Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks. In addition, you can invoke the rune as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to increase your sturdiness. For 10 minutes, you gain a +2 bonus to all ability checks and saving throws that use Strength or Constitution. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.1.details.hillRune: {@b Hill Rune (Level 7+).} This rune's magic bestows a resilience reminiscent of a hill giant. While wearing or carrying an object that bears this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to poison damage. In addition, you can invoke the rune as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, gaining resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage for 1 minute. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.1.details.stoneRune: {@b Stone Rune.} This rune's magic channels the judiciousness associated with stone giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks, and you have {@sense darkvision|XPHB} out to a range of 120 feet. In addition, when a creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to invoke the rune and force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. Unless the save succeeds, the creature is {@condition charmed|XPHB} by you for 1 minute. While {@condition charmed|XPHB} in this way, the creature has a speed of 0 and is {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}, descending into a dreamy stupor. The creature repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.1.details.stormRune: {@b Storm Rune (Level 7+).} Using this rune, you can glimpse the future like a storm giant seer. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) checks, and you can't be {@condition surprised|XPHB} as long as you aren't {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}. In addition, you can invoke the rune as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to enter a prophetic state for 1 minute or until you're {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}. Until the state ends, when you or another creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, a saving throw, or an ability check, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause the roll to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} or {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.1.name: Rune Carver
rune-knight.features.2.description: You have learned how to imbue yourself with the might of giants. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you magically gain the following benefits, which last for 1 minute: If you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If you lack the room to become Large, your size doesn't change. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. Once on each of your turns, one of your attacks with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage to a target on a hit. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.2.name: Giant's Might
rune-knight.features.3.description: You learn to invoke your rune magic to protect your allies. When another creature you can see within 60 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force the attacker to reroll the {@dice d20} and use the new roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.3.name: Runic Shield
rune-knight.features.4.description: The magic of your runes permanently alters you. When you gain this feature, roll {@dice 3d4}. You grow a number of inches in height equal to the roll. Moreover, the extra damage you deal with your Giant's Might feature increases to {@damage 1d8}.
rune-knight.features.4.name: Great Stature
rune-knight.features.5.description: You can invoke each rune you know from your Rune Carver feature twice, rather than once, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rune-knight.features.5.name: Master of Runes
rune-knight.features.6.description: You learn how to amplify your rune-powered transformation. As a result, the extra damage you deal with the Giant's Might feature increases to {@damage 1d10}. Moreover, when you use that feature, your size can increase to Huge, and while you are that size, your reach increases by 5 feet.
rune-knight.features.6.name: Runic Juggernaut
rune-knight.name: Рунный Рыцарь
rune-knight.shortDescription: Enhance Equipment with Giant Rune Magic
samurai.description: The Samurai is a fighter who draws on an implacable fighting spirit to overcome enemies. A Samurai's resolve is nearly unbreakable, and the enemies in a Samurai's path have two choices: yield or die fighting.
samurai.features.0.description: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.
samurai.features.0.name: Дополнительное владение
samurai.features.1.description: Your intensity in battle can shield you and help you strike true. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can give yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on weapon attack rolls until the end of the current turn. When you do so, you also gain 5 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. The number of temporary hit points increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 10 at 10th level and 15 at 15th level. You can use this feature three times, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
samurai.features.1.name: Fighting Spirit
samurai.features.2.description: Your discipline and attention to detail allow you to excel in social situations. Whenever you make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier. Your self-control also causes you to gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).
samurai.features.2.name: Elegant Courtier
samurai.features.3.description: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no uses of Fighting Spirit remaining, you regain one use.
samurai.features.3.name: Tireless Spirit
samurai.features.4.description: You learn to trade accuracy for swift strikes. If you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an attack roll against one of the targets, you can forgo the advantage for that roll to make an additional weapon attack against that target, as part of the same action. You can do so no more than once per turn.
samurai.features.4.name: Rapid Strike
samurai.features.5.description: Your fighting spirit can delay the grasp of death. If you take damage that reduces you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and doesn't kill you outright, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to delay falling {@condition unconscious|XPHB}, and you can immediately take an extra turn, interrupting the current turn. While you have 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} during that extra turn, taking damage causes death saving throw failures as normal, and three death saving throw failures can still kill you. When the extra turn ends, you fall {@condition unconscious|XPHB} if you still have 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
samurai.features.5.name: Strength Before Death
samurai.name: Самурай
sangromancer.description: You study an uncommon school of magic known as Sangromancy or blood magic. Despite its dark reputation, there's nothing inherently evil about the practice of Sangromancy, though its demands on its practitioners are gruesome. As a Sangromancer, your chosen magic demands more than knowledge. It demands sacrifice. Other Wizards may view you with skepticism or even outright hostility, but none can deny the potency of your art.
sangromancer.features.0.description:
Sangromancy spells count as Wizard spells for you. Choose two Sangromancy spells, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Sangromancy spell to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
sangromancer.features.0.name: Sangromancy Savant
sangromancer.features.1.description:
You draw magic from blood. It is represented by your Sangromancy Dice, which fuel powers you have from this subclass. You have a pool of {@dice d12}s that you can expend instead of a {@dice Hit Die} when you cast Sangromancy spells. The number of dice in the pool equals 1 plus your Wizard level.

You regain one expended Sangromancy Die when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all expended Sangromancy Dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sangromancer.features.1.name: Full-Blooded
sangromancer.features.2.description: Your Hit Point maximum increases by 6, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Wizard level. In addition, whenever you cast a Sangromancy spell with a spell slot, you regain Hit Points equal to the level of the spell slot.
sangromancer.features.2.name: Sanguine Vigor
sangromancer.features.3.description: Once on each of your turns when you deal damage to one or more creatures with a Wizard spell you cast, you can spend a Hit Point Die or a Sangromancy Die, roll the die, and deal extra damage to one of those creatures equal to the roll. If the creature is {@status Bloodied}, you can roll twice and use the higher of the two rolls.
sangromancer.features.3.name: Blood for Blood
sangromancer.features.4.description: When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you regain expended Hit Point Dice and Sangromancy Dice equal to half your Wizard level. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sangromancer.features.4.name: Red Renewal
sangromancer.name: Сангромант
sanguine-thief.description: In dark alleyways across Etharis, deals are made in coin and blood, but a few enterprising Rogues have posed the question: 'Why not both?' Sanguine Thieves are assassins who harness the power of Sanguromancy to fuel their abilities, finding new and innovative ways to utilize the blood they see all too frequently in their line of work. Sanguine Thieves learn to draw upon the vitae of their victims, extending their ability to use blood magic beyond the limits of their own vitality. When cornered, Sanguine Thieves can unleash this power in a hail of crimson darts or fade before a foe's blade, leaving nothing behind but a red mist.
sanguine-thief.features.0.description: You have learned to cast Wizard spells, as well as spells from the Sanguromancy school. All Sanguromancy spells and cantrips are treated as Wizard spells for the purposes of this subclass.
sanguine-thief.features.0.details.cantri: You know three cantrips from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard} and from the list of Sanguromancy spells. Whenever you gain a Rogue level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another Wizard cantrip of your choice. When you reach Rogue level 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice.
sanguine-thief.features.0.details.changi: Whenever you gain a Rogue level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Wizard spell for which you have spell slots.
sanguine-thief.features.0.details.prepar: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose three level 1 Wizard spells. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Rogue levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Sanguine Thief Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Wizard spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the Sanguine Thief Spellcasting table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
sanguine-thief.features.0.details.spellS: The Sanguine Thief Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sanguine-thief.features.0.details.spellc: Интеллект — ваша заклинательная характеристика для заклинаний Волшебника.
Вы можете использовать {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB|Магическую фокусировку} в качестве Заклинательной фокусировки для своих заклинаний Волшебника.
sanguine-thief.features.0.name: Использование заклинаний
sanguine-thief.features.1.description: You draw magic from blood. It is represented by your Sanguromancy Dice, which fuel powers you have from this subclass. You have a pool of {@dice d8}s that you can use on Sanguine Thief features. The number of dice in the pool equals the number of damage dice as shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue Features table. You can't have more Sanguromancy Dice than the number shown in the Sneak Attack column for your level, unless you have Sanguromancy Dice from a different source.
sanguine-thief.features.1.details.bloodM: You can spend Sanguromancy Dice instead of {@variantrule Hit Dice|XPHB} when you cast Sanguromancy spells.
sanguine-thief.features.1.details.recove: Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sanguine-thief.features.1.name: Stolen Power
sanguine-thief.features.2.description: When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can restore 1 Sanguromancy Die. If you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, instead of restoring 1 Sanguromancy Die when you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can immediately roll the die and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sanguine-thief.features.2.name: Steal Blood
sanguine-thief.name: Кровавый Вор
sanguine-thief.shortDescription: Eliminate Your Prey with Blood Magic
scion-of-the-three.description: A Scion of the Three draws power from a group of malevolent gods known as the Dead Three: Bane, deity of tyranny; Bhaal, deity of violence and murder; and Myrkul, deity of death. While some Rogues of this subclass pledge themselves ardently to those three macabre gods, others find themselves thrust on this path by a curse. Either way, a scion's power manifests as various occult gifts, as well as an uncanny talent for striking and terrifying foes.
scion-of-the-three.features.0.descriptio: You forge a dark pact with one of the Dead Three, gaining occult gifts. Choose one of the following allegiances. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choice. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for the cantrip you gain from this feature.
scion-of-the-three.features.0.details.ba: {@b Bane.} You gain Resistance to Psychic damage and learn the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} cantrip.
scion-of-the-three.features.0.details.bh: {@b Bhaal.} You gain Resistance to Poison damage and learn the {@spell Blade Ward|XPHB} cantrip.
scion-of-the-three.features.0.details.my: {@b Myrkul.} You gain Resistance to Necrotic damage and learn the {@spell Chill Touch|XPHB} cantrip.
scion-of-the-three.features.0.name: Dread Allegiance
scion-of-the-three.features.1.descriptio: The Dead Three grant you the power to cull the weak, giving you the following benefits.
scion-of-the-three.features.1.details.bo: {@b Bonus Damage.} When your Sneak Attack hits a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature, the target takes extra damage of the weapon's type equal to half your Rogue level (round up).
scion-of-the-three.features.1.details.te: {@b Teleport Strike.} When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you takes damage and is {@status Bloodied|XPHB} after taking that damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of that creature and make one melee attack against it. You can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
scion-of-the-three.features.1.name: Bloodthirst
scion-of-the-three.features.2.descriptio: Your Cunning Strike gains the following additional option.
scion-of-the-three.features.2.details.te: {@b Terrify (Cost: 1d6).} The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB} in this way, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the target. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
scion-of-the-three.features.2.name: Strike Fear
scion-of-the-three.features.3.descriptio: Your Bloodthirst's Teleport Strike now radiates deadly energy. When you teleport using Teleport Strike, each creature of your choice within 10 feet of the space you left or the space you arrive in takes damage equal to your Intelligence modifier. The damage type is determined by your current Dread Allegiance: Psychic for Bane, Poison for Bhaal, or Necrotic for Myrkul. This damage ignores Resistance.
scion-of-the-three.features.3.name: Aura of Malevolence
scion-of-the-three.features.4.descriptio: You become a living avatar of the Dead Three, gaining the following benefits.
scion-of-the-three.features.4.details.cu: {@b Cutthroat.} When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you regain one expended use of Bloodthirst's Teleport Strike.
scion-of-the-three.features.4.details.mu: {@b Murderous Intent.} When you roll a Sneak Attack damage die and get a 1 or 2, you can treat the die as a 3.
scion-of-the-three.features.4.name: Dread Incarnate
scion-of-the-three.name: Наследник Троих
scion-of-the-three.shortDescription: Channel the Power of the Dead Three
scout.description: You are skilled in stealth and surviving far from the streets of a city, allowing you to scout ahead of your companions during expeditions. Rogues who embrace this archetype are at home in the wilderness and among barbarians and rangers, and many Scouts serve as the eyes and ears of war bands. Ambusher, spy, bounty hunter—these are just a few of the roles that Scouts assume as they range the world.
scout.features.0.description: You are difficult to pin down during a fight. You can move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when an enemy ends its turn within 5 feet of you. This movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.
scout.features.0.name: Skirmisher
scout.features.1.description: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Nature} and {@skill Survival} skills if you don't already have it. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.
scout.features.1.name: Survivalist
scout.features.2.description: Your walking {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. If you have a Climb or Swim Speed, this increase applies to that {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} as well.
scout.features.2.name: Superior Mobility
scout.features.3.description: You excel at leading ambushes and acting first in a fight. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls. In addition, the first creature you hit during the first round of a combat becomes easier for you and others to strike; attack rolls against that target have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.
scout.features.3.name: Ambush Master
scout.features.4.description: You can strike with deadly speed. If you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can make one additional attack as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. This attack can benefit from your Sneak Attack even if you have already used it this turn, but you can't use your Sneak Attack against the same target more than once in a turn.
scout.features.4.name: Sudden Strike
scout.name: Разведчик
scribes.description:
Magic of the book—that's what many folk call wizardry. The name is apt, given how much time wizards spend poring over tomes and penning theories about the nature of magic. It's rare to see wizards traveling without books and scrolls sprouting from their bags, and a wizard would go to great lengths to plumb an archive of ancient knowledge.

Among wizards, the Order of Scribes is the most bookish. It takes many forms in different worlds, but its primary mission is the same everywhere: recording magical discoveries so that wizardry can flourish. And while all wizards value spellbooks, a wizard in the Order of Scribes magically awakens their book, turning it into a trusted companion. All wizards study books, but a wizardly scribe talks to theirs!
scribes.features.0.description:
{@i 2nd-level Order of Scribes feature}

As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically create a Tiny quill in your free hand. The magic quill has the following properties:

• The quill doesn't require ink. When you write with it, it produces ink in a color of your choice on the writing surface.

• The time you must spend to copy a spell into your spellbook equals 2 minutes per spell level if you use the quill for the transcription.

• You can erase anything you write with the quill if you wave the feather over the text as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, provided the text is within 5 feet of you.

This quill disappears if you create another one or if you die.
scribes.features.0.name: Wizardly Quill
scribes.features.1.description:
{@i 2nd-level Order of Scribes feature}

Using specially prepared inks and ancient incantations passed down by your wizardly order, you have awakened an arcane sentience within your spellbook.

While you are holding the book, it grants you the following benefits:

• You can use the book as a spellcasting focus for your Wizard spells.

• When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot, you can temporarily replace its damage type with a type that appears in another spell in your spellbook, which magically alters the spell's formula for this casting only. The latter spell must be of the same level as the spell slot you expend.

• When you cast a Wizard spell as a ritual, you can use the spell's normal casting time, rather than adding 10 minutes to it. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

If necessary, you can replace the book over the course of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} by using your Wizardly Quill to write arcane sigils in a blank book or a magic spellbook to which you're attuned. At the end of the rest, your spellbook's consciousness is summoned into the new book, which the consciousness transforms into your spellbook, along with all its spells. If the previous book still existed somewhere, all the spells vanish from its pages.
scribes.features.1.name: Awakened Spellbook
scribes.features.2.description:
{@i 6th-level Order of Scribes feature}

You can conjure forth the mind of your Awakened Spellbook. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} while the book is on your person, you can cause the mind to manifest as a Tiny spectral object, hovering in an unoccupied space of your choice within 60 feet of you. The spectral mind is intangible and doesn't occupy its space, and it sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius. It looks like a ghostly tome, a cascade of text, or a scholar from the past (your choice).

While manifested, the spectral mind can hear and see, and it has {@sense darkvision} with a range of 60 feet. The mind can telepathically share with you what it sees and hears (no action required).

Whenever you cast a wizard spell on your turn, you can cast it as if you were in the spectral mind's space, instead of your own, using its senses. You can do so a number of times per day equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can cause the spectral mind to hover up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you or it can see. It can pass through creatures but not objects.

The spectral mind stops manifesting if it is ever more than 300 feet away from you, if someone casts {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} on it, if the Awakened Spellbook is destroyed, if you die, or if you dismiss the spectral mind as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.

Once you conjure the mind, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a spell slot of any level to conjure it again.
scribes.features.2.name: Manifest Mind
scribes.features.3.description:
{@i 10th-level Order of Scribes feature}

Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create one magic scroll by touching your Wizardly Quill to a blank piece of paper or parchment and causing one spell from your Awakened Spellbook to be copied onto the scroll. The spellbook must be within 5 feet of you when you make the scroll.

The chosen spell must be of 1st or 2nd level and must have a casting time of 1 action. Once in the scroll, the spell's power is enhanced, counting as one level higher than normal. You can cast the spell from the scroll by reading it as an action. The scroll is unintelligible to anyone else, and the spell vanishes from the scroll when you cast it or when you finish your next long rest.

You are also adept at crafting spell scrolls, which are described in the treasure chapter of the Dungeon Master's Guide. The gold and time you must spend to make such a scroll are halved if you use your Wizardly Quill.
scribes.features.3.name: Master Scrivener
scribes.features.4.description:
{@i 14th-level Order of Scribes feature}

Your connection to your Awakened Spellbook has become so profound that your soul has become entwined with it. While the book is on your person, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) checks, as the spellbook helps you remember magical lore.

Moreover, if you take damage while your spellbook's mind is manifested, you can prevent all of that damage to you by using your {@action Reaction|XPHB} to dismiss the spectral mind, using its magic to save yourself. Then roll {@dice 3d6}. The spellbook temporarily loses spells of your choice that have a combined spell level equal to that roll or higher. For example, if the roll's total is 9, spells vanish from the book that have a combined level of at least 9, which could mean one 9th-level spell, three 3rd-level spells, or some other combination. If there aren't enough spells in the book to cover this cost, you drop to 0 Hit Points.

Until you finish {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}s, you are incapable of casting the lost spells, even if you find them on a scroll or in another spellbook. After you finish the required number of rests, the spells reappear in the spellbook.

Once you use this {@action Reaction|XPHB}, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
scribes.features.4.name: One with the Word
scribes.name: Орден Писцов
scribes.shortDescription: Awaken Your Spellbook as a Companion
sea.description: Druids of the Circle of the Sea draw on the tempestuous forces of oceans and storms. Some view themselves as embodiments of nature's wrath, seeking vengeance against those who despoil nature. Others seek mystical unity with nature by attuning themselves to the ebb and flow of the tides, following the rush of currents and waves and listening to the inscrutable whispers and roars of the winds.
sea.features.0.description: When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the Sea Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
sea.features.0.name: Circle of the Sea Spells
sea.features.1.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to manifest a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} that takes the form of ocean spray that surrounds you for 10 minutes. It ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), manifest it again, or have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition. When you manifest the {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} and as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your subsequent turns, you can choose another creature you can see in the {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB}. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take Cold damage, and if the creature is Large or smaller, be pushed up to 15 feet away from you. To determine this damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one {@dice d6}).
sea.features.1.name: Wrath of the Sea
sea.features.2.description: The size of the {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} created by your Wrath of the Sea increases to 10 feet. In addition, you gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
sea.features.2.name: Aquatic Affinity
sea.features.3.description: Your Wrath of the Sea confers two more benefits while active, as detailed below. {@b Flight.} You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. {@b Resistance.} You have Resistance to Cold, Lightning, and Thunder damage.
sea.features.3.name: Stormborn
sea.features.4.description: Instead of manifesting the {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} of Wrath of the Sea around yourself, you can manifest it around one willing creature within 60 feet of yourself. That creature gains all the benefits of the {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} and uses your spell save DC and Wisdom modifier for it. In addition, you can manifest the {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} around both the other creature and yourself if you expend two uses of your Wild Shape instead of one when manifesting it.
sea.features.4.name: Oceanic Gift
sea.name: Круг Моря
sea.shortDescription: Become One with Tides and Storms
secret-agent.description: Knowledge is power. The best way to defeat your enemies is by stealing what they know and replacing it with misinformation. To that end, you have been trained in the ways of covert warfare, giving you a broad range of abilities to complement your fearsome gunnery skills.
secret-agent.features.0.description: Your covert training grants you the following benefits.
secret-agent.features.0.details.conceale: You learn the {@spell Concealed Shot|GC:VSS'24} cantrip. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this cantrip (choose when you select this subclass).
secret-agent.features.0.details.operativ: You gain a {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB} and {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, and you have proficiency with them.
secret-agent.features.0.details.skillPro: You gain proficiency in two of these skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Persuasion}, {@skill Sleight of Hand}, or {@skill Stealth}.
secret-agent.features.0.name: Operative Training
secret-agent.features.1.description: [Maneuver] When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Dodge|XPHB} action on your turn, you can expend one Risk Die to make a ranged attack using a weapon as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Add the Risk Die to the damage roll on a hit.
secret-agent.features.1.name: Parting Shot
secret-agent.features.2.description: Your experience in the field grants you the following benefits.
secret-agent.features.2.details.quickCha: Using a {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}, you can create a Costume and don it as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
secret-agent.features.2.details.slickTal: Whenever you make a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) or Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
secret-agent.features.2.name: Fieldcraft
secret-agent.features.3.description: When you take damage, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to evade further harm. You have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn, and you can immediately move up to 10 feet. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending one Risk Die (no action required).
secret-agent.features.3.name: Exit Strategy
secret-agent.features.4.description: Whenever you deal damage with a Ranged weapon, you can expend either one or two Risk Dice and add them to the damage roll. If you roll the highest number on a Risk Die, you can roll the die again and add it to the damage without expending it, rolling again if it is the highest number again, and so on. The maximum number of Risk Dice you can add to the damage equals your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.
secret-agent.features.4.name: License to Kill
secret-agent.name: Тайный Агент
shadow.description: You are a creature of shadow, for your innate magic comes from the Shadowfell itself. You might trace your lineage to an entity from that place, or perhaps you were exposed to its fell energy and transformed by it. The power of shadow magic casts a strange pall over your physical presence. The spark of life that sustains you is muffled, as if it struggles to remain viable against the dark energy that imbues your soul.
shadow.features.0.description: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet. When you reach 3rd level in this class, you learn the {@spell Darkness|XPHB} spell, which doesn't count against your number of sorcerer spells known. In addition, you can cast it by spending 2 Sorcery Points or by expending a spell slot. If you cast it with Sorcery Points, you can see through the {@spell Darkness|XPHB} created by the spell.
shadow.features.0.details.darkness: {@b Darkness.} You can expend 1 Focus Point to cast the {@spell Darkness|XPHB} spell without spell components. You can see within the spell's area when you cast it with this feature. While the spell persists, you can move its area of {@spell Darkness|XPHB} to a space within 60 feet of yourself at the start of each of your turns.
shadow.features.0.details.darkvision: {@b Darkvision.} You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet.
shadow.features.0.details.shadowyFigment: {@b Shadowy Figments.} You know the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} spell. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.
shadow.features.0.name: Eyes of the Dark
shadow.features.1.description: Your existence in a twilight state between life and death makes you difficult to defeat. When damage reduces you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can make a Charisma saving throw (DC 5 + the damage taken). On a success, you instead drop to 1 Hit Point. You can't use this feature if you are reduced to 0 Hit Points by Radiant damage or by a critical hit. After the saving throw succeeds, you can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
shadow.features.1.name: Strength of the Grave
shadow.features.2.description:
You gain the ability to call forth a howling creature of darkness to harass your foes. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend 3 Sorcery Points to magically summon a hound of ill omen to target one creature you can see within 120 feet of you. The hound uses the {@creature Dire Wolf}'s statistics, with the following changes:

- The hound is size Medium, not Large, and it counts as a Monstrosity, not a Beast.
- It appears with a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your Sorcerer level.
- It can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. The hound takes 5 Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.
- At the start of its turn, the hound automatically knows its target's location. If the target was hidden, it is no longer hidden from the hound.

The hound appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 30 feet of the target. Roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} for the hound. On its turn, it can move only toward its target by the most direct route, and it can use its action only to attack its target. The hound can make {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}, but only against its target. Additionally, while the hound is within 5 feet of the target, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against any spell you cast. The hound disappears if it is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, if its target is reduced to 0 Hit Points, or after 5 minutes.
shadow.features.2.name: Hound of Ill Omen
shadow.features.3.description: You gain the ability to step from one shadow into another. When you are in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or darkness, as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can magically teleport up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or darkness.
shadow.features.3.details.invisibility: {@b Invisibility.} You have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition.
shadow.features.3.details.partiallyIncor: {@b Partially Incorporeal.} You can move through occupied spaces as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. If you end your turn in such a space, you are shunted to the last unoccupied space you were in.
shadow.features.3.details.shadowFlurry: {@b Shadow Flurry.} You can use your Flurry of Blows without expending any Focus Points.
shadow.features.3.name: Shadow Walk
shadow.features.4.description:
You can spend 6 Sorcery Points as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically transform yourself into a shadowy form. In this form, you have Resistance to all damage except Force and Radiant damage, and you can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. You take 5 Force damage if you end your turn inside an object.

You remain in this form for 1 minute. It ends early if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, if you die, or if you dismiss it as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
