shadow.features.4.name: Umbral Form
shadow.name: Теневая Магия
shepherd.description: Druids of the Circle of the Shepherd commune with the spirits of nature, especially the spirits of beasts and the fey, and call to those spirits for aid. These druids recognize that all living things play a role in the natural world, yet they focus on protecting animals and fey creatures that have difficulty defending themselves. Shepherds, as they are known, see such beasts and fey as their charges. They ward off monsters that threaten them, rebuke hunters who kill more prey than necessary, and prevent civilization from encroaching on rare animal habitats and on sites sacred to the fey.
shepherd.features.0.description: You gain the ability to converse with beasts and many fey. You learn to speak, read, and write Sylvan. In addition, beasts can understand your speech, and you gain the ability to decipher their noises and motions. Most beasts lack the intelligence to convey or understand sophisticated concepts, but a friendly beast could relay what it has seen or heard in the recent past. This ability doesn't grant you friendship with beasts, though you can combine this ability with gifts to curry favor with them as you would with any nonplayer character.
shepherd.features.0.name: Speech of the Woods
shepherd.features.1.description: You can call forth nature spirits to influence the world around you. As a bonus action, you can magically summon an incorporeal spirit to a point you can see within 60 feet of you. The spirit creates an aura in a 30-foot radius around that point. It counts as neither a creature nor an object, though it has the spectral appearance of the creature it represents. As a bonus action, you can move the spirit up to 60 feet to a point you can see. The spirit persists for 1 minute or until you're incapacitated. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. The effect of the spirit's aura depends on the type of spirit you summon from the options below.
shepherd.features.1.details.bearSpirit: {@b Bear Spirit.} The bear spirit grants you and your allies its might and endurance. Each creature of your choice in the aura when the spirit appears gains temporary hit points equal to 5 + your druid level. In addition, you and your allies gain advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws while in the aura.
shepherd.features.1.details.hawkSpirit: {@b Hawk Spirit.} The hawk spirit is a consummate hunter, aiding you and your allies with its keen sight. When a creature makes an attack roll against a target in the spirit's aura, you can use your reaction to grant advantage to that attack roll. In addition, you and your allies gain advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks while in the aura.
shepherd.features.1.details.unicornSpiri: {@b Unicorn Spirit.} The unicorn spirit lends its protection to those nearby. You and your allies gain advantage on all ability checks made to detect creatures in the spirit's aura. In addition, if you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores hit points to any creature inside or outside the aura, each creature of your choice in the aura also regains hit points equal to your druid level.
shepherd.features.1.name: Spirit Totem
shepherd.features.2.description: Beasts and fey that you conjure are more resilient than normal. Any beast or fey summoned or created by a spell that you cast gains the following benefits: the creature appears with more hit points than normal: 2 extra hit points per Hit Die it has. The damage from its natural weapons is considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks and damage.
shepherd.features.2.name: Mighty Summoner
shepherd.features.3.description: Your Spirit Totem safeguards the beasts and fey that you call forth with your magic. When a beast or fey that you summoned or created with a spell ends its turn in your Spirit Totem aura, that creature regains a number of hit points equal to half your druid level.
shepherd.features.3.name: Guardian Spirit
shepherd.features.4.description: The nature spirits you commune with protect you when you are the most defenseless. If you are reduced to 0 hit points or are incapacitated against your will, you can immediately gain the benefits of {@spell Conjure Animals} as if it were cast using a 9th-level spell slot. It summons four beasts of your choice that are challenge rating 2 or lower. The conjured beasts appear within 20 feet of you. If they receive no commands from you, they protect you from harm and attack your foes. The spell lasts for 1 hour, requiring no concentration, or until you dismiss it (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
shepherd.features.4.name: Faithful Summons
shepherd.name: Круг Пастыря
shepherd.shortDescription: Summoner of Nature Spirits and Protector of Beasts
slaughter.description: The only truth of war is death. The only virtue is to kill. The only victory is to survive. Heroism, honor, and glory are illusions. This is the creed of Tormach, Arch Daemon of Wrath, but this philosophy rings true for all warriors who devote themselves, body and soul, to the thrill of combat and the delight of bloodshed. Such champions find themselves at the forefront of armies, using the most violent methods at their disposal to bring battles to a swift close... or drawing out the war to sate their own appetites.
slaughter.features.0.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Slaughter Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
slaughter.features.0.name: Oath of Slaughter Spells
slaughter.features.1.description: You can harness the oath of battle to drive you to even greater acts of violence. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to enter a battle frenzy. You gain the following benefits while this feature is active.
slaughter.features.1.details.conditionRe: {@b Condition Resistance.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, and {@condition Stunned|XPHB} conditions.
slaughter.features.1.details.duration: {@b Duration.} Frenzied Slaughter lasts until the end of your next turn, and it ends early if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. If your Frenzied Slaughter is still active on your next turn, you can extend it for another round by doing one of the following: make an attack roll against an enemy; force an enemy to make a saving throw; or you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB} at the end of your turn. Each time Frenzied Slaughter is extended, it lasts until the end of your next turn. You can maintain it for up to 1 minute.
slaughter.features.1.details.reflexiveAt: {@b Reflexive Attack.} When you miss with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make another attack with the same weapon.
slaughter.features.1.name: Frenzied Slaughter
slaughter.features.2.description: Your lust for blood affects those around you. When a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} ally within your Aura of Protection makes an attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it gains a bonus to damage. The bonus equals your Charisma modifier. Additionally, when a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature within your Aura of Protection makes a saving throw against a Sanguimancy spell, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the save.
slaughter.features.2.name: Bloodthirst Aura
slaughter.features.3.description: When a creature in your Aura of Protection becomes {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and make a Melee weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. This movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.
slaughter.features.3.name: Follow Through
slaughter.features.4.description: Your bloodlust imbues you with preternatural strength and resilience, allowing you to keep sowing slaughter. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). {@b Crimson Armor.} When a creature within your Aura of Protection becomes {@status Bloodied|XPHB} by an enemy, you gain 30 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. {@b Seeing Red.} When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. {@b Wanton Slaughter.} When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can choose any number of creatures within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach. Each chosen creature takes Force damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1) plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.
slaughter.features.4.name: Blood Knight
slaughter.name: Клятва Бойни
soulknife.description: A Soulknife strikes with the mind, cutting through barriers both physical and psychic. These Rogues discover psionic power within themselves and channel it to do their roguish work. They find easy employment as members of thieves' guilds, though they are often mistrusted, and most combatants find a fight against a Soulknife to be an unsettling experience.
soulknife.features.0.description: You harbor a wellspring of psionic energy within yourself. It is represented by your Psionic Energy Dice, which fuel various powers you have from this subclass. The Soulknife Energy Dice table shows the die size and number of these dice you have when you reach certain Rogue levels.
soulknife.features.0.details.psiBolstere: {@b Psi-Bolstered Knack.} If you fail an ability check using a skill or tool with which you have proficiency, you can roll one Psionic Energy Die and add the number rolled to the check, potentially turning failure into success. You expend the die only if the roll then succeeds.
soulknife.features.0.details.psychicWhis: {@b Psychic Whispers.} You can establish telepathic communication between yourself and others. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose one or more creatures you can see, up to a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus, and then roll one Psionic Energy Die. For a number of hours equal to the number rolled, the chosen creatures can speak telepathically with you, and you can speak telepathically with them. To send or receive a message (no action required), you and the other creature must be within 1 mile of each other. A creature can end the telepathic connection at any time (no action required). The first time you use this power after each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you don't expend the Psionic Energy Die. All other times you use the power, you expend the die.
soulknife.features.0.name: Psionic Power
soulknife.features.1.description: You can manifest shimmering blades of psychic energy. Whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action or make an {@variantrule Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, you can manifest a psychic blade in your free hand and make the attack with that blade. The magic blade has the following traits.
soulknife.features.1.details.bonusAttack: {@b Bonus Attack.} After you attack with the blade on your turn, you can make a melee or ranged attack with a second psychic blade as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on the same turn, provided your other hand is free to create it. The damage die of this bonus attack is {@damage 1d4}, instead of {@damage 1d6}.
soulknife.features.1.details.stats: The blade deals {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage on a hit and has the {@variantrule Finesse|XPHB} and {@variantrule Thrown|XPHB} properties, with a normal range of 60 feet and no long range. The blade vanishes the instant after it hits or misses its target. It leaves no mark on its target.
soulknife.features.1.name: Psychic Blades
soulknife.features.2.description: Your Psychic Blades are now an expression of your psi-suffused soul, giving you the following powers that use your Psionic Energy Dice.
soulknife.features.2.details.homingStrik: {@b Homing Strikes.} If you make an attack roll with your Psychic Blade and miss the target, you can roll one Psionic Energy Die and add the number rolled to the attack roll. If this causes the attack to hit, you expend the Psionic Energy Die.
soulknife.features.2.details.psychicTele: {@b Psychic Teleportation.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you manifest a Psychic Blade, expend one Psionic Energy Die and roll it, and throw the blade at an unoccupied space you can see up to a number of feet away equal to 10 times the number rolled. You then teleport to that space, and the blade vanishes.
soulknife.features.2.name: Soul Blades
soulknife.features.3.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can magically become {@condition Invisible|XPHB}, along with anything you are wearing or carrying, for 1 hour. This {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition ends early immediately after you deal damage to a creature or you force a creature to make a saving throw. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a Psionic Energy Die (no action required) to restore your use of it.
soulknife.features.3.name: Psychic Veil
soulknife.features.4.description: When you use your Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can force that target to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your proficiency bonus plus your Dexterity modifier). If the save fails, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The {@condition Stunned|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend three Psionic Energy Dice (no action required) to restore your use of it.
soulknife.features.4.name: Rend Mind
soulknife.name: Нож Души
spellfire.description: Your innate power stems from the source of magic itself: the Weave. This connection manifests as a rare ability known as spellfire, and you surge with radiant bursts of this raw magic. Your talent with spellfire allows you to heal allies, sear enemies, and absorb powerful spells. Wielders of spellfire tend to have a penchant for wandering. Many travel between cosmopolitan settlements, such as those along the Sword Coast, and wield their magic in service of the common good. Others realize their own strange powers by roaming equally strange lands, from the magic-blasted wastes of the desert of Anauroch to the god-touched wilds of the Old Empires. Wherever they go in the Realms, spellfire Sorcerers are courted by factions with interests in the arcane arts, such as the Harpers, Cult of the Dragon, and Red Wizards.
spellfire.features.0.description: When you spend at least 1 Sorcery Point as part of the {@action Magic|XPHB} action or a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can unleash one of the following magical effects of your choice. You can do so only once per turn.
spellfire.features.0.details.bolsteringF: {@b Bolstering Flames.} You or one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d4} plus your Charisma modifier.
spellfire.features.0.details.radiantFire: {@b Radiant Fire.} One creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself takes {@dice 1d4} Fire or Radiant damage (your choice).
spellfire.features.0.name: Spellfire Burst
spellfire.features.1.description: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Spellfire Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
spellfire.features.1.name: Spellfire Spells
spellfire.features.2.description:
You always have {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} prepared.

Additionally, whenever a target fails the saving throw against a {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} you cast, you regain {@dice 1d4} Sorcery Points.
spellfire.features.2.name: Absorb Spells
spellfire.features.3.description: Your Spellfire Burst improves. You add your Sorcerer level to the {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} gained from Bolstering Flames, and the damage of Radiant Fire increases to {@dice 1d8}.
spellfire.features.3.name: Honed Spellfire
spellfire.features.4.description:
When you use Innate Sorcery, you can alter it and infuse yourself with the essence of spellfire, gaining the following benefits while this use of Innate Sorcery is active. Once you use this feature to alter Innate Sorcery, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

{@b Burning Life Force.} Once per turn when you are hit by an attack roll, you can expend a number of Hit Point Dice, up to a maximum equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). Roll the expended dice, and reduce the amount of damage from that attack equal to the total rolled.

{@b Flight.} You gain a Fly Speed of 60 feet and can hover.

{@b Spell Avoidance.} When you're subjected to a spell or magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You can't use this benefit if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
spellfire.features.4.name: Crown of Spellfire
spellfire.name: Магический Огонь
spellslinger.description: Магия и оружие не так уж различны — арканная сила подобна пороху, заклинание подобно пуле, а вы — оружию, направляющему свои заклинания с точностью на несчастливые цели. Заклинатели сочетают дисциплины стрельбы и сотворения заклинаний, иногда заряжая свои выстрелы арканными зарядами и стреляя пулями, усиленными электричеством, огнём или пламенем.
spellslinger.features.0.description: Вы дополняете свои пули способностью сотворять заклинания.
spellslinger.features.0.details.cantrips: Вы знаете два заговора на ваш выбор из списка заклинаний Волшебника (см. список в разделе этого класса). Рекомендуются {@spell Fire Bolt|XPHB|Огненный снаряд} и {@spell Message|XPHB|Послание}. Каждый раз, получая уровень Стрелка, вы можете заменить один из этих заговоров другим заговором на ваш выбор из списка заклинаний Волшебника. Когда вы достигаете 10-го уровня Стрелка, вы изучаете ещё один заговор Волшебника на ваш выбор.
spellslinger.features.0.details.changing: Каждый раз, получая уровень Стрелка, вы можете заменить одно заклинание из вашего списка другим заклинанием Волшебника, для которого у вас есть ячейки заклинаний.
spellslinger.features.0.details.prepared: Вы готовите список заклинаний 1-го уровня и выше, доступных для сотворения этим умением. Для начала выберите три заклинания 1-го уровня из списка заклинаний Волшебника. Рекомендуются {@spell Chromatic Orb|XPHB|Хроматический шар}, {@spell Jump|XPHB|Прыжок} и {@spell Shield|XPHB|Щит}. Количество заклинаний в вашем списке увеличивается по мере получения уровней Стрелка, как показано в столбце «Подготовленные заклинания» таблицы «Сотворение заклинаний Заклинателя». Каждый раз, когда это число увеличивается, выбирайте дополнительные заклинания из списка заклинаний Волшебника, пока количество заклинаний в вашем списке не сравняется с числом в таблице. Выбранные заклинания должны быть уровня, для которого у вас есть ячейки заклинаний. Например, если вы Стрелок 7-го уровня, ваш список подготовленных заклинаний может включать пять заклинаний Волшебника 1-го и 2-го уровней в любом сочетании.
spellslinger.features.0.details.spellSlo: В таблице «Сотворение заклинаний Заклинателя» показано, сколько у вас ячеек заклинаний для сотворения заклинаний 1-го уровня и выше. Вы восстанавливаете все потраченные ячейки, когда завершаете {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}.
spellslinger.features.0.details.spellcas: Интеллект — ваша заклинательная характеристика для заклинаний Стрелка.
Вы можете использовать {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB|Магическую фокусировку} или Дальнобойное оружие как {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB|Заклинательную фокусировку} для заклинаний Волшебника.
spellslinger.features.0.name: Использование заклинаний
spellslinger.features.1.description: Вы можете использовать магию вместо оружия.
spellslinger.features.1.details.arcaneSh: Когда вы попадаете по цели атакой {@spell Finger Guns|GC:VSS'24|Пальцы-пистолеты}, вы можете {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием} потратить одну Кость риска и добавить её к броску урона.
spellslinger.features.1.name: Бах, ты мёртв!
spellslinger.features.2.description: Когда в свой ход вы совершаете действие {@action Attack|XPHB|Атака}, вы можете заменить одну из атак сотворением одного из ваших заговоров Волшебника со временем сотворения «действие».
spellslinger.features.2.name: Заклинательный выстрел
spellslinger.features.3.description: Ваш опыт борьбы с заклинателями даёт вам следующие преимущества.
spellslinger.features.3.details.abrogati: Каждый раз, когда вы совершаете бросок дальнобойной атаки, вы временно нарушаете защитную магию, влияющую на цель. На время атаки эффекты заклинаний, нацеленных на существо, такие как {@spell Mage Armor|XPHB|Магический доспех}, а также свойства и силы магических предметов, надетых или несомых существом, подавляются и не функционируют. Цель атаки не может {@action Reaction|XPHB|Реакцией} сотворять заклинания вроде {@spell Shield|XPHB|Щит} в ответ на эту атаку или урон.
spellslinger.features.3.details.antimagi: Когда вы наносите {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Критическое попадание}, и цель находится под действием вашего умения Выстрел в живот, оно также мешает цели сотворять заклинания. Пока снаряд застрял в цели, она не может сотворять заклинания или совершать действие {@action Magic|XPHB|Магия}. Кроме того, цель совершает с {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB|Помехой} спасброски Телосложения для поддержания {@status Concentration|XPHB|Концентрации}.
spellslinger.features.3.details.inuredTo: Когда вы проваливаете спасбросок против заклинания или магического эффекта, вы можете {@action Reaction|XPHB|Реакцией} бросить {@dice 1d6} и добавить к броску, потенциально превратив провал в успех.
spellslinger.features.3.name: Контр-маг
spellslinger.features.4.description: [Манёвр] Когда вы совершаете бросок атаки заклинанием, вы можете {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием} потратить одну Кость риска, чтобы заменить атаку заклинанием на дальнобойную атаку дальнобойным оружием. Добавьте Кость риска к броску атаки. При попадании атака наносит обычный урон оружия в дополнение к эффектам атаки заклинанием.
spellslinger.features.4.name: Магический шквал
spellslinger.name: Заклинатель
spirits.description: Bards of the College of Spirits seek tales with inherent power—be they legends, histories, or fictions—and bring their subjects to life. Using occult trappings, these bards conjure spiritual embodiments of powerful forces to change the world once more. Such bards congregate in candlelit séances, at forgotten shrines, and at crossroads to reach the dead and channel otherworldly forces.
spirits.features.0.description: You can reach out to spirits to guide you and others. You learn the {@spell Guidance|XPHB} cantrip, which doesn't count against the number of Bard cantrips you know. The range of the cantrip is 60 feet for you when you cast it.
spirits.features.0.name: Guiding Whispers
spirits.features.1.description: You employ tools that aid you in channeling spirits. You can use the following objects as a spellcasting focus for your Bard spells: a candle, a crystal ball, a skull, a spirit board, or a {@item tarokka deck|VRGR}.
spirits.features.1.name: Spiritual Focus
spirits.features.2.description:
You reach out to spirits who tell their tales through you. While you are holding your Spiritual Focus, you can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration and roll on the Spirit Tales table using your Bardic Inspiration die to determine the tale the spirits direct you to tell. You retain the tale in mind until you bestow the tale's effect or you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You can use an action to choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you (this can be you) to be the target of the tale's effect. Once you do so, you can't bestow the tale's effect again until you roll on the Spirit Tales table again.

You can retain only one of these tales in mind at a time, and rolling on the Spirit Tales table immediately ends the effect of the previous tale. If the tale requires a saving throw, the DC equals your spell save DC.
spirits.features.2.name: Tales from Beyond
spirits.features.3.description:
Spirits provide you with supernatural insights. You can conduct an hour-long ritual channeling spirits (which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}) using your Spiritual Focus. You can conduct the ritual with a number of willing creatures equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (including yourself). At the end of the ritual, you temporarily learn one spell of your choice from any class.

The spell you choose must be of a level equal to the number of creatures that conducted the ritual or less, the spell must be of a level you can cast, and it must be in the school of Divination or Necromancy. The chosen spell counts as a Bard spell for you but doesn't count against the number of Bard spells you know.

Once you perform the ritual, you can't do so again until you start a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
spirits.features.3.name: Spirit Session
spirits.features.4.description: You now have the ability to nudge the spirits to bring forth tales that are more powerful. Whenever you roll on the Spirit Tales table, you can roll the die twice and choose which of the two effects to bestow. If you roll the same number on both dice, you can ignore the number and choose any effect on the table.
spirits.features.4.name: Mystical Connection
spirits.name: Коллегия Духов
spores.description: This subclass is from a different game edition. For a given subclass feature, you may gain that feature at a different level from the one specified in the subclass feature. Druids of the Circle of Spores find beauty in decay. They see within mold and other fungi the ability to transform lifeless material into abundant, albeit somewhat strange, life. These druids believe that life and death are parts of a grand cycle, with one leading to the other and then back again. Death isn't the end of life, but instead a change of state that sees life shift into a new form. Druids of this circle have a complex relationship with the undead. They see nothing inherently wrong with undeath, which they consider to be a companion to life and death. But these druids believe that the natural cycle is healthiest when each segment of it is vibrant and changing. Undead that seek to replace all life with undeath, or that try to avoid passing to a final rest, violate the cycle and must be thwarted.
spores.features.0.description: Your symbiotic link to fungus and your ability to tap into the cycle of life and death grants you access to certain spells. At 2nd level, you learn the {@spell Chill Touch} cantrip. At 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level you gain access to the spells listed for that level in the Circle of Spores Spells table. Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.
spores.features.0.name: Circle Spells
spores.features.1.description: You are surrounded by invisible, necrotic spores that are harmless until you unleash them on a creature nearby. When a creature you can see moves into a space within 10 feet of you or starts its turn there, you can use your reaction to deal {@dice 1d4} necrotic damage to that creature unless it succeeds on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. The necrotic damage increases to {@dice 1d6} at 6th level, {@dice 1d8} at 10th level, and {@dice 1d10} at 14th level.
spores.features.1.name: Halo of Spores
spores.features.2.description: You gain the ability to channel magic into your spores. As an action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to awaken those spores, rather than transforming into a beast form, and you gain 4 temporary hit points for each level you have in this class. While this feature is active, you gain the following benefits: When you deal your Halo of Spores damage, roll the damage die a second time and add it to the total. Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra {@dice 1d6} necrotic damage to any target they hit. These benefits last for 10 minutes, until you lose all these temporary hit points, or until you use your Wild Shape again.
spores.features.2.name: Symbiotic Entity
spores.features.3.description: Your spores gain the ability to infest a corpse and animate it. If a beast or a humanoid that is Small or Medium dies within 10 feet of you, you can use your reaction to animate it, causing it to stand up immediately with 1 hit point. The creature uses the {@creature Zombie} stat block. It remains animate for 1 hour, after which time it collapses and dies. In combat, the zombie's turn comes immediately after yours. It obeys your mental commands, and the only action it can take is the Attack action, making one melee attack. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.
spores.features.3.name: Fungal Infestation
spores.features.4.description: You gain the ability to seed an area with deadly spores. As a bonus action while your Symbiotic Entity feature is active, you can hurl spores up to 30 feet away, where they swirl in a 10-foot cube for 1 minute. The spores disappear early if you use this feature again, if you dismiss them as a bonus action, or if your Symbiotic Entity feature is no longer active. Whenever a creature moves into the cube or starts its turn there, that creature takes your Halo of Spores damage, unless the creature succeeds on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature can take this damage no more than once per turn. While the cube of spores persists, you can't use your Halo of Spores reaction.
spores.features.4.name: Spreading Spores
spores.features.5.description: The fungal spores in your body alter you: you can't be {@condition Blinded}, {@condition Deafened}, {@condition Frightened}, or {@condition Poisoned}, and any critical hit against you counts as a normal hit instead, unless you're {@condition Incapacitated}.
spores.features.5.name: Fungal Body
spores.name: Круг Спор
spores.shortDescription: Master of Decay, Spores, and Rebirth
stars.description: The Circle of Stars has tracked heavenly patterns since time immemorial, discovering secrets hidden amid the constellations. By understanding these secrets, the Druids of this circle seek to harness the powers of the cosmos.
stars.features.0.description: You've created a star chart as part of your heavenly studies. It is a Tiny object, and you can use it as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Druid spells. You determine its form by rolling on the Star Map table or by choosing one. While holding the map, you have the {@spell Guidance|XPHB} and {@spell Guiding Bolt|XPHB} spells prepared, and you can cast {@spell Guiding Bolt|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. You can cast it in that way a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If you lose the map, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to magically create a replacement. This ceremony can be performed during a {@variantrule Short|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, and it destroys the previous map.
stars.features.0.name: Star Map
stars.features.1.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to take on a starry form rather than shape-shifting. While in your starry form, you retain your game statistics, but your body becomes luminous, your joints glimmer like stars, and glowing lines connect them as on a star chart. This form sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. The form lasts for 10 minutes. It ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition, or use this feature again. Whenever you assume this form, choose which of the following constellations glimmers on your body; your choice gives you certain benefits while in the form.
stars.features.1.details.archer: {@b Archer.} A constellation of an archer appears on you. When you activate this form and as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your subsequent turns while it lasts, you can make a ranged spell attack, hurling a luminous arrow that targets one creature within 60 feet of yourself. On a hit, the attack deals Radiant damage equal to {@dice 1d8} plus your Wisdom modifier.
stars.features.1.details.chalice: {@b Chalice.} A constellation of a life-giving goblet appears on you. Whenever you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a creature, you or another creature within 30 feet of you can regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d8} plus your Wisdom modifier.
stars.features.1.details.dragon: {@b Dragon.} A constellation of a wise dragon appears on you. When you make an Intelligence or a Wisdom check or a Constitution saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration}, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the {@dice d20} as a 10.
stars.features.1.name: Starry Form
stars.features.2.description: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can consult your Star Map for omens and roll a die. Until you finish your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you gain access to a special {@action Reaction|XPHB} based on whether you rolled an even or odd number on the die. {@b Weal (Even).} Whenever a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is about to make a {@dice D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to roll {@dice 1d6} and add the number rolled to the total. {@b Woe (Odd).} Whenever a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is about to make a {@dice D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to roll {@dice 1d6} and subtract the number rolled from the total. You can use this {@action Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
stars.features.2.name: Cosmic Omen
stars.features.3.description: The constellations of your Starry Form improve. The {@dice 1d8} of the Archer and the Chalice becomes {@dice 2d8}, and while the Dragon is active, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 20 feet and can hover. Moreover, at the start of each of your turns while in your Starry Form, you can change which constellation glimmers on your body.
stars.features.3.name: Twinkling Constellations
stars.features.4.description: While in your Starry Form, you become partially incorporeal, giving you Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
stars.features.4.name: Full of Stars
stars.name: Круг Звёзд
storm-herald.description: All barbarians harbor a fury within. Their rage grants them superior strength, durability, and speed. Barbarians who follow the Path of the Storm Herald learn to transform that rage into a mantle of primal magic, which swirls around them. When in a fury, a barbarian of this path taps into the forces of nature to create powerful magical effects.
storm-herald.features.0.description: Starting at 3rd level, you emanate a stormy, magical aura while you rage. The aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through {@variantrule total cover|XPHB}. Your aura has an effect that activates when you enter your rage, and you can activate the effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action. Choose desert, sea, or tundra. Your aura's effect depends on that chosen environment, as detailed below. You can change your environment choice whenever you gain a level in this class.
storm-herald.features.0.details.dc: If your aura's effects require a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.
storm-herald.features.0.details.desert: When this effect is activated, all other creatures in your aura take 2 fire damage each. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th level, 5 at 15th level, and 6 at 20th level.
storm-herald.features.0.details.sea: When this effect is activated, you can choose one other creature you can see in your aura. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. The target takes {@dice 1d6} lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to {@dice 2d6} at 10th level, {@dice 3d6} at 15th level, and {@dice 4d6} at 20th level.
storm-herald.features.0.details.tundra: When this effect is activated, each creature of your choice in your aura gains 2 temporary hit points, as icy spirits inure it to suffering. The temporary hit points increase when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th level, 5 at 15th level, and 6 at 20th level.
storm-herald.features.0.name: Storm Aura
storm-herald.features.1.description: At 6th level, the storm grants you benefits even when your aura isn't active. The benefits are based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.
storm-herald.features.1.details.desert: You gain resistance to fire damage, and you don't suffer the effects of extreme heat, as described in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Moreover, as an action, you can touch a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried by anyone else and set it on fire.
storm-herald.features.1.details.sea: You gain resistance to lightning damage, and you don't suffer the effects of extreme cold, as described in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Moreover, you can breathe underwater. You also gain a swimming speed of 30 feet.
storm-herald.features.1.details.tundra: You gain resistance to cold damage, and you don't suffer the effects of extreme cold, as described in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Moreover, as an action, you can touch water and turn a 5-foot cube of it into ice, which melts after 1 minute. This action fails if a creature is in the cube.
storm-herald.features.1.name: Storm Soul
storm-herald.features.2.description: At 10th level, you learn to use your mastery of the storm to protect others. Each creature of your choice has the damage resistance you gained from the Storm Soul feature while the creature is in your Storm Aura.
storm-herald.features.2.name: Shielding Storm
storm-herald.features.3.description: At 14th level, the power of the storm you channel grows mightier, lashing out at your foes. The effect is based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.
storm-herald.features.3.details.desert: Immediately after a creature in your aura hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes fire damage equal to half your barbarian level.
storm-herald.features.3.details.sea: When you hit a creature in your aura with an attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is knocked {@condition prone|XPHB}, as if struck by a wave.
storm-herald.features.3.details.tundra: Whenever the effect of your Storm Aura is activated, you can choose one creature you can see in the aura. That creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw, or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn, as magical frost covers it.
storm-herald.features.3.name: Raging Storm
storm-herald.name: Путь Вестника Бури
storm.description: Your innate magic comes from the power of elemental air. Many with this power can trace their magic back to a near-death experience caused by the Great Rain, but perhaps you were born during a howling gale so powerful that folk still tell stories of it, or your lineage might include the influence of potent air creatures such as djinn. Whatever the case, the magic of the storm permeates your being. Storm sorcerers are invaluable members of a ship's crew. Their magic allows them to exert control over wind and weather in their immediate area. Their abilities also prove useful in repelling attacks by sahuagin, pirates, and other waterborne threats.
storm.features.0.description: The arcane magic you command is infused with elemental air. You can speak, read, and write Primordial. Knowing this language allows you to understand and be understood by those who speak its dialects: Aquan, Auran, Ignan, and Terran.
storm.features.0.name: Wind Speaker
storm.features.1.description: Starting at 1st level, you can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to cause whirling gusts of elemental air to briefly surround you, immediately before or after you cast a spell of 1st level or higher. Doing so allows you to fly up to 10 feet without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.
storm.features.1.name: Tempestuous Magic
storm.features.2.description: You gain Resistance to Lightning and Thunder damage. In addition, whenever you start casting a spell of 1st level or higher that deals Lightning or Thunder damage, stormy magic erupts from you. This eruption causes creatures of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of you to take Lightning or Thunder damage (choose each time this ability activates) equal to half your Sorcerer level.
storm.features.2.name: Heart of the Storm
storm.features.3.description:
You gain the ability to subtly control the weather around you.

If it is raining, you can use an action to cause the rain to stop falling in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on you. You can end this effect as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.

If it is windy, you can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} each round to choose the direction that the wind blows in a 100-foot-radius sphere centered on you. The wind blows in that direction until the end of your next turn. This feature doesn't alter the speed of the wind.
storm.features.3.name: Storm Guide
storm.features.4.description: When you are hit by a melee attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal Lightning damage to the attacker. The damage equals your Sorcerer level. The attacker must also make a Strength saving throw against your sorcerer spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker is pushed in a straight line up to 20 feet away from you.
storm.features.4.name: Storm's Fury
storm.features.5.description:
You gain Immunity to Lightning and Thunder damage.

You also gain a magical flying speed of 60 feet. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can reduce your flying speed to 30 feet for 1 hour and choose a number of creatures within 30 feet of you equal to 3 + your Charisma modifier. The chosen creatures gain a magical flying speed of 30 feet for 1 hour. Once you reduce your flying speed in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
storm.features.5.name: Wind Soul
storm.name: Штормовая Магия
storm.shortDescription: Command the Power of the Storm
sun-soul.description: Monks of the Way of the Sun Soul learn to channel their life energy into searing bolts of light. They teach that meditation can unlock the ability to unleash the indomitable light shed by the soul of every living creature.
sun-soul.features.0.description: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you can hurl searing bolts of magical radiance. You gain a new attack option that you can use with the {@action Attack|XPHB} action. The special attack is a ranged spell attack with a range of 30 feet. You are proficient with it, and you add your Dexterity modifier to its attack and damage rolls. Its damage is radiant, and its damage die is a {@dice d4}. This die changes as you gain monk levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Monk table. When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and use this special attack as part of it, you can spend 1 ki point to make the special attack twice as a bonus action. When you gain the Extra Attack feature, this special attack can be used for any of the attacks you make as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action.
sun-soul.features.0.name: Radiant Sun Bolt
sun-soul.features.1.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to channel your ki into searing waves of energy. Immediately after you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can spend 2 ki points to cast the {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} spell as a bonus action. You can spend additional ki points to cast {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} as a higher-level spell. Each additional ki point you spend increases the spell's level by 1. The maximum number of ki points (2 plus any additional points) that you can spend on the spell equals half your monk level.
sun-soul.features.1.name: Searing Arc Strike
sun-soul.features.2.description: At 11th level, you gain the ability to create an orb of light that erupts into a devastating explosion. As an action, you magically create an orb and hurl it at a point you choose within 150 feet, where it erupts into a sphere of radiant light for a brief but deadly instant. Each creature in that 20-foot-radius sphere must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} radiant damage. A creature doesn't need to see the orb to be affected. You can increase the sphere's damage by spending ki points. Each point you spend, to a maximum of 3, increases the damage by {@damage 2d6}.
sun-soul.features.2.name: Searing Sunburst
sun-soul.features.3.description: At 17th level, you become wreathed in a luminous, magical aura. You shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. You can extinguish or restore the light as a bonus action. If a creature hits you with a melee attack while this light shines, you can use your reaction to deal radiant damage to the creature. The radiant damage equals 5 + your Wisdom modifier.
sun-soul.features.3.name: Sun Shield
sun-soul.name: Путь Солнечной Души
sun-soul.shortDescription: Channel Life Energy into Radiant Light
swarmkeeper.description: Feeling a deep connection to the environment around them, some rangers reach out through their magical connection to the world and bond with a swarm of nature spirits. The swarm becomes a potent force in battle, as well as helpful company for the ranger. Some Swarmkeepers are outcasts or hermits, keeping to themselves and their attendant swarms rather than dealing with the discomfort of others. Other Swarmkeepers enjoy building vibrant communities that work for the mutual benefit of all those they consider part of their swarm.
swarmkeeper.features.0.description: A swarm of intangible nature spirits has bonded itself to you and can assist you in battle. While you're alive, the swarm remains in your space, crawling on you or flying and skittering around you within your space. You determine its appearance, or you generate its appearance by rolling on the Swarm Appearance table. Once on each of your turns, you can cause the swarm to assist you in one of the following ways, immediately after you hit a creature with an attack: the attack's target takes 1d6 piercing damage from the swarm; the attack's target must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be moved by the swarm up to 15 feet horizontally in a direction of your choice; or you are moved by the swarm 5 feet horizontally in a direction of your choice.
swarmkeeper.features.0.name: Gathered Swarm
swarmkeeper.features.1.description: You learn the {@spell Mage Hand} cantrip if you don't already know it. When you cast it, the hand takes the form of your swarming nature spirits. You also learn an additional spell of 1st level or higher when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Swarmkeeper Spells table. Each spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.
swarmkeeper.features.1.name: Swarmkeeper Magic
swarmkeeper.features.2.description: You can condense part of your swarm into a focused mass that lifts you up. As a bonus action, you gain a flying speed of 10 feet and can hover. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
swarmkeeper.features.2.name: Writhing Tide
swarmkeeper.features.3.description: Your Gathered Swarm grows mightier in the following ways: the damage of Gathered Swarm increases to 1d8; if a creature fails its saving throw against being moved by Gathered Swarm, you can also cause the swarm to knock the creature prone; when you are moved by Gathered Swarm, it gives you half cover until the start of your next turn.
swarmkeeper.features.3.name: Mighty Swarm
swarmkeeper.features.4.description: You can discorporate into your swarm, avoiding danger. When you take damage, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to that damage. You vanish into your swarm and then teleport to an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of you, where you reappear with the swarm. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
swarmkeeper.features.4.name: Swarming Dispersal
swarmkeeper.name: Хранитель Роя
swarmkeeper.shortDescription: Bond with a Swarm of Nature Spirits
swashbuckler.description: You focus your training on the art of the blade, relying on speed, elegance, and charm in equal parts. While some warriors are brutes clad in heavy armor, your method of fighting looks almost like a performance. Duelists and pirates typically belong to this archetype. A Swashbuckler excels in single combat, and can fight with two weapons while safely darting away from an opponent.
swashbuckler.features.0.description: During your turn, if you make a melee attack against a creature, that creature can't make {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against you for the rest of your turn.
swashbuckler.features.0.name: Fancy Footwork
swashbuckler.features.1.description: Your confidence propels you into battle. You can give yourself a bonus to your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls equal to your Charisma modifier. You also gain an additional way to use your Sneak Attack; you don't need {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll to use your Sneak Attack against a creature if you are within 5 feet of it, no other creature is within 5 feet of you, and you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. All the other rules for Sneak Attack still apply to you.
swashbuckler.features.1.name: Rakish Audacity
swashbuckler.features.2.description: Your charm becomes extraordinarily beguiling. As an action, you can make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check contested by a creature's Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check. The creature must be able to hear you, and the two of you must share a language.
swashbuckler.features.2.details.hostileC: {@b Hostile Creature.} If you succeed against a Hostile creature, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against targets other than you and can't make {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against targets other than you. This effect lasts for 1 minute, until one of your companions attacks the target or affects it with a spell, or until you and the target are more than 60 feet apart.
swashbuckler.features.2.details.nonHosti: {@b Other Creatures.} If you succeed against a creature that isn't Hostile to you, it is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you for 1 minute. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, the creature regards you as a friendly acquaintance. This effect ends immediately if you or your companions do anything harmful to it.
swashbuckler.features.2.name: Panache
swashbuckler.features.3.description: You can use a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) or Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check you make during the same turn.
swashbuckler.features.3.name: Elegant Maneuver
swashbuckler.features.4.description: Your mastery of the blade lets you turn failure into success in combat. If you miss with an attack roll, you can roll it again with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. Once you do so, you can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
swashbuckler.features.4.name: Master Duelist
swashbuckler.name: Дуэлянт
swashbuckler.shortDescription: Fight with Speed, Elegance, and Charm
swords.description: Bards of the College of Swords are called blades, and they entertain through daring feats of weapon prowess. Blades perform stunts such as sword swallowing, knife throwing and juggling, and mock combats. Though they use their weapons to entertain, they are also highly trained and skilled warriors in their own right.
swords.features.0.description: You gain proficiency with medium armor and with the {@item scimitar|XPHB}.
swords.features.0.name: Bonus Proficiencies
swords.features.1.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take the same Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.
swords.features.1.details.dueling: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.
swords.features.1.details.twoWeaponFight: When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack.
swords.features.1.name: Боевой стиль
swords.features.2.description: Whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the turn, and if a weapon attack that you make as part of this action hits a creature, you can use one of the following Blade Flourish options of your choice. You can use only one Blade Flourish option per turn.
swords.features.2.details.defensiveFlour: You can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target you hit. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die. You also add the number rolled to your AC until the start of your next turn.
swords.features.2.details.mobileFlourish: You can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target you hit. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die. You can also push the target up to 5 feet plus a number of feet equal to the number you roll on that die. You can then immediately use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to your walking speed to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target.
swords.features.2.details.slashingFlouri: You can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target you hit and to any other creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of you. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die.
swords.features.2.name: Blade Flourish
swords.features.3.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.
swords.features.3.name: Дополнительная атака
swords.features.4.description: Whenever you use a Blade Flourish option, you can roll a {@dice d6} and use it instead of expending a Bardic Inspiration die.
swords.features.4.name: Master's Flourish
swords.name: Коллегия Мечей
swords.shortDescription: Entertain and Fight with Blade Flourishes
thief.description: A mix of burglar, pickpocket, and explorer, a Thief is a Rogue who tends to use stealth and dexterity over brute force. You are adept at quickly taking items and getting into places others can't reach, drawing on skills of infiltration and quick reflexes.
thief.features.0.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can do one of the following.
thief.features.0.details.sleightOfHand: {@b Sleight of Hand.} Make a Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) check to pick a lock or disarm a trap with {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB} or to pick a pocket.
thief.features.0.details.useAnObject: {@b Use an Object.} Take the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, or take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to use a magic item that requires that action.
thief.features.0.name: Fast Hands
thief.features.1.description: You have gained the following movement abilities.
thief.features.1.details.climber: {@b Climber.} You gain a Climb Speed equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
thief.features.1.details.jumpDistance: {@b Jump's Distance.} When you take the {@action Jump|XPHB} action, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.
thief.features.1.name: Second-Story Work
thief.features.2.description: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on every Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) check you make, provided you aren't wearing Medium or Heavy armor.
thief.features.2.name: Supreme Sneak
thief.features.3.description: In your treasure hunting, you have learned how to maximize use of magic items, giving you the following benefits.
thief.features.3.details.attunement: {@b Attunement.} You can attune to up to four magic items at once.
thief.features.3.details.charges: {@b Charges.} Whenever you use a magic item property that expends charges, roll a {@dice d6}. On a roll of 6, you use the property without expending the charges.
thief.features.3.details.scrolls: {@b Scrolls.} You can use any {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability for the spell. If the spell is a cantrip or a level 1 spell, you can cast it reliably. If the spell is level 2 or higher, you must succeed on an Intelligence check with a DC of 10 plus the spell's level. On a failed check, the spell disappears from the scroll with no other effect.
thief.features.3.name: Use Magic Device
thief.features.4.description: You have become adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and your second turn at your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} minus 10.
thief.features.4.name: Thief's Reflexes
thief.name: Вор
thief.shortDescription: Use Nimbleness and Stealth to Steal Anything
trapper.description: Легенды гласят, что основатели Гильдии Ловчих были охотниками на дичь, которым наскучило выслеживать обычных оленей и мечтавшим о добыче покрупнее. Ловчие гордятся своими убийствами и изобретательностью, которую демонстрирует идеально сработавшая ловушка. Наследие Гильдии Ловчих — постоянное свидетельство преимуществ выжидания. Элемент неожиданности первостепенен для ловчего, чей набор может проявить себя ещё до встречи охотника и монстра. Подготовка — ключ ко всему, и находчивый ловчий имеет приспособления, помогающие свалить даже сильнейших врагов.
trapper.features.0.description: Вы получаете владение навыком {@skill Stealth|XPHB|Скрытность} и {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB|Инструментами ремонтника}.
trapper.features.0.name: Скрытный и хитроумный
trapper.features.1.description: Вы научились создавать приспособления и механизмы, помогающие на охоте. Как часть {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгого отдыха}, вы можете изготовить два Приспособления ловчего, если у вас под рукой материалы и {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB|Инструменты ремонтника}. Также, потратив 1 час работы с таким набором и материалы стоимостью 20 ЗМ (например, снаряжение или заготовки монстров), вы можете создать одно Приспособление ловчего из {@filter списка Приспособлений ловчего|optionalfeatures|feature type=MH:TG}. Некоторые Приспособления ловчего позволяют цели совершать проверку характеристики или спасбросок, чтобы сопротивляться эффектам приспособления. Сл спасброска рассчитывается так: {@b Сл спасброска от Приспособлений ловчего} = 8 + модификатор Интеллекта + {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB|Бонус мастерства}.
trapper.features.1.name: Приспособления ловчего
trapper.features.2.description: Вы стали свирепым устроителем засад. Когда вы совершаете бросок {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB|Инициативы}, вы можете добавить к броску свой модификатор Интеллекта. Кроме того, вас не могут застать врасплох враги, включающие типы существ из вашего Гримуара монстров.
trapper.features.2.name: Преимущество устроителя засад
trapper.features.3.description: Вы можете отскочить в сторону, чтобы избежать врагов, бросающихся на вас. Когда существо совершает бросок рукопашной атаки против вас, вы можете {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Реакцией} наложить {@status Disadvantage|XPHB|Помеху} на этот бросок и использовать свой Изученный отклик в рамках той же {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Реакции}. Независимо от того, попадает атака или промахивается, вы затем можете переместиться на расстояние вплоть до половины вашей {@variantrule Speed|XPHB|Скорости}. Это перемещение не провоцирует {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB|Провоцированные атаки}.
trapper.features.3.name: Проворный отклик
trapper.features.4.description: Вы научились изготавливать комплект Лёгкого или Среднего доспеха, используя чешую дракона, шкуру вервольфа, кожу тролля или подобный компонент монстра. Доспех принимает выбранный вами вид, отражающий компонент, из которого он сделан. Доспех имеет те же свойства, что Лёгкий или Средний доспех (на ваш выбор при создании доспеха), и получает две модификации из {@filter списка Модификаций доспехов|optionalfeatures|feature type=MH:AM}. Каждый раз, получая уровень Охотника на монстров, вы можете заменить одну из этих модификаций другой модификацией. Модификации требуют, чтобы доспех был надет для функционирования.
trapper.features.4.name: Доспех из шкуры монстра
trapper.features.5.description: Вы стали способны изготавливать инструменты ловчего гораздо быстрее. Вы можете потратить 1 минуту, чтобы изготовить Приспособление ловчего без затраты ЗМ или компонентов. Вы можете использовать это умение дважды и восстанавливаете все потраченные использования, когда завершаете {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}.
trapper.features.5.name: Быстрый мастер
trapper.name: Гильдия Ловчих
trick-shot.description: Точность для разных людей значит разное. Для вас истинная точность не обязательно в попадании в цель с первого выстрела, а скорее в попадании после того, как пуля отскочит десяток раз. Ваши атаки опасны как при промахе, так и после попадания в цель — настолько же, насколько чужие атаки опасны, пока ещё в воздухе.
trick-shot.features.0.description: Вы можете заставить свои снаряды двигаться неожиданными путями. Ваши дальнобойные атаки оружием игнорируют {@variantrule Half Cover|XPHB|Половинное укрытие} и {@variantrule Three-Quarters Cover|XPHB|Укрытие на три четверти}.
trick-shot.features.0.name: Творческая траектория
trick-shot.features.1.description: [Манёвр] Когда вы промахиваетесь дальнобойной атакой оружием, вы можете {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием} потратить одну Кость риска, чтобы перебросить атаку и добавить Кость риска к броску. Вы должны использовать новый бросок.
trick-shot.features.1.name: Рикошет
trick-shot.features.2.description: Ваши эффектные трюки с оружием дают вам следующие преимущества.
trick-shot.features.2.details.gunSpinnin: Один раз за ход, когда вы совершаете проверку Харизмы ({@skill Performance|XPHB|Выступление}) или Ловкости ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB|Ловкость рук}) с использованием одного из ваших Дальнобойных оружий, вы можете бросить Кость риска и добавить её к проверке характеристики, не тратя её.
trick-shot.features.2.details.speedLoade: В свой ход вы можете перезарядить оружие со свойством {@property Reload|GC:VSS'24|Перезарядка} без затраты действия или {@action Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусного действия}.
trick-shot.features.2.name: Эффектная стрельба
trick-shot.features.3.description: [Манёвр] Вы можете отстреливать снаряды в воздухе. Когда по союзнику в пределах 30 футов от вас попадает атака, вы можете {@action Reaction|XPHB|Реакцией} потратить одну Кость риска, чтобы дать этому союзнику преимущество манёвра На волоске против этой атаки. Вы должны держать Дальнобойное оружие, чтобы использовать этот манёвр.
trick-shot.features.3.name: Ловкое отражение
trick-shot.features.4.description: Один раз в каждый ваш ход, когда вы попадаете по существу дальнобойной атакой оружием, вы можете отрикошетить снаряд по дополнительным целям. Выберите другую цель в пределах 30 футов от первой и совершите бросок атаки против неё. При попадании вы можете повторить эту атаку по новой цели в пределах 30 футов, пока не промахнётесь или не совершите всего пять атак. Вы не можете нацелить одно и то же существо более чем одной атакой каждый раз, когда используете это умение. Использовав это умение, вы не можете использовать его снова, пока не завершите {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Короткий отдых} или {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Долгий отдых}. Вы также можете восстановить его использование, потратив две Кости риска (действие не требуется).
trick-shot.features.4.name: Выстрел-пинбол
trick-shot.name: Трюкач
trickery.description: The Trickery Domain offers magic of deception, illusion, and stealth. Clerics who wield this magic are a disruptive force in the world, puncturing pride, mocking tyrants, freeing captives, and flouting hollow traditions. They prefer subterfuge and pranks to direct confrontation. Gods of trickery are mischief-makers and instigators who stand as a constant challenge to the accepted order among both gods and mortals. They embody the forces of change and social upheaval, and they're patrons of thieves, scoundrels, gamblers, rebels, and liberators. Religious orders that operate in secret, especially those that seek to undermine oppressive governments or hierarchies, also draw on the power of the Trickery Domain. Make Mischief and Challenge Authority.
trickery.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Trickery Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
trickery.features.0.name: Trickery Domain Spells
trickery.features.1.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can choose yourself or a willing creature within 30 feet of yourself to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks. This blessing lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} or you use this feature again.
trickery.features.1.name: Blessing of the Trickster
trickery.features.2.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to create a perfect visual illusion of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The illusion is intangible and doesn't occupy its space. It lasts for 1 minute, but it ends early if you dismiss it (no action required) or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. The illusion is animated and mimics your expressions and gestures. While it persists, you gain the following benefits: {@b Cast Spells.} You can cast spells as though you were in the illusion's space, but you must use your own senses. {@b Distract.} When both you and your illusion are within 5 feet of a creature that can see the illusion, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature, given how distracting the illusion is to the target. {@b Move.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move the illusion up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is within 120 feet of yourself.
trickery.features.2.name: Invoke Duplicity
trickery.features.3.description: Whenever you take the {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to create or move the illusion of your Invoke Duplicity, you can teleport, swapping places with the illusion.
trickery.features.3.name: Trickster's Transposition
trickery.features.4.description: The illusion of your Invoke Duplicity has grown more powerful in the following ways. {@b Shared Distraction.} When you and your allies make attack rolls against a creature within 5 feet of the illusion, the attack rolls have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. {@b Healing Illusion.} When the illusion ends, you or a creature of your choice within 5 feet of it regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Cleric level.
trickery.features.4.name: Improved Duplicity
trickery.name: Домен Обмана
twilight.description: The twilit transition from light into darkness often brings calm and even joy, as the day's labors end and the hours of rest begin. The darkness can also bring terrors, but the gods of twilight guard against the horrors of the night. Clerics who serve these deities—examples of which appear on the Twilight Deities table—bring comfort to those who seek rest and protect them by venturing into the encroaching darkness to ensure that the dark is a comfort, not a terror.
twilight.features.0.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Twilight Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
twilight.features.0.name: Twilight Domain Spells
twilight.features.1.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.
twilight.features.1.name: Bonus Proficiencies
twilight.features.2.description: You can see through the deepest gloom. You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} out to a range of 300 feet. As an action, you can magically share this Darkvision with willing creatures you can see within 10 feet of you, up to a number of creatures equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one creature). The shared Darkvision lasts for 1 hour. Once you share it, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, unless you expend a spell slot of any level to share it again.
twilight.features.2.name: Eyes of Night
twilight.features.3.description: The night has taught you to be vigilant. As an action, you give one creature you touch (including possibly yourself) {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll the creature makes. This benefit ends immediately after the roll or if you use this feature again.
twilight.features.3.name: Vigilant Blessing
twilight.features.4.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to refresh your allies with soothing twilight. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB}, and a sphere of twilight emanates from you. The sphere is centered on you, has a 30-foot radius, and is filled with {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}. The sphere moves with you, and it lasts for 1 minute or until you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or die. Whenever a creature (including you) ends its turn in the sphere, you can grant that creature one of these benefits: You grant it {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Cleric level. You end one effect on it causing it to be {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB}.
twilight.features.4.name: Twilight Sanctuary
twilight.features.5.description: You can draw on the mystical power of night to rise into the air. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} when you are in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or darkness, you can magically grant yourself a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 minute. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
twilight.features.5.name: Steps of Night
twilight.features.6.description: The twilight that you summon offers a protective embrace: you and your allies have half cover while in the sphere created by your Twilight Sanctuary.
twilight.features.6.name: Twilight Shroud
twilight.name: Домен Сумерек
twilight.shortDescription: Guardian of the Twilight Between Light and Dark
undead.description:
You've made a pact with a deathless being, a creature that defies the cycle of life and death, forsaking its mortal shell so it might eternally pursue its unfathomable ambitions. For such beings, time and mortality are fleeting things, the concerns of those for whom grains of sand still rush through life's hourglass. Having once been mortal themselves, these ancient undead know firsthand the paths of ambition and the routes past the doors of death. They eagerly share this profane knowledge, along with other secrets, with those who work their will among the living.

Beings of this type include the demilich Acererak, the vampire tyrant Kas the Bloody-Handed, the githyanki lich-queen Vlaakith, the dracolich Dragotha, the undead pharaoh Ankhtepot, and the elusive Darklord, Azalin Rex.
undead.features.0.description: The Undead lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.
undead.features.0.name: Expanded Spell List
undead.features.1.description: You manifest an aspect of your patron's dreadful power. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you transform for 1 minute. You gain the following benefits while transformed:
undead.features.1.details.appearance: The appearance of your Form of Dread reflects some aspect of your patron. For example, your form could be a shroud of shadows forming the crown and robes of your lich patron, or your body might glow with glyphs from ancient funerary rites and be surrounded by desert winds, suggesting your mummy patron.
undead.features.1.details.frighten: Once during each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can force it to make a Wisdom saving throw, and if the saving throw fails, the target is {@status Frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.
undead.features.1.details.frightenedImmu: You are immune to the {@status Frightened} condition.
undead.features.1.details.temporaryHitPo: You gain temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d10} + your warlock level.
undead.features.1.details.uses: You can transform a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
undead.features.1.name: Form of Dread
undead.features.2.description:
Your patron's powers have a profound effect on your body and magic. You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe.

In addition, once during each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll and roll damage against the creature, you can replace the damage type with necrotic damage. While you are using your Form of Dread, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the necrotic damage the target takes.
undead.features.2.name: Grave Touched
undead.features.3.description:
Your connection to undeath and necrotic energy now saturates your body. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. If you are transformed using your Form of Dread, you instead become immune to Necrotic damage.

In addition, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you can use your {@action Reaction|XPHB} to drop to 1 hit point instead and cause your body to erupt with deathly energy. Each creature of your choice that is within 30 feet of you takes necrotic damage equal to {@dice 2d10} + your warlock level. You then gain 1 level of {@status exhaustion}. Once you use this reaction, you can't do so again until you finish {@dice 1d4} long rests.
undead.features.3.name: Necrotic Husk
undead.features.4.description:
Your spirit can become untethered from your physical form. As an action, you can project your spirit from your body. The body you leave behind is {@status Unconscious|XPHB} and in a state of suspended animation.

Your spirit resembles your mortal form in almost every way, replicating your game statistics but not your possessions. Any damage or other effects that apply to your spirit or physical body affects the other. Your spirit can remain outside your body for up to 1 hour or until your {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} is broken (as if concentrating on a spell). When your projection ends, your spirit returns to your body or your body magically teleports to your spirit's space (your choice).

While projecting your spirit, you gain the following benefits:
undead.features.4.details.flying: You have a flying speed equal to your walking speed and can hover. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, but you take {@dice 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object.
undead.features.4.details.necroticHealin: While you are using your Form of Dread, once during each of your turns when you deal Necrotic damage to a creature, you regain hit points equal to half the amount of Necrotic damage dealt.
undead.features.4.details.resistance: Your spirit and body gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
undead.features.4.details.spellcasting: When you cast a spell of the Conjuration or Necromancy school, the spell doesn't require Verbal or Somatic components or Material components that lack a gold cost.
undead.features.4.details.uses: Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
undead.features.4.name: Spirit Projection
undead.name: Нежить
undying.description:
Death holds no sway over your patron, who has unlocked the secrets of everlasting life, although such a prize—like all power—comes at a price. Once mortal, the Undying has seen mortal lifetimes pass like the seasons, like the flicker of endless days and nights. It has the secrets of the ages to share, secrets of life and death. Beings of this sort include Vecna, Lord of the Hand and the Eye; the dread Iuz; the lich-queen Vol; the Undying Court of Aerenal; Vlaakith, lich-queen of the githyanki; and the deathless wizard Fistandantalus.

In the Realms, Undying patrons include Larloch the Shadow King, legendary master of Warlock's Crypt, and Gilgeam, the God-King of Unther.
undying.features.0.description: The Undying lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.
undying.features.0.name: Expanded Spell List
undying.features.1.description:
You learn the {@spell Spare the Dying} cantrip, which counts as a warlock cantrip for you. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against any disease.

Additionally, undead have difficulty harming you. If an undead targets you directly with an attack or a harmful spell, that creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC (an undead needn't make the save when it includes you in an area effect, such as the explosion of {@spell Fireball|XPHB}). On a failed save, the creature must choose a new target or forfeit targeting someone instead of you, potentially wasting the attack or spell.
